Unified bootstrap sort with the ⇅ Sort Time button — both now use the animated flipResort FLIP glide (700ms, overshoot easing). Retired the silent one-shot sortAllColumnsOnceAfterFill + its only helper _sortOnceKey + the wb:sorted-once localStorage guard.

templates/board.html:
- Both bootstrap-finish call sites (after fill, and after reconcile-following-fill) now setTimeout(flipResort, 600) instead of the static snap-sort. _bootstrapFillSeen still tracked/reset (now cosmetic; harmless).
- Deleted sortAllColumnsOnceAfterFill() and _sortOnceKey(); kept _cardSortTs (used by flipResort). Replaced with a #601 retirement comment.

Why safe: the call is gated behind the bootstrap/reconcile SSE 'final' event — it does NOT fire on ordinary page reloads, so a user's manual drag-order is preserved between sessions (the old localStorage one-shot guard was redundant given that gating). Re-sorting is idempotent (already-sorted → flipPlayCards sees <1px delta → no animation).

Verified: node --check on extracted scripts OK; server serves updated board.html fresh (per-request _send_file from templates/); button path (flipResort) byte-unchanged. Bootstrap glide will show on next clean-slate+reinstall.

cards.title: (Reconly 7)Review-coverage: bootstrap backfill of past reviews + multi-card/uncommitted fallback
cards.origin: Deferred from #598 (core shipped). The risky/fuzzy + edge pieces.
cards.notes:
Deferred tail of #598 (core ledger shipped: card.py review, list --pending-review/--stale-review, advisory Stop-backstop, SKILL.md LAW).

═══ REVISED DESIGN (260610) — bucket-native, supersedes the old time-proximity matcher ═══

KEY INSIGHT (user): a /code-review shows up as a `Skill` tool_use in the TRANSCRIPT JSONL — NOT in git. discover2 mines transcripts independently of git, so review backfill works even for NO-GIT users. (Git mining is only for commits/ship-signals.)

The hard part of the OLD plan was matching a review event to the right card AFTER the fact (time-proximity + text overlap = fuzzy, mis-stamp risk). DROP THAT ENTIRELY. discover2 ALREADY groups turns into a unit-of-work card (its ~10-30min buckets). The /code-review invocation IS one of those turns → the review lives INSIDE the very turns that become the card. So there's nothing to "match" — ride the bucketing discover2 already does:

  1) scripts/discover2_sources.py harvest_jsonl — capture `Skill` review-skill events (today files_from_tool_use whitelists only Edit/Write/Read/etc and DROPS Skill). Add review-skill detection: a tool_use with name=="Skill" and input.skill.split(":")[-1] in {code-review,security-review,simplify,review,ultrareview}. Emit into the event meta (e.g. meta.review_skills=[{skill,ts}]).
  2) scripts/discover2_extract.py extract_tasks (~318, where tool_calls are tallied per task) — if ANY turn in a bucket carried a review skill → set task.reviewed = {skill, at}.
  3) emit (hourly_emit.py / the card.py add path) — when the card is created, ALSO stamp it: reuse the shipped `card.py review` mechanism (reviewed tag + reviewedAt/reviewedSha + 🔍 subtask). i.e. card.py review <new#> --skill <skill> --at <ts> --findings "[bootstrap]".

WHY BETTER than old time-proximity matcher: no git needed · matching is FREE (review is in the card's own turns) · high precision · complexity drops from "a whole matcher + dry-run gate" to "a flag during extraction".

ONLY real failure mode (acceptable): build in bucket 1 but review in bucket 2 → stamp lands on bucket 2's card. Rare (build+review usually same bucket); short boundary; stamping "where the review activity was" is still defensible and far better than no backfill or a wrong text-match.

NOTE: the stamp is the `reviewed` TAG (orthogonal to the Done column) — NOT moving the card to Done.

═══ STILL DEFERRED: multi-card / uncommitted fallback (was #598 st6) ═══
When a LIVE review maps to no single card (range spans several, or uncommitted sweep) → create one lightweight standalone review card linked to what it covered. Lower priority; revisit after the bucket-native backfill is in use.
cards.writeup: Bucket-native review-coverage backfill (#598 deferred tail). The review SKILL (/code-review etc.) is a Skill tool_use in the transcript JSONL (not git) → backfill works even no-git, and the review lives in the very turns that become the card → attribution at grouping time, no fuzzy matching. (1) discover2_sources: review_skills_from_tool_use() + carry review_skills in harvest_jsonl asst meta (shared by both pipelines). (2) LLM bootstrap path (the REAL fresh-install fill): build_digest emits REVIEW line + LLM card schema gets a reviewed field + emit_card stamps via shipped card.py review (findings=[bootstrap], sha=''). (3) discover2 seed path: _attach_context accumulates review_skills→task.reviewed; serve_bootstrap stamps after add. Verified: e2e 22 passed (was 13, +9 review-backfill), live board untouched; real discover2 over WB history backfilled 2 actual code-reviews correctly attributed; back-compat (no review→no stamp). Deferred st6 (multi-card/uncommitted fallback) still open.

subtasks.text: Harvest review-skill signal: review_skills_from_tool_use() + carry review_skills in harvest_jsonl asst meta (shared by both pipelines)
LLM path: digest REVIEW line + 'reviewed' card-schema field + emit_card stamps via card.py review
discover2 seed path: _attach_context accumulates review_skills -> task record; serve_bootstrap stamps after add
Verify: discover2 fixture + /e2e backfill assertion + LLM spot-check + back-compat

cards.title: Code-review coverage tracking: stamp the reviewed card (reviewed tag + subtask w/ effort/SHA/#findings); 'pending review' = Done cards without the stamp; backfill during bootstrap
cards.origin: each /code-review or any review SKILL must be documented as a card so we know which sections are reviewed vs pending. Trigger on the explicit review skill, not ambient review (else it cards every time). Stamp the code card (recommended). NOTE: also do it during bootstrap.
cards.notes:
GOAL: a review-coverage ledger. Answer "which Done work has NOT been code-reviewed yet?" without creating a parallel pile of review cards.

MODEL (chosen): stamp the CODE card that was reviewed — add a `reviewed` tag + a subtask line carrying {effort level, commit SHA reviewed, # findings, skill name}. Pending review = Done cards lacking the `reviewed` tag.

TRIGGER (resolves the "cards every time" tension): fire ONLY on an explicit review-SKILL invocation — /code-review, /security-review, /simplify, /review, /ultrareview — NOT on ambient code-reading (which happens every task). A skill invocation is a concrete, detectable event; "Claude looked at code" is not.

ENFORCEMENT: advisory Stop-hook nudge (consistent with #591/#592 advisory-by-default) — if a review skill ran this turn and no card got a `reviewed` stamp, remind; do NOT hard-block.

BOOTSTRAP BACKFILL (user add): during History-Replay extraction, detect PAST review-skill events in the mined git/transcript history and stamp the matching cards `reviewed`, so the ledger is accurate from install — existing Done cards correctly show reviewed vs pending, not all-pending.
Building Phase 1-2: review-stamp cmd + pending/stale query + advisory backstop + SKILL.md LAW. Backfill (st7) + multi-card (st6) deferred.
st2 trigger-detection delivered via _review_skill_in() inside the EXISTING Stop recon hook (+ SKILL.md LAW), NOT a separate PreToolUse-on-Skill hook — per user's perf decision (no per-call hook). Ambient code-reading never fires (only a Skill tool_use with input.skill in REVIEW_SKILLS).
cards.writeup:
Shipped 5/7 (core ledger) — review-coverage tracking, stamp-the-card model. Subtasks 6 (multi-card fallback) + 7 (bootstrap backfill) deferred to #599 (fuzzy matching = mis-stamp risk; core answers the primary question without them).

DELIVERED:
1) DATA MODEL (card_commands.py): per reviewed card — reviews[] ({at,sha,skill,effort,findings}) + denormalized reviewedAt/reviewedSha + 'reviewed' tag + a done 🔍 audit subtask. Additive/back-compat (c.get('reviews',[]) on all 430 existing cards).
2) card.py review <ref> (card.py parser + cmd_review): --skill/--effort/--sha/--findings/--at. Stamp, NOT a column move. 'reviewed' tag written directly (bypasses _check_tags, like cmd_bug's 'bug' tag) — provenance marker, no taxonomy slot. sha defaults to repo HEAD via _current_sha.
3) list/query --pending-review: Done cards lacking a review = the coverage gap. _is_pending_review.
4) list/query --stale-review (re-review expiry, query-time, NOT auto-clear): _last_code_change_at compares reviewedAt vs history →inprogress/→done + doneAt ONLY. A --note edit does NOT un-review (verified); a bug-reopen+reship DOES (verified).
5) Advisory Stop-hook backstop (_hook_stop_recon.py): _review_skill_in detects a Skill tool_use with input.skill in REVIEW_SKILLS this turn; review_gap = review skill ran but no 'card.py review'. Writes recon_pending.json nudge; NEVER blocks (verified — empty stdout even under BOARD_STEWARD_STRICT=1). No new hook, no per-call latency — folds into the scan that already runs.
6) SKILL.md LAW: 'Review (LAW)' line under After-ship — stamp the card after any review skill. Ambient reading is not a review.

TRIGGER = LAW + free backstop (user's call): no PreToolUse-on-Skill hook (would add per-call latency). Detection lives in the existing Stop scan.

Verified end-to-end on a throwaway board (live board untouched, rev 558→ unchanged during test): stamp✓ pending-review✓ stale-review (note-safe + reopen-stale)✓ advisory-nudge✓ never-blocks✓ gap-suppressed-when-stamped✓ back-compat✓.

Files: scripts/card.py, scripts/card_commands.py, scripts/_hook_stop_recon.py, SKILL.md.

subtasks.text: card.py mechanism to stamp a card reviewed: e.g. `card.py review <n> --skill code-review --effort high --sha <SHA> --findings N` → adds 'reviewed' tag + a subtask 🔍 line. Records reviewedAt + reviewedSha.
Trigger detection: hook on the Skill tool matching {code-review, security-review, simplify, review, ultrareview}. Ambient code-reading never fires.
Advisory Stop-hook nudge: review skill ran this turn but no card got a 'reviewed' stamp → remind (no hard block), per #591/#592.
Query 'pending review': card.py list --pending-review = Done cards without the 'reviewed' tag (the coverage ledger).

Re-review expiry: if a card's reviewed code changes after reviewedAt (updatedAt / new commit touching its files), clear or flag the stamp so it re-surfaces as pending. (Key for the 'what's pending' goal.)
Multi-card / uncommitted fallback: when a review maps to no single card (range spans several, or uncommitted sweep), create one lightweight standalone review card linked to what it covered.
BOOTSTRAP BACKFILL: History-Replay extraction detects past review-skill events in mined git/transcript history and stamps matching cards 'reviewed' → ledger accurate from install.

cards.writeup:
Extended the #659 deselect handler to also clear stray text-selections that span MULTIPLE column titles / cards. (Originally #51 on the fresh board; re-added here.)

USER REPORT
"when i 'highlight' the columns, or multiple columns, i also want to click an empty area and make it go away, just now was 'within the text box'"

ROOT CAUSE
#659's handler bailed when activeElement.isContentEditable === false. Drag-selecting across multiple sibling column headers leaves NO single contentEditable focused, so the handler exited and the blue stuck — same #555-class root: drag handlers preventDefault the mousedown, suppressing browser-default click-clears-selection.

CHANGE (single file: templates/board.html ~3516)
Same capture-phase mousedown listener, now two branches:
- (a) #659 path — single focused contentEditable: blur (commits the rename) + clear, as before.

cards.title: card.py 'Exit code 1/2' cascade: auto-resolve board from registry when run outside repo + position-independent --board + add --note to update
cards.origin: i keep seeing Error: Exit code 2 when u fly a card... so many exit codes. fix it
cards.writeup:
Fixed 6/10 — killed the "Exit code 1/2" cascade users kept hitting when running card.py from $HOME instead of the repo. Three changes, all verified from $HOME:

1) BOARD AUTO-RESOLVE (card_state.py find_board): when cwd-walk finds no board/board.json, fall back to port_registry.get_active() (the last-active board, ~/.board-steward/last-active) before erroring. Run from anywhere → just works. Was Exit 1 "no board found".

2) POSITION-INDEPENDENT --board (card.py _hoist_board + main): --board / --board=PATH is now pulled from anywhere in argv, so it works BEFORE or AFTER the subcommand. Was Exit 2 "unrecognized arguments: --board" (argparse treats globals as pre-subcommand only).

3) update --note APPEND (card.py + card_commands.py): `update` now accepts --note (append one line), matching `fly --note`. --notes still REPLACES. Was Exit 2 "ambiguous option: --note could match --notes/--notes-stdin".

Also improved the final error message to mention the registry fallback + that --board works either side. Verified: show from $HOME ✓, --board after subcommand ✓, --board before ✓, update --note appends ✓.

cards.title: Card-time badge/tooltip showed wrong dates — badge now shows COMPLETION time for Done cards; tooltip simplified to Created + Updated
cards.writeup:
Fixed 6/10 in templates/board.html. Two parts:

1) TOOLTIP (hover the time badge): old version rendered created/updated/subtask/done. The "subtask" line did fmtTime(card.lastTouchedSubtask), but that field is OVERLOADED — set to a timestamp at L3447 yet to a subtask ID string at L2729/L6248. When it held an ID (e.g. "s-extraction-...--4" on #350), it parsed as garbage → "2001-04-01 / 25y ago". FIX: tooltip is now just `created : …` + `updated : … (Xago)`.

2) BADGE TEXT (the visible "9d ago"): previously always relativeTime(createdAt), so a card done today but created 9d ago read "9d ago". Added cardDisplayTime(card): Done column → doneAt (fallback updatedAt/createdAt); otherwise createdAt. Applied in both the initial render (~L3620) and the 1s tickTimes() refresh (~L2959). Now a Done card's badge reads when it was COMPLETED.

Browser refresh picks it up (server serves templates/board.html directly; no restart). lastTouchedSubtask stays overloaded — fine for #254 active-work-follow, just no longer rendered as a date.

cards.title: Recon-only: cut In-Progress pile-up by matching stranded historical IP cards to commit file-lists inside hourly_reconcile.py ONLY (never the shared bootstrap harvest). Successor to #350 after the extraction-side fix degraded carding perf.

Fixture test_recon_filematch.py already exists (was 3/3 fixed vs 1/3 pre-fix) — reuse it.

cards.title: Measure + document install/recon Haiku token cost
cards.origin: yes run it and update the doc — measure real Haiku token consumption of the bootstrap/recon fill and write it into TOKEN_BUDGET.md
cards.writeup: Measured real Haiku token cost of the install/recon backfill and updated docs/TOKEN_BUDGET.md. Method: drop-in 'claude' wrapper (CLAUDE_BIN) that re-runs each extractor call with --output-format json to capture usage, emits plain text so prod parse path is untouched (--output-format text in prod unchanged). Ran one DEFAULT 2-day Haiku bootstrap on WorkBoard history: 38 Haiku calls → 81 cards; output 23,249, uncached input 200, cache-creation 265,028, cache-read 1,263,563 = 1,528,791 input-side total. Doc updates: (1) corrected stale 'no per-prompt injection' claim — #360 UserPromptSubmit nudge injects ~309 tok (≈355 Claude) EVERY prompt, ~15.7K over 50 turns; (2) added Install/recon Haiku-token section (tokens only, NO dollar figures per user — framed as cheapest tier, separate detached stream, zero interactive-context tokens); (3) fixed peer-benchmark ~0→~309; (4) added 'trim #360 nudge' as highest-leverage follow-up. cl100k proxy noted. NOT committed yet.

cards.title: Stop hook advisory-by-default; gate blocking behind BOARD_STEWARD_STRICT
cards.origin: the running stop hook is very annoying... wanna make it not suddenly block users; does it cost tokens — make it advisory default, blocking opt-in
cards.writeup: Stop hook now ADVISORY by default. scripts/_hook_stop_recon.py: gated the decision:block emission behind BOARD_STEWARD_STRICT env flag (added 'import os'). Default = recon_pending.json note only (0 model tokens, no user-visible forced continuation; genuine miss caught next SessionStart). BOARD_STEWARD_STRICT=1 restores same-turn blocking for power users. Verified: advisory run on a would-block transcript emits empty stdout + writes recon_pending; STRICT=1 emits decision:block. smoke_test.py --fast 8/8 green. Updated docs/KEY_FEATURES.md Stop-hook section to reflect advisory-default. Also answered user: blocking is the ONLY token-costing path (1 extra model turn/fire); 'render for 1s' not possible (hook can't control Claude Code UI). NOT yet committed.

cards.title: Fix Stop-hook false positives: ship-signal mention-match (D) + log/scratch edits (A)
cards.origin: fix A and D — Stop-hook precision: 9/14 prod blocks were false alarms (read-only git inspection counted as ship-signal; non-work edits counted)
cards.writeup: Fixed Stop-hook false positives in scripts/_hook_stop_recon.py. D (ship-signal): replaced naive SHIP_RE_WORDS substring match with _is_ship_command() — evaluates per pipeline segment, excludes read-only git/inspection prefixes (git log/show/diff/status/branch, grep/curl/cat/echo), excludes --grep/--merged mention-matches, excludes card.py commands (a --writeup quoting 'shipped' is the carding action, not a ship), requires real mutating verb (git commit/push/merge) or bare shipped/deployed outside a read context. A (edits): _in_scope_edits now skips in-repo session/log/scratch artifacts (conversation_verbatim_*/conversation_raw_*/session_log*/*.log) even under project_root. Added 'import re'; removed dead SHIP_RE_WORDS. Verified with focused test reproducing all prod false positives (git branch --merged, git log --grep, card.py writeup with 'shipped', verbatim-dump edit) → now correctly NOT flagged; genuine git commit/push/merge still flagged. Repo smoke_test.py --fast 8/8 green. NOT yet committed.

Plus zero-input auto-logging, token-efficiency ladder, animated UI, History Replay, cross-platform autostart, data-safety. Also confirmed for user: verbatim dump (render_session_raw.py) is dev-only, NOT shipped in plugin, and zero model tokens (pure stdlib). Fix audit delivered separately in-chat.

cards.title: Undo/redo SSE echo steals active-work pulse
cards.origin: with A pulsating, move B in/out, then Cmd+Shift+Z: B jumps to top of IP and pulsates, A stops — undo/redo restore-move echo claims the pulse
cards.writeup: Undo/redo echo stole the pulse. Root cause: _tagRestoreMoves stamps reverted moves via:'undo'/'redo'; the SSE echo of that POST ran handleCardUpdated->simulateUserDragMove->_moveByUser, which only treated via==='user' as user, so undo/redo read as agent and CLAIMED the pulse for the restored card — overriding the snapshot's authoritative activeWorkId (set by _restoreSnap). Fix (templates/board.html _moveByUser): treat via in {user,undo,redo} as user (reverts are restores, not fresh work; snapshot already carries activeWorkId). No claim flow touched. node --check OK. Repro: A pulsing, drag B in/out, Cmd+Z/Cmd+Shift+Z -> A keeps pulsing now.

cards.title: Source-aware active-work pulse (user-drag never steals)
cards.origin: when Card A is in progress and I shift Card B into IP, Card B pulsates+tops; only agent-pushed cards should steal the pulse, user-shifted cards must not
cards.writeup: Source-aware active-work pulse. templates/board.html: setActiveWorkForMove/applyActiveWorkTransition gained byUser param — into-IP claims pulse only when !byUser (agent); new _moveByUser(card,from,to) helper reads last history event's via==='user'. Local user paths (commitCardDrag, moveCardToColumn, improveCard, reopenAsBug) pass byUser=true → never steal; SSE paths (handleCardAdded x2, handleCardUpdated, simulateUserDragMove land) derive byUser from via. card_commands.py _set_active_work unchanged (agent always claims). Review fix: improveCard/reopenAsBug now call recordMove(card,from,'inprogress') so other tabs' SSE replay classifies the reopen as a user move (was reading a stale ship event and wrongly stealing). Verified: node --check JS OK, py_compile OK. Known pre-existing (not introduced): last-writer race if a user drag's debounced POST overwrites a concurrent agent activeWorkId — self-heals on next card.py move.

cards.title: Why "Bugged" Column cannot close? Some cards created have Cross, some dont have? all DEFAULT columns cannot close. but newly created ones can
cards.notes: INTENTIONAL by design (templates/board.html L5462-5481 + guard L6731): the × delete button renders ONLY for col.kind==='custom' (excluding the system 'Discarded' col). Default/structural columns (task, inprogress, done, backlog, blocked='Bugged', etc.) are protected so core lifecycle/routing columns can't be deleted and orphan cards. 'Bugged' is the default 'blocked' slug. Not a bug — but we CAN allow deleting EMPTY non-core defaults (like Bugged) if the user wants. Awaiting decision.
cards.writeup: DIAGNOSIS (user was right — not a default): the 'Bugged' column is {id:'blocked', name:'Bugged', kind:'blocked'} — NOT in the seed layout (seed = task/notes/ideas/backlog/inprogress/super-urgent/discarded/done) and NOT created via the normal UI path (board.html addColumn L4457 assigns id:'col-xxxx', kind:'custom'). It's a legacy/mis-assigned column that got kind:'blocked', and the delete-× only renders for kind==='custom' (board.html L5468), so it could never be closed. FIX: removed the empty column via card.py column rm blocked (0 cards, no guard needed; restorable via Reset Columns). ROOT CAUSE noted: the × gating on kind==='custom' is too narrow — any legacy/non-seed column with a non-custom kind is un-closable; a durable fix would gate on a protected-core set instead. Offered that as a follow-up.

cards.title: New card pulsates again when moved to In-Progress (regression)
cards.writeup: ROOT CAUSE (diagnosed via git): NOT the active-work logic — #546 (731c629, 6/9 12:22, sticky/anchored to the card moved into IP, not time-sorted) is intact at HEAD. The regression was the #564 REVERTS this session (b3895fa + 3814da1, done for a clean baseline) which removed the one-shot _flashIds clear. Without it, _flashIds (changed-card ids set by detectChangesAndSnapshot on poll) is never consumed, so EVERY later render() — open/close a card, swap a column, add a card, or MOVE a card into In-Progress — re-applies .flashing/.pulsing to those STALE ids = "the new card pulsates again." FIX (templates/board.html end of render ~L4482): re-applied the one-shot clear (reset _flashIds to a new empty Set) after the render applies the flashes — minimal (just part 1 of #564, not the invasive part 2). Trace: poll runs detectChangesAndSnapshot then render (L3380-81), so the genuinely-changed card still flashes ONCE; stale re-flash on later renders is gone. Verified: node --check OK; live :7891 serves it. SAME mechanism as #564 (random cards glow on open/close/add/swap) — likely fixed by this too. Hard-refresh to load.

Fix in templates/board.html _renderLogLine (~L2045): omit the kind-badge span when entry.kind==='save'; the self-describing summary 'Saved checkpoint' stands alone. All other log kinds (ADD/MOVE/EDIT/etc.) keep their badge. Verified: node --check OK on the main script block; live server (:7891 serves templates/board.html fresh per request) confirmed serving the fix via curl. User sees it on hard-refresh — no reinstall needed.

cards.title: Fast successive card moves don't get logged
cards.writeup: Fast successive card drags didn't appear in the Logs HUD. Root cause: commitCardDrag never logged the move directly (unlike moveCardToColumn) — it relied on the SSE echo (handleCardUpdated→logEvent). On a rapid next-drag that echo arrives while isDragInProgress()→ handleCardUpdated bails (defer & return) BEFORE the logEvent, so the move never logged. Fix in templates/board.html commitCardDrag: log the move directly (via=user) for a real column change + set _localMoveLog dedupe marker so the echo won't double-log — mirrors moveCardToColumn. Same-column reorders don't log. Verified: node --check OK; live :7891 serves it (curl). Hard-refresh to load.

cards.title: Reconcile hardening — per #560 audit
cards.origin: #560 reconcile audit finding
cards.writeup: Reconcile hardening — 4 #560-audit follow-ups, each implemented + proven by a fixture test that PASSES on the fix and FAILS on pre-fix code (regression gate: 13/13 checks green). (1) _hook_stop_recon.py: rev-baseline windowing — credit a board-rev advance as 'this session carded' ONLY for genuine interactive mutations (history via∉{harvest,autoship}); a between-session background reconcile no longer masks an un-carded session (test_sub1 3/3; scenario A fails pre-fix). (2) _hook_subagent_recon.py: correlate each SubagentStop to its spawn by task-prompt signature (psig) instead of blind FIFO, so parallel out-of-order finishes pair the writeup to the right card; FIFO fallback preserved (test_sub2 2/2; B-first mis-pairs pre-fix). (3) hourly_reconcile.py: log every silently-dropped mover decision (hallucinated #, invented column, fly rc!=0) to stderr + 'N moved, M skipped' summary (test_sub3 4/4; 1/4 silent pre-fix). (4) hourly_common.py _LLM_PROMPT: never card assistant prose (EOD/recaps); a user bug/request → summarized task, never raw/notes (test_sub4 4/4). FINDING: the extractor was NOT the source of the live notes pile-up (#578-585) — those were last session's ASSISTANT manually carding user messages verbatim into notes (discipline lapse, cleaned this AM); prompt change is defensive. Plus dev/bench_recon.sh: a 3-day-bootstrap A/B harness run over 7 code states (june5 0.9.13 / PRE-576 / CURRENT / after each subtask), 3 runs each, median. Key empirical result: subtasks 1-4 don't move bootstrap distribution (hook/logging fixes, as predicted); june5 extractor was ~75pct more prolific (148 vs ~85 cards, mostly→done) — #570/#576 made extraction far more selective. Answered the >24h question: removing it left Done flat (64 vs 62 median), only killed the backlog stale-sweep (recon→backlog 4→0).

cards.title: Add a separator by day; for each column, there is a day. use UI UX PROMAX.
cards.writeup: ALREADY IMPLEMENTED — no code change needed. Done column groups cards by day (board.html renderColumn line 5578, gated col.id==='done'): one .day-header per day, newest-first, with a count. formatDayHeader produces exactly the requested format: 'Today · 9 Jun 2026', 'Yesterday · 8 Jun 2026', then '7 Jun 2026' etc. Verified on the live board: 382 done cards across 15 distinct days → 15 headers render (Today·9Jun 48, Yesterday·8Jun 49, 7Jun, 6Jun, 5Jun 31, …). discovered/bootstrap cards key off createdAt (the historical day), live cards off doneAt. If only 'Today' shows, refresh the browser.

cards.writeup:
Shipped the adoption-focused docs pass (claude-mem style). Scope per user: "just the README" as one impressive page + a clean docs/ entry point.

WHAT CHANGED
- README.md — rewritten from the too-simple 6/10 version into a claude-mem-style landing page: centered hero + badges, "The problem / The fix," a "What makes it different" section (4-hook enforcement table, Do-#123, cheap recall, History Replay, animated UI — all sourced from docs/KEY_FEATURES.md, not invented), a prominent 📦 Installation section (plugin path + clone path + Requirements), 🚀 Quick start transcript, 🛠️ How it works with a card.py command reference, ⚙️ Configuration (BOARD_STEWARD_STRICT, --auth-token, --project, --demo), a "vs claude-mem/mem0/letta" comparison, and Learn-more links. 73→173 lines.
- docs/README.md — NEW. Clean entry point so newcomers landing in docs/ aren't dumped into dev notes: points back to root README, splits user docs (KEY_FEATURES, TOKEN_BUDGET, BOOTSTRAP, DISCOVERY, PLAYBOOK, DEVELOPMENT) from clearly-labeled internal/dev notes. Non-destructive — no files moved.
- LICENSE — NEW. README badge+footer claimed MIT but no LICENSE file existed; added standard MIT (2026, malco) so the claim is real before any public push.

VERIFICATION
- Facts match .claude-plugin/*: version 0.9.26, repo slug malcolm1232/WorkBoard, plugin id board-steward@workboard.
- All 6 relative links resolve (KEY_FEATURES, TOKEN_BUDGET, DEVELOPMENT, CHANGELOG, LICENSE, docs/README).
- No leaked /Users/malco paths; no secrets.

DECISIONS / SPLIT-OFFS
- discover2.py naming → LEFT as-is per user (it's the live discovery engine, ~16 callers; the "2" is harmless cosmetic debt).
- dev/ folder still ships to the public plugin → split into its own card #658 (the remaining hygiene item). board/, training_data/ already gitignored; secret scan clean.
- Not committed/pushed — pre-release, gate public release until user confirms.

cards.title: Improve bootstrap card-title quality (Haiku header prompt)
cards.origin: the Titles on the cards are so much simpler than what u wrote... is the bootstrap telling haiku wrongly? can we improve the header?
cards.writeup: Sharpened the extraction title spec in _LLM_PROMPT (hourly_common.py:182). Root cause: spec asked for terse 'verb+noun ≤70 chars' with only single-clause examples, so Haiku collapsed bundled multi-part work into a generic comma-run — unlike richer live-carded titles. Fix: demand specificity (concrete subject, not vague abstract header) + teach the ' + ' multi-part glance convention from SKILL.md (≤4 segments; single-part stays plain). Verified with REAL Haiku before/after: OLD='Add column delete, drag grip, drag-to-trash discard, and smooth card reorder animation' (89ch comma-run) -> NEW='Column delete + grip drag + drag-to-trash + FLIP reorder' (the glance, matches live). Single-unit digest still yields plain title (no over-bias). Commit on main.

cards.title: Audit reconcile correctness via Explore agent
cards.origin: reconcile can we use an explore agent to identify if its doing the 'correct' thing?
cards.writeup: AUDIT COMPLETE (2 parallel Explore agents over mover + backstop; agent severities recalibrated against actual code). VERDICT: reconcile is doing the correct thing by design. Verified safety: (1) interactive sessions (CLAUDECODE=1) take the INLINE path — defer to main Claude, NO autonomous moves (hourly_reconcile.py:305); autonomous moves only in background spawns. (2) The agent's 'CRITICAL stale-candidates data loss' is OVERSTATED — stale snapshot only affects the skip-check; actual write is card.py fly subprocess re-reading LIVE board via find_card+atomic_save (atomic, rolling backups). (3) Autonomous path is lock-serialized (recon_lock, no double-run), LLM-noop-safe (malformed/empty Haiku => safe no-op), bootstrap-gated. (4) Backstop: per-turn windowing, read-only detection (only Bash tool_use counts), rev belt-and-suspenders, seeding-turn safe, loop-guarded. RESIDUAL (non-destructive) gaps carded as follow-ups: #566 rev baseline credits between-session background reconcile (missed-gap edge), #567 subagent FIFO mis-pair on true-parallel subagents (bounded by skip-markers), #568 mover silent-skip observability. No fixes applied — card was AUDIT-only.

FOUND & FIXED the real linking bug: handleCardAdded (L5971) and handleCardUpdated (L6080) did 'if (_view!=="board") return;' — they updated state but never re-rendered the Calendar, so a remote SSE change (Claude/CLI/another tab) only appeared on the calendar after a manual view switch = the 'main board ↔ calendar doesn't sync' the user asked about. cards.title: _BROAD_ROOTS hardcodes macOS /Users (lacks Linux /home) → cross-project leak off-macOS
cards.origin: User portability audit (/code-review on the discover2 chain): 'find history despite user NOT being malco, or not Desktop — check it's stable.' Discovery itself is portable, but the review found this macOS-bias in event scoping.
cards.notes: hourly_extractor.py:167 _BROAD_ROOTS = {Path.home(), home/Desktop, '/', Path('/Users')}. The literal /Users is macOS-only; Linux users-root /home is absent. In _cwd_in_project (line 186) an ANCESTOR cwd is kept unless it's a broad root — so on Linux a session run from the users-root leaks every project's events into a board (the #508 class, off-macOS). FIX: replace Path('/Users') with Path.home().parent (=/Users on macOS, /home on Linux, C:\\Users on Windows). Discovery/picker (list_projects, _is_non_project) already portable. Secondary review notes (NOT this card): convo HH:MM timezone handling + files_from_tool_use hardcoded tool list = future-fragility, different axis.
cards.writeup: Fixed in 545a7ce. _BROAD_ROOTS now uses Path.home().parent (portable users-root: /Users macOS, /home Linux, C:\\Users Win) instead of hardcoded /Users. Verified: macOS set still contains /Users (no regression); users-root ancestor scoping excluded=False, project cwd kept=True; smoke 14/14. Discovery/picker already portable (cwd read from jsonl, not slug decode; #280).

cards.title: SSE self-heal watchdog: re-open stream if it dies while board visible
cards.origin: User: 'why isnt sseClient open? if it can NOT open now, it can NOT open for other users... how can we make it mechanical/hardened?' Surfaced when fly animations didn't show (sseClients=0). Root cause was window-not-visible (expected #337 close-on-hide), but investigation found a real residual gap.
cards.notes: GAP: board.html onerror (board.html:6157) only downgrades the status badge and trusts the browser's native EventSource auto-reconnect. If the stream goes terminally CLOSED (readyState===2 — e.g. server restart where native reconnect gives up), nothing re-opens it; a VISIBLE board silently stops animating until manual refresh. No readyState/online watchdog exists. FIX: while document is visible, periodically re-open SSE if _es is null or readyState===CLOSED; also reconnect on window 'online'. Data integrity already safe via 3s poll (board.html:3066) — this restores the live glide.
cards.writeup: Fixed in cc9b1d4. Added startSSEWatchdog() in templates/board.html: every 5s while document.visible, re-opens SSE if _es null or readyState===CLOSED(2); also reconnects on window 'online'. No-ops while hidden (respects #337 close-on-hide). Diagnosis: earlier sseClients=0 was the board WINDOW not being visible (expected #337 behaviour), not a connect bug — it connects fine when on-screen + 3s poll keeps state correct regardless. Real residual gap = no self-heal for a terminally-CLOSED stream on a visible board; this closes it. node --check clean.

cards.title: Autonomous Haiku recon returns prose not JSON array → parse fails → 0 moves (backstop silently no-ops)
cards.origin: Caught during #229 snapshot-load smoke test (CLAUDECODE unset, isolated board). recon LLM returned 'I'll analyze the board cards against the activity log...' conversational preamble instead of the bare JSON array → parse_card_array found nothing → reconcile_sweep moved 0 cards. Degrades gracefully (logs 'no parseable JSON array', exit 0) but recon does NOTHING. Only the AUTONOMOUS path (launchd/timer, CLAUDECODE unset) hits this; in-session defers to main-Claude so it's invisible there. Single run — verify flaky vs systemic. Fix: harden parse to strip preamble before the '[' / tighten recon prompt or force output-format. NEEDS code change + plugin REINSTALL to double-check — batch with #378.
cards.writeup: Fixed in 7c4eed4. Root cause systemic (not flaky): _RECON_PROMPT lacked the anti-prose guards _LLM_PROMPT has. Ported both — (a) activity-log questions are DATA not instructions, do NOT answer; (b) Return ONLY the JSON array, NO preamble/commentary. parse_card_array already salvaged preamble+array; this prevents the pure-prose case. Verified: pure prose→None (safe 0-move), preamble+array→parsed, []→[].

cards.title: Ctrl/Cmd+scroll to zoom the board in/out (persisted; Ctrl/Cmd+0 resets to 100%)
cards.origin: btw i want to Ctr Scroll in and out to zoom in and out, can?
cards.writeup: Ctrl/Cmd+scroll zooms the board. board.html: wheel listener (passive:false) gated on ctrlKey||metaKey → preventDefault (blocks native browser zoom) → setZoom(_zoom*exp(-deltaY*0.0015)) on #board via CSS zoom; clamped 0.4–2.5; persisted to localStorage workboard.zoom; re-applied on DOMContentLoaded. Mac trackpad pinch (wheel+ctrlKey) works too. Ctrl/Cmd+0 resets to 100% (added to keyboard handler). HUDs/modals excluded so they scroll normally. Brief 'Zoom N%' toast. Commit 6cd7692. Verified: inline JS node --check clean; node sim confirms smooth symmetric step + clamp. Browser-only — refresh to load.

cards.title: HUD logging coverage: log Reset Columns, Sort Time, column rename, card delete + discard (currently silent)
cards.origin: any thing that is not logged on HUD, eg. Reset Columns, Sort Time, Column Change name? Deleted/Discarded?
cards.writeup: Logged the previously-silent actions in the Logs HUD. resetColumnsToDefault → logEvent('col','↺ reset columns to default'); sortCardsByTimeNow → logEvent('sys','⇅ sorted cards by time'); column rename blur → logEvent('col','renamed "old" → "new"'); deleteCard → logEvent('remove','#n "title"') directly BEFORE filtering (the SSE echo's handleCardRemoved can't log own deletes — card already removed from local state when it fires). Discard needed nothing — it routes through moveCardToColumn which already logs (#518). Commit 4c94f3d. Verified: inline JS node --check clean. Browser-only — refresh to load.

cards.title: Cmd+drag pan over EMPTY space needs a second click (works first-try over a card) — dedup vs #127 background-pan so Cmd-drag engages anywhere first-try
cards.origin: when i Cmd and drag OVER A CARD it moves as it should. but if i Cmd OVER AN EMPTY SPACE then i need to click again. can i not click again? so wherever i click its just Cmd click?
cards.writeup: Cmd+drag pan now engages first-try over empty space too. Root cause: over empty #board, the existing #127 background drag-to-pan ALSO fired; #514's mouseup stopPropagation (to swallow the click) prevented #127's document mouseup cleanup → #127 stuck panning=true → next gesture needed an extra click to reset. Over a card #127 never fires (target≠board) so it worked. Fix: #514 mousedown now e.stopPropagation() (capture phase) so #127 never co-engages for a Cmd+drag — #514 is the sole pan handler everywhere. Commit de71165. Verified: inline JS node --check clean. Browser-only — refresh to load.

cards.title: Retire shift-click multi-select (#515): remove selection state/group-move to keep backend clean — not an important feature

Verified: no _selected/clearSelection/sel-pill refs remain (only native opt.selected for <select>); inline JS node --check clean. Single-card drag + right-click move untouched. Commit 928522c.

cards.title: Right-click 'Move to' doesn't log in Logs HUD — log the move directly in moveCardToColumn (via=user), not via the flaky SSE echo
cards.origin: the right click move also doesnt show in LOGS HUD
cards.writeup: Right-click 'Move to' now logs in the Logs HUD. Root cause: moveCardToColumn (kebab/select move) relied on the SSE echo for logging like drags do, but it calls render() which disrupts that echo path → no log line. Fix: log directly (logEvent move, via=user) in moveCardToColumn + record _localMoveLog.set(card.id,ts); handleCardUpdated move branch skips logging if the card was directly logged <4s ago (consumes the marker) so the echo can't double-log. Drag path untouched (still echo-logs once). Commit 0f346b8. Verified: inline JS node --check clean. Browser-only — refresh to load.

cards.title: Reset Columns / Sort Time pill: remove terracotta border 'red lining' on hover, keep per-button glow

cards.writeup: Removed terracotta 'red lining' on the Reset Columns | Sort Time pill hover. Root cause: base header .header-pill:hover sets border-color:var(--accent), inherited by the seg-pill shell. Fix: seg-pill:hover now sets border-color:var(--card-border) (neutral), so the shell no longer outlines; per-button .seg:hover glow (rgba(217,119,87,.14)) untouched. Commit 93d822b. Browser-only — refresh to load.

cards.title: Add persistent per-board toggle to disable LIVE-startup recon (keep bootstrap recon) via board/.no-live-recon marker

cards.writeup: Per-board live-recon opt-out. hook_session_start.sh: live SessionStart sweep condition gains [ ! -f board/.no-live-recon ]; presence skips the live Haiku reconcile for that board permanently. Bootstrap recon (serve.py --bootstrap, line 192) unaffected — separate path, doesn't check the marker. Created board/.no-live-recon for WorkBoard (live recon now OFF here; bootstrap recon KEPT). One-off BOARD_NO_RECON=1 still works. Commit 5c14fa5. Verified: bash -n clean.

cards.title: Shift-click multi-select cards + drag the group to move them all at once
cards.origin: if we allow multiple cards to move, by clicking left shift to select multiple cards, can we do that?
cards.writeup: FIXED: shift-select group move only moved the dragged card. Root cause: commitCardDrag computed the group from live _selected at DROP time, so any selection change between dragstart and drop (stray click, re-render, document clear-handler) reduced it to just the dragged card. Fix: snapshot the selection into _cardDrag.group at dragstart (Array.from(_selected) when dragged card is selected & size>1); commitCardDrag uses that locked group, immune to mid-drag changes. Also shift-click stopPropagation so the empty-space clear handler can't wipe the building selection. Commit 8d61e45. Verified: node sim — group of 3 all land in done even when _selected is cleared post-dragstart; inline JS node --check clean. Browser-only — refresh to load.

subtasks.text: 🐞 fix bug: shift-click multi-select group move only moves the dragged card; group read from _selected at drop time is lost if selection changes mid-drag — lock the group at dragstart

Card dragstart bails when Cmd/Ctrl held or _boardPan active. CSS: body.cmd-pan-ready #board=grab, body.cmd-panning=grabbing + user-select:none + cards pointer-events:none. keydown/keyup Meta toggles the grab affordance. Commit 50f6692. Verified: inline JS node --check clean. Browser-only — refresh to load.

cards.title: Cmd+Z browser undo not tagged (Undo) in Logs HUD — only CLI recover was (#507); the actual keyboard/button undo shows plain MOVE
cards.origin: where is the Undo? when i Cmd Z?
cards.writeup: Browser Cmd+Z undo (and Cmd+Shift+Z redo) now tagged (Undo)/(Redo) in Logs HUD. Root cause: undo()/redo() restore a whole-state snapshot via _restoreSnap — the reverted move never goes through recordMove, so it stayed untagged (plain MOVE). Fix: new _tagRestoreMoves(snap, via) diffs the about-to-restore snapshot against live state, stamps {from:current, to:restored, at, via} as the LAST history entry on each card that visibly moves, returns the re-serialised snapshot which is what _restoreSnap now receives. undo→'undo', redo→'redo'; VIA_LABEL += Redo. Commit 8f66841. Verified via node sim: done→task revert stamps via=undo, untouched cards untouched, renders (Undo) MOVE. This is the USER-FACING undo (#507 was the CLI recover path; both complement). Browser-only — refresh to load.

cards.title: Attribute every Logs HUD move: (User) for browser drag/menu, (Agent) default for CLI card.py fly
cards.origin: i want to differentiate between user move vs claude move... so Move has (User) Move vs (Agent) Move. word: Agent
cards.writeup: Every move now attributed in Logs HUD. board.html recordMove (browser drag + kebab/select 'Move to') stamps via:'user'; card_commands._record_move defaults via='agent' for all CLI moves, with recon/undo/harvest/autoship overriding. VIA_LABEL += user:'User',agent:'Agent'; viaPrefix coloured user=teal #6fb3a8, agent=lavender #b6a8e0, automated=grey. Commit (this ship is itself an (Agent) move). Verified: plain fly→via=agent, --via recon overrides, browser via=user survives POST/diff and renders (User) teal. Refresh board to see it.

cards.title: Remove Pulsating, cos when its pulsating, i cant move other cards from one column to another. when it is oulsating, i can oNLY MOVE THAT CARD. the rest, all cant move. so for now, lets remove the pulsating. because i think the wirintg of pulsating bugs out the diffrent layers we did today.
cards.writeup: Pulse decoupled + drag freeze fixed + sticky + undo-synced. ROOT CAUSE (da41aba): reflowCardDragPreview called Animation.finish() on every dragover to settle FLIP transitions, but the active-work pulse ran 'infinite' and finish() THROWS InvalidStateError on an infinite effect — aborting the reflow board-wide the instant any card pulsed = 'can't drag while a card pulses'. Fix: only settle FINITE animations + try/catch. Also: pulse → box-shadow only (no scale) (eba711e); #494 defer render-during-drag + #499 dragstart safety net; activeWorkId rides undo/redo snapshots (3ffc4c1); STICKY active-work — new IP arrivals don't steal the glow, kept unique to the genuine WIP card (28c649a); confirmed glow is display-only, never decides which card moves. Verified live by user: dragging works while pulsing; sticky holds; undo keeps pulse in lockstep.

cards.title: When i move one card, to ONE other column, some other column bounves, idk why. when i drag and move the whole page around, then that bug is gone..
cards.writeup: Column-bounce-on-card-move resolved as a SIDE EFFECT of the #503 root-cause work (eba711e + da41aba). Mechanism: the infinite transform:scale() pulse perturbed getBoundingClientRect during FLIP measurement → neighbouring column bounced; .finish() (meant to settle it) THREW on the infinite effect so it never settled; panning fixed it via a clean full re-measure. Fix: pulse is box-shadow only (no geometry change → FLIP can't bounce) + .finish() skips infinite animations. No separate change needed.

cards.title: Within-column drag + ghost break while Claude cards (render-during-drag)
cards.origin: cos claude is working, im not able to move WITHIN columns. between works. ghost UI gone when claude is working
cards.writeup: Shipped to main in eba711e (#494 repaint-deferral + #499 dragstart safety net), the exact work this card was blocked on (was on branch feat/logs-persist). isDragInProgress()/_pendingResync defer poll+5 SSE handlers during a drag; drag-end flushes one render. Verified live.

cards.title: Re-apply cross-column top-preview rule (clean, isolated)
cards.origin: re-apply only the #479 preview rule
cards.writeup: branch feat/logs-persist: dragover handler only — cross-column hover pins beforeId to siblings[0] (top); within-column keeps cursor slot. Baseline commitCardDrag inserts before hoverBeforeCardId so preview+commit agree, no commit-side change. EXCLUDES #497 render+FLIP, #494 deferral, markSelfMove (the double-take/freeze causes). Verified: grep shows 0 of that machinery present. node --check clean; served live.

cards.title: Column drag leaves col-dragging-active stuck → card drag-drop dies
cards.origin: sometimes when i refresh or drag a column then i cant transfer from one column to another, or even within a column, in #321 i cant rearrange it
cards.writeup: templates/board.html: global capture-phase dragstart safety net clears stale drag residue (_cardDrag, _colDragSrcId, card/col-dragging-active body classes) before the real dragstart handlers run. Root cause: col-dragging-active gates card drag-drop and is cleared only in the column dragend handler, which is missed when a mid-drag render() (column-reorder revert / card move) detaches the dragged node -> class stuck -> card dragover bails before preventDefault -> cards can't be dropped; _colDragSrcId stuck also froze repaints. Commit on fix/drag-reliability. node --check clean; served live. Root (avoid full render() during column drag) left as deeper follow-up; safety net recovers within one drag.

cards.title: Harden drag fixes per code review (self-move guard + drag-state detection)
cards.origin: code review findings #1 and #2 are worth fixing now
cards.writeup: Fixes 2 correctness findings from the /code-review of fix/drag-reliability, in templates/board.html: (#1) consumeSelfMove was keyed on cardId alone, so ANY update to a just-moved card within 4s (a concurrent in-place edit, or a remote move elsewhere) was swallowed — DOM repaint skipped, no self-heal since handleRevBumped advances lastRev. Now keyed on (cardId,targetCol): only OUR move-echo (real column change landing in the column we moved to) is swallowed; in-place edits (from===to) and moves to other columns fall through to normal handling. markSelfMove now takes the target col; both call sites + consumeSelfMove updated. (#2) isDragInProgress() depended on the card/col-dragging-active body classes, removed only inside dragend listeners — a missed dragend would pin it true and freeze ALL poll/SSE repaints. Now keys on _cardDrag and _colDragSrcId (cleared deterministically in commit/cancel/col-drag-end). node --check passes; served live. Deferred (noted in review): #3 dragend rescue is intended commit-what-you-see; #5 logEvent perf; #6/#7/#8 minor/altitude.

cards.title: Cross-column drop commits to state but UI doesn't repaint (reconcile via render+FLIP)
cards.origin: now when i move my backlog 283 to super urgent, not moving, the UI isnt registering
cards.writeup: Diagnosed via board.json: #283 history showed backlog→super-urgent at 01:58:35 savedBy:user — the move DID commit + persist; only the UI failed to repaint. Root: commitCardDrag skipped render() and trusted the live-preview DOM; when the preview was stale (fast flick / deferred #494 mid-drag repaint / dropped SSE echo) + with the self-move echo suppressed, the DOM never reconciled to state → 'drop didnt register' even though data moved. Fix (templates/board.html): cross-column commit now does captureCardRects()→render()→flipPlayCards(320ms) so DOM==state always, gliding the card from wherever it sat to its committed slot; markSelfMove still suppresses the echo to avoid double-animation. Same-column reorder keeps the fast no-render preview path. node --check passes; served live. Also: headless-chrome load showed no parse/top-level JS errors in board.html.

cards.title: Drop near column edge/gap silently reverts (commit-what-you-see)
cards.origin: sometimes when i drag and drop to another column, it doesnt register
cards.writeup: templates/board.html dragend handler (code-tagged #495; re-filed as this num after the original card was removed during live drag testing). Root: a card drop only commits when released over a .column zone; releasing over a dead zone (inter-column gap, header, +Add button, floating LOGS HUD/toast, past a column edge) fired NO drop event, so dragend ran cancelCardDrag() and the card reverted — the intermittent 'drop doesnt register'. Fix: on dragend with _cardDrag still pending, if last hoverCol is a real column != srcCol (the live preview already shows the card there), commitCardDrag(); else revert. Commit-what-you-see; undoable via Cmd-Z. Successful drops unchanged. node --check passes; served live at :7891.

cards.title: Defer board repaints during drag (freeze while Claude works)
cards.origin: when claude is working, or smth in progress, all the cards cant move. why? is it still like that now?
cards.writeup: Render-during-drag freeze fixed (eba711e). Poll loop + all 5 SSE handlers now defer their repaint while a card/column is being dragged (isDragInProgress/_pendingResync); state still merges, the drag's end flushes ONE reconciling render(). #499 capture-phase dragstart safety net recovers a missed dragend. Excludes the double-take machinery (markSelfMove, commit-time render+FLIP) reverted off main.

cards.title: Persist Logs HUD history across refresh
cards.origin: i want the logs to persist. on refresh, it keeps going missing
cards.writeup: Re-applied clean on branch feat/logs-persist off baseline 72ce241 (commit ac24338), after reverting the session's tangled drag patches. templates/board.html: logEvent persists each entry to a rolling in-memory buffer _logsBuf (last 200, 7-day TTL); _logsHud replays via replayLogs() on open. Perf per code review: in-memory cache + debounced localStorage write (_logsBufSaveSoon), O(1) per line not parse+stringify+write each call. _renderLogLine shared by live + replay. Independent of drag. node --check clean; served live.

cards.title: Fix Logs HUD resize-handle inversion
cards.origin: expanded Logs HUD: dragging bottom-right corner to resize inverts (side effect of #487 bottom-anchoring)
cards.writeup: templates/board.html: new _anchorHudTopLeft(el) converts a HUD to left/top anchoring from its current rect (clears right/bottom). Called at end of setLogsMinimized (after the bottom-pinned collapse/grow), at HUD creation (after appendChild), and in resetHudsToDefault — so the native bottom-right resize handle grows normally instead of inverting. Minimize/maximize behavior unchanged (still bottom-pinned during the toggle). Commit above. Verified served + JS parses.

cards.title: Smooth FLIP glide for HUDs on reset columns
cards.origin: when the Logs go back to place on reset columns, want the smooth drag UX same as when columns are dragging
cards.writeup: templates/board.html resetHudsToDefault: capture each HUD's getBoundingClientRect BEFORE repositioning, then in a rAF play a translate FLIP from the delta (duration 1080ms, easing cubic-bezier(0.34,1.4,0.64,1)) — identical glide to flipPlayColumns. Logs + recon HUDs now smoothly glide to default on Reset columns instead of teleporting. Commit above. Verified served + JS parses.

cards.title: Trim Reset-columns confirm copy

No cards deleted — '+undoHint()+' to undo.' Dropped stacking parenthetical + 'extras by recency' + legacy-Mandatory note. Commit above. Verified served.

cards.title: Reset columns also resets Logs + recon HUD positions
cards.origin: reset columns should reset all columns including LOGS HUD and recon HUD to original position
cards.writeup: templates/board.html: resetColumnsToDefault() now calls new resetHudsToDefault() — clears localStorage (workboard.logsHudPos/Size/Min + workboard.loadHudPos) and repositions live HUDs to default (Logs → bottom-right 340x240 un-minimized; recon/load → top:110px right:12px). Button tooltip updated; toast now 'Columns + HUDs reset'. Commit above. Verified served + inline JS parses.

cards.title: Logs HUD minimize anchors to bottom edge
cards.origin: Logs card: when minimized it goes downwards/off-screen; want it to collapse upward — lower bound fixed, only upper bound moves
cards.writeup: templates/board.html setLogsMinimized: pin bottom edge (window.innerHeight - rect.bottom) + clear top before the height change, so minimize/expand keeps the lower bound fixed and moves only the top edge (collapse upward, no off-screen growth). Commit above. Verified: served HTML has the fix, inline JS parses clean. Refresh board + toggle the 📜 LOGS minimize to see it.

cards.title: Remove Velocity perpetually,  Add Column Sort time for so its Reset Columns so the pill looks like: (Reset Columns | Sort Time) in ONE pill.
cards.writeup: Removed Velocity view perpetually (tab button, #velocity div, renderVelocity(), velocity CSS, setView handling). Replaced lone 'Reset columns' button with segmented header pill (↺ Reset Columns | ⇅ Sort Time) in one shell split by hairline divider. Sort Time = sortCardsByTimeNow(): a thin pushUndo + flipResort() + toast wrapper — REUSES the existing flipResort() (#91), the same newest-first card sort (createdAt desc, num tiebreak) with FLIP glide that the board already auto-runs once during bootstrap reconcile (board.html:5857). No duplicate sort logic. File: templates/board.html. Commits 11a0d15 + f04b5e1 on main; JS syntax-checked; live server (7891) serves new markup.

cards.title: Standardize multi-part units as header card + subtasks in SKILL.md
cards.origin: User: for consistent naming across installs — a multi-part request in one unit should become a clean verb+noun header card + one subtask per part, NOT a colon-list title. Make SKILL.md mandate it (front-of-lifecycle is discipline, so the lever is the SKILL.md wording).
cards.writeup: Codified the multi-part LAW in SKILL.md (commit 2d037cf). Rewrote the Shape→pattern table into 5 numbered canonical shapes, gated by the HEADER TEST as master discriminator: if one honest 'verb+noun' header covers all parts → 1 card + N subtasks (parts as subtasks, NEVER in the title; check off each → strike-through + N/M count); if no single header → N cards (independent units, none buried). Added the explicit rule that the title stays clean verb+noun. Sharpened shape 5 (mid-task branch): the test is 'does it serve the current card's goal?' not 'am I mid-task' — yes→subtask (parent stays inprogress), no (e.g. backend work spots unrelated UI bug)→NEW card parked in Task, keep one pulse. Resolves the #474 inconsistency where multi-part work got crammed into one title.

cards.title: Reset columns: migrate legacy mandatory column → super-urgent (rename + move cards)
cards.origin: User: clicked reset, still sees 'mandatory' — legacy board's column isn't renamed. Reset should conform old boards to super-urgent.
cards.writeup: Shipped @ f84afce. resetColumnsToDefault() now runs _conformLegacyColumns() first: a legacy 'mandatory' column is renamed in place → 'super-urgent' (🚨 SUPER URGENT) with its cards moved; if super-urgent already exists, mandatory cards merge into it and the legacy column is dropped (stackUnder re-pointed). Cards moved never deleted, idempotent. So clicking Reset on a pre-#104 board now fully conforms it to the super-urgent default. Confirm copy updated. Verified HUD 24/24. To see it: refresh the board, click ↺ Reset columns → the 📌 Mandatory column upgrades to 🚨 SUPER URGENT in place.

cards.title: Unify urgent column
cards.origin: User: one urgent column. Live + reconcile both route to 🚨 super-urgent; mandatory retired; priority stays a separate axis; align digest vs prelaunch-check blocking logic
cards.writeup: Shipped @ 29d0d33 (revert anchor: good-state-260605-pre-unify-urgent). One urgent column: retired 📌 mandatory, everything routes to 🚨 super-urgent. Live + reconcile + extraction all route urgent keywords → super-urgent (was: live→super-urgent, reconcile→mandatory — the split-destination clash). super-urgent takes mandatory's exact layout slot (Discarded stacked under it). card.py fly auto-creates super-urgent if missing. Launch-block: dropped mandatory from _LAUNCH_BLOCKING_COLS + digest count now reuses the constants (DRY, no drift). Dropped mandatory from _DIGEST_ORDER/_metrics/_render/whitelists; reset-cols SPEC remaps legacy mandatory boards. Priority (critical/mid/low) untouched — separate axis. Verified: imports clean, smoke 14/14, HUD 24/24, fly→super-urgent auto-creates col, 0 leftover mandatory column refs. 11 files.

cards.title: Stamp historical doneAt at replay → drop doneSortTs discovered special-case
cards.origin: code-review #5 altitude: doneSortTs is a presentation patch for a data defect (replay stamps doneAt=now). Deeper fix: --done-at at replay so doneAt is the single source of truth; the discovered branch disappears. (Overlaps the deferred --done-at idea.)

cards.title: Centralize newest-first card comparator (3 copies)
cards.origin: code-review #4 cleanup: same comparator in flipResort, sortAllColumnsOnceAfterFill, and Done-sort. Centralize so the tiebreak rule lives once.
cards.writeup:
Closed as already-resolved (superseded by #601) — verified against current code, not assumed.

#387 wanted to centralize the "newest-first card comparator (createdAt desc + num tiebreak)" that it said lived in 3 copies: flipResort, sortAllColumnsOnceAfterFill, and Done-sort. Current state (templates/board.html):
- flipResort (~2688) uses the canonical rule via the _cardSortTs() helper (~4803): `_cardSortTs(b) - _cardSortTs(a) || (b.num||0)-(a.num||0)`. The createdAt-parsing now lives ONCE in _cardSortTs.
- sortAllColumnsOnceAfterFill — RETIRED in #601 (the silent one-shot snap-sort + its _sortOnceKey helper were removed; bootstrap uses flipResort everywhere). That copy is gone.
- "Done-sort" (~5927) sorts by doneSortTs (done-time LIFO, #89) — a DIFFERENT, intentional rule, never a duplicate of the createdAt comparator.
Other sorts (recency reorder ~4489, active-card pin ~4689, num-asc ~5286/5421) are distinct rules, not the createdAt comparator.
Net: the duplication #387 flagged no longer exists; the tiebreak rule lives once. No code change needed.

cards.title: Extract shared _flock helper (recon_lock duplicates board_lock scaffolding)
cards.origin: code-review #3 cleanup: recon_lock is a near-verbatim copy of board_lock with only the wait-loop swapped. Extract _flock(target,name,blocking,timeout) both call.

cards.title: _bootstrapFillSeen can stick true → live recon wrongly fires flipResort
cards.origin: code-review #2: flag reset only in the bootstrap-reconcile branch (board.html ~5733). If a bootstrap recon bails without a final event, flag stays true → a later standalone live recon triggers a full-board flipResort animation. Fix: reset on every final (or explicit backend after_fill signal).
cards.writeup:
Fixed: _bootstrapFillSeen no longer sticks true past the run that set it, so a standalone live recon can't be misclassified as a bootstrap and fire a spurious full-board flipResort.

ROOT CAUSE (confirmed still live before fix — user thought it was solved, it wasn't)
templates/board.html: _bootstrapFillSeen was set true on a fill stage (replay/speedup/solo, ~6921) but RESET only INSIDE the `if (d.phase === 'reconcile' && _bootstrapFillSeen)` branch. So any final that wasn't a bootstrap-reconcile left the flag set → a later standalone recon (same un-reloaded page) hit the bootstrap branch and ran flipResort. grep confirmed only 4 refs, one reset, no fix commit in git log.

FIX (templates/board.html, ~6936, single file)
Moved the reset OUT of the inner branch: on EVERY `d.final`, capture the flag into `afterBootstrapFill` then set `_bootstrapFillSeen = false` unconditionally, and branch on the captured value. The flag can no longer outlive its run. Set-point (6921) unchanged.

WHY NOT THE DEEPER FIX
The card's option B (explicit backend `after_fill` signal on the reconcile event) is the only thing that closes 100% of cases, but it's a 4-file change deep in the bootstrap/reconcile path (serve.py /progress whitelist + hourly_reconcile _emit_progress + bootstrap-vs-live caller plumbing + client) — disproportionate and risky for a [M] cosmetic glitch (worst outcome = one unexpected sort animation, no data loss). Chose the card's option A ("reset on every final").

RESIDUAL (documented in code comment, not papered over)
One narrow path remains: a bootstrap reconcile that ABNORMALLY bails emitting ZERO final events can't be cleared by an on-final reset, so the NEXT recon's final could misfire once (then self-corrects, since that final now also resets). Fully closing this needs the backend after_fill signal — offered as a follow-up. This is an abnormal-termination edge (reconcile crash), distinct from the normal-termination stick the fix eliminates.

VERIFICATION
- grep: _bootstrapFillSeen now reset unconditionally at ~6937 (every final), inside `if (d.final)` before the branch; branch reads captured afterBootstrapFill.
- Other readers: none — only the set (6921) and this consume site; change is self-contained.
- node --check on the edited block shape: parses clean.
- Served live from templates/board.html (no rebuild); reload :7891 to pick up.
- NOT committed/pushed (pre-release gate).

cards.title: Replay-gate stuck closed if bootstrap reconcile raises (no try/finally)
cards.origin: code-review #1: _mark_replay_complete runs after reconcile_sweep with no try/finally (hourly_extractor.py:899, regression from 23fdc02). If recon raises, gate stays completed_card_replay=0 → all future SessionStart recon permanently skip that board. Fix: try/finally so _mark_replay_complete always runs.
cards.writeup: Wrapped end-of-replay reconcile in try/finally (hourly_extractor.py:892-906) so _mark_replay_complete(board) always runs even if reconcile_sweep raises. Prevents completed_card_replay from sticking at 0 (which permanently disabled SessionStart recon for that board). Regression from 23fdc02. py_compile clean. Commit 26d58fb.

cards.writeup: Shipped @ c8f97bd. BOARD-SYNC HUD auto-hide 6000ms → 3250ms (board.html done()). 3.25s = brisk success-toast dwell within Material's ~2-4s snackbar range. test_hud_single updated (wait 3800ms → asserts hidden), 24/24.

cards.title: Auto-flipResort after bootstrap reconcile; remove the Sort-by-time button
cards.origin: User: don't want the UI button, keep the function, call it ONLY after recon completes, ONLY during bootstrap
cards.writeup: Shipped @ 7f41a52. Removed the '⇅ Sort by time' button + enterApp wiring; KEPT flipResort() (+ window.flipResort). The extract_progress SSE handler now sets _bootstrapFillSeen on any replay/speedup/solo phase; on the reconcile FINAL event, if a fill preceded it (= bootstrap), it auto-calls flipResort() after 600ms so all cards glide into time order — exactly once, only at bootstrap end. A standalone live SessionStart recon (no preceding fill) keeps the cheap snap-sort, so it does NOT trigger the glide. Verified board.html parses (HUD 24/24); button absent (0), function+export kept (2), flag wired at 3 sites. To watch: re-bootstrap → after RECONCILING completes, cards fly into chronological order automatically.

cards.title: Done column: sort/group bootstrap-mined cards by createdAt, live by doneAt
cards.origin: User: Done sorted by doneAt but replay stamps doneAt=now → all bunch under today. Use existing 'discovered' tag (no new tag) so only bootstrap cards key off createdAt; live cards keep doneAt.
cards.writeup: Shipped @ 9fe5de7. Done column now groups+sorts by createdAt for bootstrap-mined cards (existing 'discovered' tag → doneAt is the bogus replay timestamp) and by doneAt for live cards (keeps the 'shipped on <day>' grouping). Display-only via a doneSortTs() helper in renderColumn — no new tag, no backend/card.py/replay change. Runs every render but only changes behavior for discovered cards, so it's effectively bootstrap-scoped. Verified: live board Done regroups {06-05:80} → {06-05:28, 06-04:45, 06-03:7}; board.html parses (HUD 24/24). Fixes existing boards on refresh, no re-bootstrap.

cards.title: Fill HUD: true progress — reach N/N only when the last chunk is done (#76)
cards.origin: Shipped this revamp window (git 6456ae4)
cards.writeup:
Regression from d384010 (today's single-HUD rewrite): the bar used
cur = min(done+1, total) ('current item in flight'), so it displayed N/N
when only N-1 chunks were actually complete. When the last chunk was slow
(e.g. a recursive retry after the LLM returned non-JSON — ~54s in the
reported case), the HUD sat at a full '9/9 replaying last 24h' for ~1 min
before handing off to speed-up — looking done-then-stalled. User: 'previously
it went from replaying instantly to speed up.'

Fix: update() shows raw completed count (min(done,total)) for all phases, so
the bar reads k/N while chunk N is still running. The terminal handoff()/
done() paths still force N/N — so it snaps to N/N + speed-up the instant the
last chunk truly finishes (instant transition restored), with no premature
full bar. (Reconcile already used raw done; now unified.)

test_hud_single.py: 'starts 1-based' → 'starts at true progress 0/N'; added a
regression guard that N-1 done shows N-1/N (not premature N/N); replay still
ends at N/N via handoff. 24 checks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

User feedback: the floating left-to-right sweep wasn't wanted. Make reconcile
a plain determinate bar like the extraction phases — EMPTY at start (0/1),

- Dropped the .lh-indeterminate sweep CSS/keyframe.

cards.title: HUD: indeterminate 'loading' bar during reconcile (bootstrap + live)
cards.origin: Shipped this revamp window (git ccd96c6)
cards.writeup:
The reconcile phase emits done/total = 0/1 then 1/1, so the bar sat at a
static full 100% — it looked already done while the Haiku sweep was still
running (user screenshot). Reconcile has no real progress fraction (one LLM
call, not N chunks), so render it as an INDETERMINATE loading bar instead: a
~35% segment sweeping across while running, snapping to a full 'loaded' bar
on completion.

board.html only (no backend): new .lh-recon:not(.lh-done) .lh-fill rule
(width:35% + lh-indeterminate keyframe; transform:none beats base scaleX(0)),
and update()/handoff() clear the inline scaleX during reconcile so the CSS
sweep drives the bar. done() adds .lh-done → rule stops matching → reverts to
the full scaleX(1) bar. Both bootstrap and live go through the same
reconcile_sweep → phase:'reconcile' events, so this fixes both.

test_hud_single.py +2 assertions (partial bar during sweep fillRatio<0.6,
full bar on completion fillRatio>0.9) → 22 checks.

commit bdac38e

cards.title: Fix bootstrap reconcile: premature COMPLETE + concurrent recon race (#76)
cards.origin: Shipped this revamp window (git 23fdc02)
cards.writeup:
Two distinct defects produced the user's bug report (HUD flashes ✓COMPLETE
then RECONCILING reappears; multiple recon passes race → 'already up to
date' then cards shuffle then 'N up to date', repeated):

1. Premature '✓ COMPLETE'. The tier-fly path runs reconcile OUTSIDE the
   window, so the speedup/solo tier was called reconcile=False and its last
   chunk computed is_final=True -> HUD done()+auto-hide, THEN the end-of-
   replay sweep re-showed the HUD. Thread will_reconcile through
   _run_window -> _extract_haiku so the last tier HANDS OFF to the reconcile
   stage on the same HUD instead of finalizing.

2. Concurrent reconcile passes. _mark_replay_complete() flipped the replay
   gate to 'done' BEFORE the end-of-replay reconcile ran, so a SessionStart
   --reconcile-only firing in that ~10s window saw the gate open and raced
   it; nothing serialized reconcile. Now: (a) reconcile FIRST, flip the gate
   AFTER (gate stays closed for the sweep's duration -> recon-only stands
   down), and (b) new _boardio.recon_lock — a bail-if-held flock on
   <board>/.recon.lock that makes any second concurrent reconcile skip.

Result: EXTRACTING -> speeding up -> RECONCILING on one coherent

commit 70680cf

cards.title: Gate reconcile behind completed_card_replay (no recon during fly-in)
cards.origin: Shipped this revamp window (git 0cf2ea7)
cards.writeup:
The bootstrap fly-in and a concurrent SessionStart recon-only pass both
reconciled the board while cards were still streaming in — cards 'jumped
all over the place'. Two reconciles + live emits racing the same board.

Add a durable replay gate (board/.replay_state.json):
- run() tier-fly: mark completed_card_replay=0 up front, run ALL tiers with
  reconcile=False, flip to 1 only after the LAST tier finishes, then run the
  single reconcile_sweep ONCE against the whole replay span.
- _run_reconcile_only() (SessionStart): stand down entirely while a replay is
  in progress (completed_card_replay==0); resumes next session.
- Default-open: boards with no replay state reconcile as before.

Literal semantics the user asked for: if completed_card_replay==1 then reconcile.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit 217f35c

cards.title: Subagent hook: card-tracking mode dial off/subtask/collab (#79)
cards.origin: Shipped this revamp window (git 8d93c64)
cards.writeup:
Stops auto-carding every subagent as a top-level card (the pollution that
created 5 orphan #68-72 sim cards). New mode dial, resolved per board
(env BOARD_SUBAGENT_CARDS -> board.settings.subagentCards -> default):
  - off     : no subagent tracking.
  - subtask : (DEFAULT, =1c) subagent -> subtask of the active In-Progress
              card; NO active card -> create NOTHING (internal helper = noise).
  - collab  : (=1b opt-in) subagent -> own child card linked to an epic
              (settings.subagentEpic), mirroring the agent tree for
              agent-to-agent product builds.
do_stop closes the subtask in subtask mode, flies the child card done in
collab mode; FIFO queue + skip markers keep spawn<->stop aligned. Helpers:
resolve_mode / active_card_num (activeWorkId, fallback newest IP) / epic_num.

Tested: subtask+active=subtask(closed on stop); subtask+no-active=nothing;
off=nothing; collab=linked child card flown done.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit ccb9c55

cards.title: Stop backstop: kill two false-positive classes (#78)
cards.origin: Shipped this revamp window (git 83bfd9b)
cards.writeup:
The blocking un-carded backstop false-fired on (1) turns editing files
OUTSIDE the board project (~/.claude memory files, other repos) and
(2) the cross-turn carding pattern (fly inprogress in turn N, edits in
N+1, fly done in N+2 — the middle edit-heavy turn ran no card.py).

Fix:
- _in_scope_edits(): count only edit/write tool_uses whose file_path is
  INSIDE project_root (board_path.parent.parent). Out-of-project edits no
  longer push a turn over EDIT_THRESHOLD. Unknown/unparseable path counts
  (conservative). scan_transcript() takes project_root.
- uncarded_risk also requires NOT inprogress: an in-flight card proves the
  unit is declared live, so the middle turn won't block. The deferred
  In-Progress reminder still fires (non-blocking).

Genuine misses (in-project edits, no card.py, no rev bump, nothing in
flight) STILL block. Verified: genuine-miss blocks; <threshold / IP-card /
out-of-project all don't; path scoping unit-tested.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

cards.title: gitignore .reinstall-backups/ (local plugin-reinstall state)

commit a5cd209

cards.title: feat(hooks): auto-card subagents via SubagentStop + Agent-spawn hooks
cards.origin: Shipped this revamp window (git 9339d5a)
cards.writeup:
Every subagent (Agent tool / Workflow agent) now auto-cards regardless of
source — closing the one blind spot from project_agent_to_agent_next.md (no
SubagentStop hook was wired, so spawned Claudes never flew onto the board).

- PreToolUse(matcher 'Agent') -> hook_subagent_spawn.sh: adds a card and flies
  it task -> inprogress, records it on a FIFO sidecar queue.
- SubagentStop -> hook_subagent_stop.sh: pops the oldest queued card and flies
  it -> done, scanning the subagent transcript for nested spawns (-> subtask)
  and explicit bug markers (-> writeup warning, never auto-tagged).
- Read-only subagent types (Explore/Plan) are skipped; a skip-marker keeps the
  FIFO aligned when read-only + working subagents interleave sequentially.
- Carding is done BY the hook (autonomous) — the subagent need not know the
  board protocol. Hooks never block the subagent. Silent-fail, 6s timeout.

Correct for sequential subagents (the claude -p agent-to-agent loop); parallel
completions may mis-pair a writeup, which is logged in the writeup itself.

plugin.json 0.9.2 -> 0.9.3.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

preserving order, so one global sort makes every column newest-first; the
In-Progress active-work top-pin still floats over this base order. Per-board
localStorage guard fires it exactly once ever (never on refresh/reconnect);
normal live use (new cards at top) resumes after. Replaces the reverted animated
version (#100).

commit 5eb8f93

cards.title: fix(#508): attribute each turn to a project by FILES TOUCHED, not just cwd
cards.origin: Shipped this revamp window (git 63a4cef)
cards.writeup:
Follow-up to the cwd-scope fix. cwd is frequently $HOME while a turn edits
another project's files by absolute path (e.g. a home session editing
WorkBoard/templates/board.html) — so cwd alone BOTH leaks other projects' work
AND drops legit in-project work done from home. The decisive signal is what the
turn changed.

New _event_in_project(event, project): if the turn edited a file under the
project → belongs here (whatever the cwd); if it edited ONLY other projects'
files → excluded; no file signal (prompts, memory/plans/convo) → fall back to
cwd scoping. _filter_events now uses it. This is what lets ONE conversation that
hops between projects split cleanly per unit of work.

Verified on 10d real history: WorkBoard keeps 1703 WB-file edits, 0 Edu-file
leaks, and 43 home-cwd-but-WorkBoard-file turns now correctly KEPT (cwd-only
dropped them); Edu symmetric (248 kept, 0 WB leak).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit 63a4cef

cards.title: fix(#508): scope ALL cwd-bearing events to the project, not just prompts
cards.origin: Shipped this revamp window (git 9f71e32)
cards.writeup:
Education-platform cards were flying onto the WorkBoard board. Root cause:
_filter_events scoped only user_prompt events by cwd —
  scoped = [e for e in events if e["kind"] != "user_prompt" or _cwd_in_project(...)]
so asst_msg work-turns bypassed the check; 1080 Edu-cwd asst_msg events survived
the WorkBoard scope filter and became 32 Edu cards. Secondary leak:
_cwd_in_project's ancestor clause treated $HOME/~Desktop (ancestors of every
project) as in-project, so any home session leaked into every board.

- _filter_events: scope every event via _cwd_in_project (empty-cwd events —
  memory/plans/convo — still kept by the empty-cwd rule).
- _cwd_in_project: add _BROAD_ROOTS guard ($HOME, ~/Desktop, /, /Users) so an
  ancestor-cwd only counts when it's a real parent project, never a broad root.

Verified on 7d of real history: WorkBoard Edu-leak 1080→0, home-leak 1654→0,
all 15663 own-cwd events kept; HFTAgents 81 own / 0 leak; Edu keeps its own 1139.

commit 7077b86

cards.title: fix(#381): demo port mismatch + real-registry pollution
cards.origin: Shipped this revamp window (git c04d1bc)
cards.writeup:
Mock-install run reported "server did not come up" though the board was up on
a different port. Two bugs from the #374/#377 port-registry work:

1. --demo polluted the REAL ~/.board-steward/*.json: it isolated
   CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR but not the registry, so each demo wrote its /tmp board
   dir into the live registry. Teardown is manual, so the temp dir lingered →
   never GC'd (Path.exists() stays true) → permanently hogged a port. Fix:
   export BOARD_ASSIGNMENTS + BOARD_REGISTRY into DEMO_HOME. Real registry
   stays pristine; demo gets a clean slate so its preferred port is free.

2. install.sh assumed --port was authoritative, but port_registry.assign()
   bumps it when the preferred port is designated to another board. serve.py
   then bound the bumped port while install.sh health-checked the preferred one
   → false failure (also wrong final URL for real multi-project installs). Fix:
   resolve the sticky designated port via assign() (idempotent) BEFORE launch +
   health-check, so install.sh expects exactly what serve.py binds.

Verified: re-ran --demo --harvest --fill haiku → bound expected 7896, 115 cards
filled, real registry untouched (only 7891/7892/7893), demo wrote its
