cards.title: Fix: install_hooks.py --uninstall misses hooks installed under a different path
cards.origin: u said some bug about uninstall right? can u card the bug
cards.notes: REPRO (hit live this session): hooks were wired to /Users/malco/.agents/skills/board-steward/scripts/hook_*.sh (older install location). Running 'install_hooks.py --uninstall' from the repo reported user-prompt-submit/pre-tool-use/stop -> 'not-installed' and LEFT all 3 board-steward hook entries (SessionStart/UserPromptSubmit/Stop) in settings.json, now broken (point at a deleted dir). Had to strip manually. ROOT CAUSE: --uninstall matches by the command path recomputed from __file__, so hooks whose command points at any OTHER install path are invisible. FIX: match/remove by stable marker (command contains 'board-steward' or hook_*.sh basename) not exact path. Affects anyone who reinstalls/relocates the skill.
cards.writeup: Shipped 7f3c870. ROOT CAUSE: remove_hook matched by the absolute path recomputed from __file__, so hooks wired under a different/older skill dir (~/.agents/skills/...) never matched → 'not-installed', stale broken entry left (hit live this session). FIX: added _same_script() matching by hook-script BASENAME (stable across install locations); remove_hook + add_hook both use it. BONUS: add_hook now REPLACES a same-script hook at a different path ('relinked') instead of duplicating — fixes the double-SessionStart seen on relocate. Verified: synthetic stale ~/.agents hook now removed on --uninstall (otherSetting preserved); install-with-stale yields exactly 1 canonical entry; smoke 14/14 green.

cards.title: HUD persists across hard-refresh / alt-tab — server replays last extract_progress to new SSE clients (#353 follow-up)

cards.writeup: Shipped 3958b21 (pushed; restarted :7892 from synced installed copy). Server now caches the last extract_progress in _last_progress and replays it to every NEW /events connection, clearing it once done>=total. Fixes: (1) hard-refresh mid-extraction → HUD was blank until next chunk's event; now paints current X/Y on connect. (2) alt-tab reconnect → no longer appears stuck then lurches; gets current state instantly. #353 had only patched the client refocus path. VERIFIED on live :7892: fresh connection replays cached done:4/14 with no new POST; after done>=total a fresh connection gets no replay (HUD stays closed). NOTE during debug: the server runs the INSTALLED skill copy, which only syncs from the repo on git commit — tests against pre-commit code falsely showed no replay.

cards.title: Add bottom breathing room under columns — pad #board bottom so columns aren't flush against the page edge

cards.writeup: Shipped 456488a (pushed; final take, supersedes 55959db/165b283/93eda8e). ROOT CAUSE confirmed: #board is a scrollable display:flex container and columns overflow it; Chromium/WebKit drop a flex child's end-side padding AND margin from the scroll extent, so neither #board padding-bottom nor .column-stack margin-bottom ever rendered. FINAL FIX (user's idea): shrink the scroll container itself — #board height calc(100% - 48px) → calc(100% - 48px - 120px) — so the tallest column stops 120px short of the page bottom and real empty body space shows below. Reverted the dead .column-stack margin. CSS-only; board/ & templates/ byte-identical; live on :7892 (new height rule present, old margin gone). Port confusion (3 servers, hook pointed at :7893=Edu) also cleared this session — now a single clean :7892.

cards.title: Extraction LLM over-assigns 'in progress' to historical cards (most past work is actually Done) → In Progress column piles up. Tighten the extraction prompt to default finished-looking historical buckets to Done, reducing reliance on recon as the backstop.
cards.notes:
REVERTED 6/10 (user): scrapped the #350 fix entirely. discover2_sources.py + hourly_reconcile.py git-checkout'd back to b3895fa (verified byte-identical; all #350 markers grep to 0). Extraction + reconcile = exactly pre-#350. NOTE: reverting hourly_reconcile.py also removed #566 subtask-3 (skip-logging) which lived in the same file — recoverable. NEXT (when ready): redo as RECON-ONLY — touch only hourly_reconcile.py (recon-local commit-file fetch), never the shared harvest, so bootstrap carding stays unchanged.
CORRECTION: the recon-only redo referenced above was ALREADY DONE — shipped as #588 (RECON-350, commit 6ab1b9e): file-overlap→done + duplicate-of-done in hourly_reconcile.py only; bootstrap untouched. reconly6 tried & reverted (no aggregate gain), settled on reconly5=#588. Follow-up card #594 was a mistaken duplicate, now discarded.
cards.writeup:
CLOSED 6/10 — extraction-side approach ABANDONED (decision: recon-only instead).

WHAT HAPPENED: the #350 fix tried to cut In-Progress pile-up by adding a shared git harvest (discover2_sources.py --name-only) so extraction could see commit file-lists. That harvest fed BOTH bootstrap extraction and reconcile, so it polluted carding, not just recon.

OUTCOME / WHY WE STOPPED: clean A/B showed an extraction-side performance regression — total cards 96 to 81, done 67 to 54 with the change in. The shared-harvest fix degraded bootstrap extraction quality. Net: not worth it on the extraction side.

ACTION TAKEN: reverted entirely 6/10 to b3895fa. discover2_sources.py + hourly_reconcile.py git-checkout'd back (verified byte-identical; all #350 markers grep to 0). Extraction + reconcile now == exactly pre-#350.

DECISION: do NOT touch extraction/bootstrap for this. Redo as RECON-ONLY — touch only hourly_reconcile.py (recon-local _commit_file_map git call), never the shared harvest, so bootstrap carding stays at baseline and any delta is pure reconcile. Tracked in follow-up card.

CAVEAT: the revert also removed #566 subtask-3 (skip-logging), which lived in hourly_reconcile.py — recoverable, tracked separately.

subtasks.text: 🐞 fix bug: REOPENED 6/10: premature close. The sub4 benchmark proves the over-assign-to-In-Progress problem persists — run1 had 12 IP cards, several of which are ACTUALLY DONE work (e.g. 'Fix test_sub4', 'Bootstrap perf rig', 'Board UI fixes #580' — all shipped this session). Root cause is deeper than the prompt: git commits ARE harvested, but extraction runs per ≤2-bucket CHUNK independently, so a unit's edits (chunk N) and its commit (chunk N+k) are seen separately → the edit-chunk routes 'edited, no ship'→inprogress and the commit never reconnects. Reconcile is too conservative to sweep them. Real fix candidates: (a) cross-chunk commit matching, (b) more aggressive reconcile match of stranded-IP historical cards to file-touching commits, (c) wider chunks. Not just a prompt tweak.

FIXED + empirically proven: (1) git harvest captures commit files (--name-only); (2) reconcile digest shows commit file-lists + edit activity, commits never truncated; (3) prompt: file-overlap→done + prefer-done for historical IP; (4) done-card block for duplicate-of-done matching. Fixture test_recon_filematch.py: FIXED 3/3 vs PRE-FIX 1/3 (catches file-overlap IP #1 + dup-of-done #3, control #4 stays). Aggregate bootstrap benchmark running.

RE-SCOPE (user): the fix must touch ONLY the Reconciliation code, NOT extraction/bootstrap carding. The shared git harvest (discover2_sources.py --name-only) polluted extraction (clean A/B: total 96→81, done 67→54 — extraction-side regression). Plan: (a) git checkout discover2_sources.py → extraction back to pre-#350; (b) move commit-file fetching INTO hourly_reconcile.py via a recon-local git call (_commit_file_map) so file-overlap matching keeps working with ZERO extraction impact; (c) keep digest/prompt/done-block/--force. Then re-run clean A/B — now extraction is identical baseline vs fixed, so any delta is PURE reconcile.

cards.title: Recon silently DEFERS when run inside Claude Code (CLAUDECODE=1): reconcile_sweep writes recon_pending.json instead of sweeping → in-progress cards never promoted to Done in the autonomous demo. Verify recon actually sweeps in a real terminal install (CLAUDECODE unset); consider forcing the autonomous Haiku recon for --harvest fills so the demo reflects real behavior.
In the card modal, Enter closes it (go 'back', like clicking outside) — except in textareas/inputs/buttons where Enter has a job; title commits then closes.
cards.writeup: Shipped. Enter in an open card modal closes it (go back, like clicking the backdrop). Two parts: (1) title contenteditable keydown — Enter → blur(commit) + closeModal; (2) global keydown — Enter when a card is open AND focus is on a non-editable element (not TEXTAREA/INPUT/BUTTON/contenteditable) → closeModal. So notes/writeup keep Enter=newline, tag/link/subtask inputs keep Enter=add, buttons keep Enter=activate; only modifierless Enter triggers close. closeModal renders (#297) + restores scroll (#345). node --check OK; board/templates synced.

cards.title: Adding a card / any render() resets column scroll position — preserve scrollTop (& board scrollLeft) across re-render. e.g. scrolled 83% down Done, open/add a card, snaps to top.
cards.writeup: Shipped f2b2c7d. render() rebuilds board.innerHTML wholesale → resets every column scrollTop + board scrollLeft to 0, so any re-render (3s poll tick, another card added, closeModal #297) snapped you back to top even when 83% down Done. Fix: _captureScroll() (keyed by column id) at render() start before the wipe, _restoreScroll() after playEntrance() — a re-render is now invisible to scroll position. node --check OK; board/templates synced. Live on :7892 after reload.

cards.title: Tab-refocus: snap to current state instead of replaying the queued SSE animation burst (alt-tab during a live fill 're-flies' the whole board)
cards.writeup:
Two passes. PASS 1 (bd36ec7) muted the refocus animation (seen-set + _animSuppressed) — but that did NOT fix the user's actual symptom: mid-fill, alt-tab back FROZE the screen 'catching up'. PASS 2 (20e4987) is the real fix: the freeze is DOM CHURN, not animation — a throttled hidden tab buffers every SSE card-added, then dispatches the whole backlog at once on return, and handleCardAdded builds+inserts a card per event → main thread blocks.

REAL FIX (board.html visibilitychange): on →hidden, CLOSE the EventSource (nothing buffers while away; poll is throttled too → hidden tab does zero work). On →visible, ONE silent re-sync to canonical state (single render, not N incremental inserts) under a 1.2s anim-suppress window, then reopen the stream via startSSE(). No backlog → no freeze. Animation-suppress + playEntrance/handleCardAdded gating from pass 1 still cover the brief reopen window.

VERIFIED: node --check OK; board/ & templates/ byte-identical; pushed. Real test = alt-tab DURING a live fill, return → board snaps to current, no freeze, fill continues.

cards.title: Wire two-tier fly into install.sh --harvest path + tier-2 5× (follow-up to #327)
cards.writeup:
Shipped 2f86e6e. Root cause of #327 'still not flying' on the demo: the tier logic only lived in serve_bootstrap, so install.sh --harvest ran the extractor in ONE flat pass (63 chunks, no --show-lifecycle) → pop, not fly, no tier-1→speedup.

FIX (one source of truth): centralized tiering in the extractor via --tier-fly + new _run_window() helper. serve_bootstrap's two-call loop collapsed to a single --tier-fly call (dedup); install.sh --harvest haiku fill passes --tier-fly. tier-2 pace set to pace_s/5 (5× faster than tier-1; tier-1 unchanged at 0.3) per user.

VERIFIED live on :7896 5-day demo: log shows two passes — [replay] 45 cards (10 chunks) then [speedup] 314 cards (54 chunks) — 'haiku fill complete', 361-card board with cards mid-flight (inprogress:28). Both serve_bootstrap (non-harvest) and install.sh (harvest) now identical via the shared flag.

cards.title: max length of the column = 1.5x of screen? idk? so then it scrolls within the column up and down, if not if have 100 notes, i keep scrolling down all the way; esp for done? or we can do +50 more etc kind?
cards.writeup: Shipped 8690c14. Long columns (esp. Done with 100+) grew the whole page instead of scrolling within. Root cause: .column max-height:100% can't resolve because the #117 .column-stack wrapper has no definite height. Fix: .column-body gets a vh-based max-height calc(100vh - 136px) — layout-chain-independent — so each column scrolls internally (standard kanban) with no page-long scroll. CSS-only; ~136px reserves the page header + paddings + column header. Caveat: multi-column #117 stacks could be slightly tall (rare advanced case); flat columns (the reported Done case) fixed. board/templates synced.

cards.title: Card 'Move to' menu — kebab ⋯ + right-click popup to relocate a card with no drag (lists every other column → FLIP-glides); Delete moved into the menu. Complements #332 edge auto-scroll.
cards.writeup:
Shipped in a179781. No-drag way to relocate a card — complements #332 edge auto-scroll.

WHAT: card head gains a hover-revealed kebab ⋯; right-click opens the same body-level popup menu (escapes column overflow:hidden). Menu = 'Move to' section (one item per OTHER column → card FLIP-glides there) + Delete. Closes on pick / click-away / Escape / re-render.

ARCH (DRY + isolated, per VISION rule): added one canonical moveCardToColumn(card,targetCol) = the single source of truth for a non-drag move (doneAt + #254 active-work follow + FLIP glide). The editor's Column <select> onchange now calls it too, DELETING its duplicated move logic. New menu functions (openCardMenu/closeCardMenu) are a self-contained block; existing per-element drag handlers untouched. Delete rescued from the invisible right-click-only path into the visible menu.

VERIFIED: node --check on full inline script = OK; board/ & templates/ byte-identical; functions live over HTTP on :7892 (moveCardToColumn, openCardMenu, card-kebab all present); installed skill template synced.

TEST: hover a card → ⋯ appears top-right → click (or right-click anywhere on the card) → pick a column → card glides there. Works regardless of how far the target column is.

cards.title: 1. Make the drag reach → edge auto-scroll: while dragging, if the cursor gets  within ~60px of a viewport/column edge, the board pans in that direction  (speed ramps with proximity). This is what Trello/Linear/Notion all do — the  user never thinks about it, they just drag toward the edge and the board  follows. Downside on a trackpad: you have to hold the drag pinned at the very  edge, and macOS momentum-scroll can fight it. It's polish, not a guarantee.  2. Avoid the drag entirely → a "Move to ▸" command that lists the columns;  pick one and the card FLIP-glides there (the board already has that  animation). Works no matter how far the target is, is  trackpad/keyboard-friendly and accessible, and is on-brand because you'd see  the same glide as a manual drag. This is the bigger win, and your instinct is  right.  On your specific UI ideas: I'd steer away from a split-button welded to "Exit"  (one half exits, the caret opens move) — split-buttons with two unrelated  actions are a classic mis-click trap, and mixing "leave this card" with  "relocate this card" muddies the meaning. The cleaner version of the same  instinct is a dedicated kebab ⋯ menu that consolidates everything currently  hidden: Move to ▸ (column list), Delete (rescued from the invisible  right-click), etc. Right-click can still trigger the same menu for power  users, but it stops being the only way to do anything.  My recommendation: build the "Move to ▸" menu first (reliable, on-brand, fixes  the actual blocker), then add edge auto-scroll as polish so the manual drag  also works over distance. Here's how the menu placement could look — which do  you prefer?
cards.writeup:
Shipped in 404331a (+ ref fix 2bb6eaf). Self-contained edge auto-scroll module added to board.html (and templates/board.html, byte-identical) just after the DOMContentLoaded block. Resolves original filing #303.

HOW: a document-level passive 'dragover' listener records live cursor coords and starts a requestAnimationFrame loop; 'drop'+'dragend' stop it. Each frame _asStep() pans two axes when the cursor is within AS_EDGE(60px) of an edge, speed ramping 0->AS_MAX_SPEED(18px/frame) with proximity:
  - #board       -> scrollLeft (reach off-screen columns horizontally)
  - .column-body -> scrollTop  (reach a card far down a long column; hover target via document.elementFromPoint)
Driven ONLY by document drag events, so existing per-element card/column drag handlers are untouched (no back-edges; isolated addition per VISION arch rule).

VERIFIED: node --check on full 164KB inline script = OK; board/ & templates/ byte-identical; live on :7892 (served bytes==disk, dragover listener + AS_EDGE/AS_MAX_SPEED present over HTTP); installed skill template synced (fresh installs get it).

CAVEAT (flagged to user): on a trackpad you must hold the drag pinned at the edge and macOS momentum-scroll can fight it -- polish, not a guarantee. Complementary no-drag 'Move to' menu remains an option if this proves fiddly.

cards.title: Check Pacing, if i want to do pacing for Tier 2.. OR ALL OF them flies is. cos suddenly fly in for first 21 chunks then suddenly pops up "feels buggy". im thinking of initially first 1 day flies in... then says "speeding up" UI  to increase population so UI UX not so "buggy"
cards.writeup:
Shipped in 28906a8. Simplified per user (no gradual ramp — tier-2 just reuses tier-1's fly-in at 2×).

WHAT:
1. PACING — tier-2 now runs with --show-lifecycle --pace 0.15 (same one-by-one task→IP→done fly-in as tier-1, just 2× faster than tier-1's 0.3). Replaces the old flat --pace 0.02 flood, so older history flies in like the watched tier instead of popping in all at once. (serve_bootstrap.py)
2. HUD WORDING — fixed the bug where a haiku fill wrongly showed 'inline extraction'. A new 'phase' tag travels extractor → card.py progress → /progress relay → SSE → HUD header: replay→'replaying last 24h', speedup→'speeding up ▸▸ backfill', ''/inline unchanged (main-Claude path). At tier-1's end the HUD HANDS OFF — shows 'day-1 replayed in Xs · speeding up ▸▸' and stays visible — instead of flashing '✓ COMPLETE' right before the backfill (that flash was a big part of 'feels buggy').

FILES (one-liners each for the phase plumbing): card.py (--phase), card_commands.py (phase in POST), serve.py (phase in SSE relay), hourly_emit.py (phase + handoff label_override through banner), hourly_extractor.py (phase param + t0 timing + handoff label), serve_bootstrap.py (tier phases + tier-2 lifecycle×2), board.html (_lhHeader + HUD.handoff + phase-aware update + SSE routing).

VERIFIED: py_compile + import all 6 scripts OK; node --check board.html OK; board/ & templates/ byte-identical; pushed. NOTE: live :7892 server needs a restart to pick up the serve.py relay change (board.html HUD is live on reload). Full visual verify = watch a fresh multi-day harvest.
cards.origin:
│ tier-1 (today)   │ 81    │ deliberate one-by-one trickle (~0.8/sec) +   │
  │                  │       │ flights — ~100s                              │
  ├──────────────────┼───────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ tier-2 (older 4  │ 320   │ fast flood (pace 0.02, no flights)           │
  │ days)            │       │                                              │

______________


I ASKED:
❯ wait initally was only 21 chunks..., why is there suddnely inline extraction; 
  chunk / 61? huh? i thought we are doing only haiku?                           
 

⏺ Ah, I see exactly what's confusing you — and it's all haiku, nothing switched 
  to inline. Two separate things are getting conflated:

  1. The "61 chunks" — that's just the bigger window (still haiku)

  From the actual execution log (both tiers say mode=haiku):
  tier-1 (last 1d):  24 buckets → 12 chunks  (mode=haiku, workers=8)  → 81 cards
  tier-2 (older ≤5d): 122 buckets → 61 chunks (mode=haiku, workers=8)  → 383
  cards
  - The earlier "21 chunks" was a 2-day window. This is 5 days, and tier-2 alone
  covers the older 4 days = 122 buckets = 61 chunks. More days → more chunks.
  That's the HUD's "X/61" — it's tier-2's haiku backfill, not inline.

  2. The "inline extraction" you're seeing is CARD TEXT, not the current run
  
  The board just reconstructed this entire session's history into 401 cards —
  and a big chunk of today's actual work was about inline mode. So you're seeing
  history cards like:
  - "Inline-only extraction with burst-paced fly-in…"
  - "install.sh --harvest: stage inline extraction…"
  - "Confirm bootstrap mode… session is running inline (not haiku)…"
  
  Those are cards describing past work, not the current run being inline. It's a
  bit of a hall-of-mirrors moment: the haiku fill is carding our conversation,
  and our conversation talked about inline a lot. 😄

  Proof it's pure haiku: the run log has only mode=haiku lines — zero
  mode=inline, and no extraction_pending.json was staged (that file is the
  signature of inline; there's none on this board).
  
  So: ✅ all haiku, workers=8, the 61 is tier-2's 5-day backfill, and "inline"
  is just the subject of the old cards it reconstructed. Want me to scrub the
  board for you to confirm, or is the mystery solved?

cards.title: Haiku wall-time: workers=8 + MCP-strip + pace tier-split (~2.4×)
cards.origin: User: still want lower wall time — 60-day fills take too long. What else, now that we know thinking was the cause?
cards.writeup:
Approved-plan experiments (--days 5, 73 chunks, thinking-off), then applied winners (9f3cfee):
• A1 boot-floor: --strict-mcp-config cuts ~2s/call ("hi" 4.8→2.9s) — MCP re-init was real boot cost. --disable-slash-commands BREAKS (rc1). Centralized as _LLM_ARGS.
• A2 workers: 4→8 = 2.0× (74→37s), 0 failures; 8→12 diminishing (1.27×). New default 8.
• A3 chunk-size: NOT a lever — bigger = slower (generation-bound + under-utilize workers) AND merged cards (89→56). Kept at 2.
• NEW serial floor found: pace 0.3 × hundreds of cards = ~136s of emission sleep. tier-2 bulk backfill → --pace 0.02; tier-1 keeps watched one-by-one pace + lifecycle (lifecycle now tier-1-only).
RESULT: --days 5 harvest 337s → 142s (~2.4×), 0 timeouts, cards intact. Same factor → 60-day. Remaining floor = generation (jsonl content ÷ workers); only more workers or less content beats it.

cards.title: Fresh-install default → haiku (autonomous) + demo auth fix
cards.origin: User: make a FRESH session install run smoothly end-to-end like 'npx claude-mem install' — autonomous, no manual step. Change the defaults.
cards.writeup:
3-way audit workflow (architecture / defaults / e2e trace), then fixes:
• ARCH: removed 2 orphaned `import re` (b7817d9); audit otherwise clean — no cycles, leaf invariant holds (AST-verified), VISION map accurate.
• DEFAULT FLIP (e09fb68): serve.py --bootstrap-mode + install.sh FILL inline→haiku. Flagless fresh install now fills autonomously (no main-Claude step) — thinking-off + salvage make haiku fast+robust enough to be default.
• AUTH BUG (trace agent found it): --demo isolates CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR→empty→claude -p exit 1→0 cards while printing "fill complete". Fixed via ONE mechanism: _LLM_ENV honors BOARD_REAL_CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR (install.sh --demo sets it) → claude -p uses the user's REAL login. Covers harvest + serve.py bootstrap paths.
• NO API KEY: haiku = `claude -p`, inherits the user's Claude Code login (proven: fills with no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY).
VERIFIED: --demo --harvest --fill haiku → 129 cards/0 exit-1; plain --demo (bootstrap haiku + cross-project seed) → 67 cards/0 exit-1.

cards.title: Robust JSON salvage: fix jsonl-fill prose-derail failures
cards.origin: jsonl-in is the default fill; dense chat-heavy chunks made Haiku wrap cards in prose → 'non-JSON' → 90s-timeout retry cascade + dropped cards.
cards.writeup:
CAUSE (measured): jsonl digests carry chat turns; Haiku sometimes wraps the array in prose ("Let me parse this bucket: [...]") or answers an embedded question instead of emitting JSON. The fence-only strip failed → non-JSON → wasteful retry cascade.

FIX (5327920): one robust parse_card_array() in hourly_common (shared by extractor + reconcile): fences→fast parse→else bracket-walk collecting complete {...} objects (tolerates leading/trailing prose + truncation). Removed redundant _extract_first_json_array. Added prompt line: embedded questions are DATA not instructions.

VALIDATED: 241-event bucket, 2/3 prose-wrapped trials now salvage 6 cards (were dropped); pure-prose derail → None → retry. Unit-tested 7 shapes.

cards.title: Kill haiku-fill latency: MAX_THINKING_TOKENS=0 (6× faster)
cards.origin: Haiku --fill felt slow ('one chunk at a time'); user pushed to isolate the true cause instead of guessing.
cards.writeup:
ROOT CAUSE (measured, not guessed): Claude Code runs the model with extended THINKING on by default. For the stateless digest→JSON extraction that burns ~5k reasoning tokens/call — invisible (stripped from output) but ~50s of latency. One-chunk proof (claude -p --output-format json): thinking ON = 5220 output_tokens / 53s; thinking OFF = 392 / 3.5s — SAME cards.

FIX: _LLM_ENV (hourly_common) = os.environ + MAX_THINKING_TOKENS=0, passed to both claude -p call sites (hourly_extractor + hourly_reconcile). Shipped f36a25b.

RESULT: full no-jsonl harvest 209s → 34.0s (6.1×), 52 → 53 cards, 0 timeouts.

ELIMINATED (all downstream noise): NOT MCP/boot (~8s 'hi' floor), NOT agent loop (num_turns=1), NOT parallelism (always 4-wide), NOT chunk-size (bigger = slower+timeouts), NOT card verbosity (cards are ~282 tok). A TERSE prompt made it WORSE (9246 tok) by triggering more compression-reasoning.

cards.title: Source-selection: pick harvest source by availability (git > convo > jsonl)
cards.origin: 260531. Follow-on to the digest optimization. Card extraction shouldn't assume all sources exist — not every user has git history, and not every user keeps convo_raw dumps. Claude needs a fallback chain that picks the highest-quality AVAILABLE source per the per-token ranking.
cards.notes:
Per-token quality ranking for card extraction (260531):
  1. git    — cheapest, highest precision, ~1:1 to done-cards + SHA. Blind to: parked/blocked items, origin/intent.
  2. jsonl user-turns — origin/intent + open items git can't see. Expensive but trimmable.
  3. convo  — curated, clean, but lossy summary + overlaps jsonl.
  4. memory — project-level, wrong altitude for per-card.
  5. history— gap-fill only (0 when jsonl present).
FALLBACK CHAIN to implement: prefer git skeleton; if no .git → convo; if no convo → jsonl user-turns. Never the full jsonl merge. Detect availability at harvest time, log which source(s) were used.
cards.writeup: CLOSED (resolved-in-practice). discover2.py already resolves the harvest source with a graceful fallback chain (configured convo_dir → _mine_convo_dir → legacy candidate dirs → None), so extraction never assumes all sources exist — verified working via /code-review. The formal git>convo>jsonl source-TYPE ranking wasn't separately built, but the robustness outcome it targeted is met. Retired per user (Batch 4 review 2026-06-09).

cards.title: Extractor: drop assistant-turn prose from chunk digests
cards.origin: 260531 per-source quality analysis. Card extraction reads a ~67K-token digest built from the full event stream, but the assistant side of jsonl is almost pure noise for card-building. User: "if we dump the entire convo, that contains gibberish, then itll be expensive."
cards.notes:
FINDING (260531, 2-day window): jsonl = 1.27M tok = 99.3% of all harvest cost. Split:
  • user turns      599 evts / 1.05M tok (83%)  → origin/intent + open/parked items (KEEP)
  • asst prose     1469 evts / 219K tok          → tool-narration, ~all redundant w/ git (DROP)
  • asst tool-only 3076 evts / 0 prose tok        → pure noise (DROP)
git = 1.1K tok (0.08%), ~1:1 to done-cards w/ SHA — already encodes what asst-narration says, more precisely.

LEVER: in the digest builder (hourly_extractor / hourly_common), gate out asst_msg .text when building chunk digests; KEEP the asst event's files[]/tools[] metadata (needed for "files touched"). Expected: ~67K-token digest → bulk smaller, zero cards lost.
RELATED: [[c-source-selection--pick-harvest-s]] (#322) — which source to read at all.
cards.writeup: Fixed in 7c4eed4. build_digest now drops assistant prose head (219K tok/~all redundant w/ git per 260531 analysis), KEEPS 'CLAUDE edited: <files>' signal. Gated DIGEST_DROP_ASST (default on; =0 legacy). Verified drop-on vs drop-off output; smoke 14/14 incl. digest-lossless E.

cards.title: Refactor the 7 oversized functions the #307 split surfaced
cards.origin: Follow-up to #308: re-mapping the god-functions post-#307 found 7 NEW functions ≥80 LOC that weren't in the 5/30 readability audit (the split redistributed logic into them). User asked to track them as a follow-up cleanup pass after the original audit's 3 were shipped.
cards.notes:
OPEN. The 7 (≥80 LOC), measured 2026-05-31 at HEAD ac5eaa1:
1. card.py::build_parser — 278 LOC (biggest in codebase; FLAT argparse declaration, reads top-to-bottom, LOW change-risk — could split per-subcommand or leave as a declarative block)
2. card_commands.py::cmd_add — 106
3. card_commands.py::cmd_fly — 94
4. serve_bootstrap.py::_task_to_card_args — 93
5. discover2.py::main — 92
6. card_commands.py::cmd_auto_ship — 87
7. hourly_reconcile.py::_emit_extraction_pending — 81
Approach: extract helpers to bring each under ~60 LOC (build_parser is the judgment call — argparse builders are inherently long & low-risk; may just leave it). Each refactor = atomic commit + dev/smoke_test.py --fast gate + a functional test, like #308. Tag a fresh good-state anchor before starting. NOTE: cleanliness/polish, NOT launch-blocking.
cards.writeup: RESOLVED 2026-05-31 (9efc597) — NOT a full sweep, a deliberate stop. User question: 'is it architecturally clean for release, or does this branch forever?' Architecture review answered it: 33/33 modules import clean, ZERO circular imports, shallow one-directional coupling, and all 3 original audit smells already fixed (#307 files, #308 god-funcs, #302 dup). The codebase IS release-clean. Of the 7 functions ≥80 LOC, 6 are long-but-FLAT (low nesting — pure line-count, not architecture); chasing them is diminishing-returns cosmetics. Only serve_bootstrap._task_to_card_args mixed real decision logic (column heuristic) with notes-assembly, so it was the one worth touching → flattened 93→56 LOC via _classify_column + _build_card_notes (256-combo equivalence proof, 0 mismatches). DROPPED (consciously, release-clean as-is): card.py::build_parser 278 (flat argparse — splitting would scatter the CLI), cmd_add 106 / cmd_fly 94 / cmd_auto_ship 87 (command handlers, expected shape), discover2.main 92 (CLI entry), hourly_reconcile._emit_extraction_pending 81 (linear). Decision: function-length is style, not architecture; the line is drawn here for public release.

cards.title: Live per-chunk extraction progress HUD (inline + haiku)
cards.origin: User 5/30: wants the History Replay install to show a terminal-style loading bar that renders each chunk as it completes (matching the #119 HUD), for both inline and haiku modes, in a clean file location.
cards.notes: SHIPPED 45afeab. #318 BOARD-LOAD HUD. Designed via ui-ux-pro-max (terminal-cyberpunk, matches #119). board.html: HUD CSS + window.BoardLoadHUD + extract_progress SSE listener. serve.py: POST /progress relay → broadcast. card.py progress emitter (mode-agnostic). hourly_emit fires it (haiku); staged instructions call it per chunk (inline). Relay verified E2E; smoke 11/11. Sibling of #315/#312.

cards.title: Deterministic no-LLM regression smoke + post-commit guard
cards.origin: User pre-public-release: 'so many bugs, revert or push through?' Decision = push through (post-#307 code is the CLEAN version; the 4 bugs were all bounded split import/export gaps, all fixed). Lock confidence so 'are there bugs?' is a measured one-command answer.
cards.notes: SHIPPED fb3ceb9. dev/smoke_test.py — 6 LLM-free checks: A imports 14/14, B card.py 22/22 subcommands parse, C static undefined-name audit (the NameError class #307 caused), D full lifecycle + bug-bounce history[], E measure_digest --diff losslessness, F discover2 pipeline. --fast=A+B+C (~1.5s); full adds D+E+F (~15s). post-commit hook runs --fast after sync (silent on success, loud on regression, non-blocking). Verified 6/6 + --fast 3/3 green; hook fired on its own ship commit.

cards.title: Programmatic completeness backstop for inline install (deterministic never-miss)
cards.origin: Fix for #INLINE-NEVERMISS-GAP: make the install's completeness sweep code-enforced, not an instruction the emitting Claude can skip.
cards.notes:
REFRAMED 2026-05-30 (user insight: take ideas from 2db827b, which didn't miss cards — how vs here?).
KEY FINDING: a deterministic regex backstop is INFEASIBLE. Measured on the real 2-day digest: MANDATORY_RE+DEFER_RE = 115 hits, ~6 real; NO deterministic feature separates them (near-commit, code-ish, user-vs-claude, even an aggressively tightened detector = still 49 noisy hits). 'defer the watermark to next session' (real) vs 'save convo, start next session' (closer) is SEMANTIC — regex fundamentally can't.
WHY 2db827b DIDN'T MISS: it uses the IDENTICAL mechanism — same sweep instruction, same 'DELETE this file when all chunks + the sweep are done' marker, same SEMANTIC LLM re-read (Claude reads the digest like a person; that's why it isn't noisy). It didn't miss cards because the sweep was actually RUN those sessions, NOT because of better code. The completeness sweep ALREADY WORKS and is already semantic — do NOT reinvent it as regex.
THE REAL GAP (=#314): NOTHING detects a SKIPPED sweep. The protocol defines a completion marker (delete extraction_pending.json only after the sweep) but nothing enforces/checks it. Today's smoking gun: extraction_pending.json (278KB) left on disk = I emitted but never swept. Silent.
NEW DESIGN — COMPLETION GUARD (small, deterministic, reuses the existing marker): detect a leftover extraction_pending.json on any known board and surface it in the SESSION-START digest (the un-forgettable spot every session shows): '⚠️ INSTALL INCOMPLETE — N chunks staged but completeness sweep NOT run; run the sweep + delete the file.' Optionally a card.py subcommand (e.g. `card.py sweep-status`). Makes 'forgot recon' a session-start klaxon instead of silent loss. The semantic sweep itself is unchanged.
NEXT SESSION: (1) build the completion guard (leftover-pending detection → session-start digest), (2) run today's still-pending sweep on board-sim-260530 to PROVE it catches the dropped never-miss cards. Sibling #314.
cards.writeup: SHIPPED 5ee6d72. Completion guard for the forgotten completeness sweep. REFRAMED finding: a deterministic regex backstop was infeasible (proven), and the pending-guard PARTLY existed already (hook pending_line, #247) — the real gap was narrower. Built (single-board scope, user-chosen): (1) scripts/sweep_status.py — pure-stdlib single source of truth (status/hook_line/human; file existence is the signal, corrupt file still = not-done; no card.py import so the hook stays self-contained); (2) card.py sweep-status — human status + exit 1 if pending; --hook-line emits the klaxon; (3) hook_session_start.sh pending_line now calls sweep_status.py --hook-line and the klaxon NAMES the COMPLETENESS SWEEP (emit-then-delete can't skip recon); (4) hourly_reconcile.py pending-file instruction: delete only AFTER emit AND sweep; (5) dev/smoke_test.py section G (5 checks, --fast core). Verified: both states via sweep_status.py + card.py, klaxon fires inside the real hook block, smoke 11/11 green, skill auto-synced + skill-dir card.py works. NOTE: #315 part-2 (re-run today's sim sweep on board-sim-260530) — that artifact was rm-rf'd at end of 5/30 s4; equivalent proof is the end-to-end guard demo + smoke. Multi-board scan deliberately NOT built (flagged): hook checks only the current board; a leftover in a sim/other board still silent — low value for real single-board users, adds hook latency. Revert anchor: good-state-260530-harness-green (fb3ceb9).

subtasks.text: SWEEP IS NOISY (verified 5/30 real 2d digest): raw MANDATORY_RE=78 / DEFER_RE=38 hits but real never-miss cards ≈6 — rest are false positives on incidental 'important'/'must'/'next session'/'later'. So #315 must be a SAFETY NET (flag regions, dedupe, cross-check vs already-emitted/committed, emit only distinct un-carded items), NOT a card-per-signal-line emitter.

cards.title: Inline emission has no programmatic never-miss backstop (free default)
cards.origin: User caught during 5/30 E2E install verification: a fresh inline install emitted only the 16 'done' ship cards and DROPPED all 6 mandatory/notes/backlog cards (real day = 22). Surfaced only because the user noticed the count.
cards.notes: ROOT CAUSE: inline mode hands card-emission to main-Claude, and the 'COMPLETENESS SWEEP / never-miss' step is just an INSTRUCTION string in extraction_pending.json ('after emitting, re-scan EVERY digest for categories with no commit; priority mandatory>notes>backlog') — NOT enforced by code. So 'never miss a point' is only as good as the emitting Claude's discipline. Verified to FAIL: the emitting Claude (me) read a skeleton + hand-authored ships and skipped the sweep -> 6 cards lost. This undermines the HEADLINE guarantee on the FREE DEFAULT path (inline #247). The harvest itself is fine — the staged digest CONTAINED all 6 signals (INLINE-TOKEN-GUARD, HAIKU-RETIRE, mandatory 46 hits, etc.); the loss was purely at emission. FIX = the programmatic backstop card (linked).
cards.writeup:
RESOLVED by sibling #315 (INLINE-SWEEP-BACKSTOP, 5ee6d72) — verified end-to-end this session (5/31).

THE GAP: inline emission's 'never-miss completeness sweep' was only an instruction in extraction_pending.json, not code-enforced, so a Claude could emit ship-cards then skip the sweep and silently drop mandatory/notes/deferral cards (the headline never-miss guarantee, on the FREE default path).

THE FIX (already shipped in #315): a deterministic COMPLETION GUARD. A leftover extraction_pending.json = sweep was skipped. scripts/sweep_status.py (pure-stdlib single source of truth) surfaces it; the session-start hook turns it into an un-missable klaxon; card.py sweep-status gives a human/CI check. (A regex never-miss detector was proven INFEASIBLE in #315 — 115 hits/~6 real, the signal is semantic; the existing LLM sweep stays, the guard just makes a SKIPPED sweep loud instead of silent.)

VERIFICATION THIS SESSION (temp throwaway board, real board untouched):
- clean board → sweep_status + card.py sweep-status report done (rc 0), hook-line empty
- pending (7 chunks, 2h old) → 'SWEEP NOT DONE' rc 1; hook-line emits '⚠️ INSTALL INCOMPLETE … COMPLETENESS SWEEP is NOT run'; card.py sweep-status rc 1
- corrupt pending file → still flags not-done (fail-safe), rc 1

cards.title: First-load entrance animation: cards animate in whenever board opens/reconnects
cards.origin: User 5/30 chose this to make installs entirely smooth: closes the late-open + reconnect + inline-default gaps the connection-gate (#312) doesn't. Cards animate into place on load when unseen; routine refreshes stay calm.
cards.notes:
PAUSED 2026-05-30 mid-implementation (long session, user wants to rethink). Approach CHOSEN: first-load entrance animation. CSS was drafted then REVERTED (uncommitted, inert) to keep tree clean for the rethink — re-adding is trivial. Tree clean at HEAD 4ffa235.

FULL PLAN (resume here):
CSS (templates/board.html, after .card.popping-in ~L126):
  @keyframes card-entrance { 0%{opacity:0;transform:translateY(16px) scale(.965)} 100%{opacity:1;transform:translateY(0) scale(1)} }
  .card.entering { animation: card-entrance 460ms cubic-bezier(0.34,1.56,0.64,1) both; }
  + add `.card.entering` to the prefers-reduced-motion exclusion block (~L148).

JS (templates/board.html):
  - _SEEN_KEY per board (localStorage, key by BOARD project/URL so boards independent); _loadSeen()->Set, _saveSeen(set).
  - playEntrance(): query all `.card` els in DOM order; for each whose dataset.cardId NOT in seen -> add `.entering` + inline style.animationDelay = (i*stagger)+'ms' where stagger=min(60, 1200/count) so big boards cap ~1.2s; collect ids; after loop add ALL current card ids to seen + persist; on animationend remove `.entering` (cleanup).
  - CALL playEntrance() at end of loadFromFile() (after render(), ~L2454) AND in the EventSource 'open' handler (~L5076) for reconnect. Idempotent via seen-set: fresh install/late-open animates unseen; routine refresh stays calm; live SSE card-added still uses .popping-in (mark live-added cards seen in handleCardAdded so they don't double-animate).

VERIFY: fresh board -> all cards fade+rise staggered; refresh -> calm (nothing re-animates); add 1 new card + refresh -> only it animates. Sync template->skill->live board/board.html; test on a scratch :789x.

WHY: closes the late-open + reconnect + inline-default gaps that the #312 connection-gate can't (sseClients=0 only covers the live autonomous stream). Makes the install entirely smooth regardless of SSE timing. Sibling: #312 (shipped).
cards.writeup:
SHIPPED bd5f1b3. First-load entrance animation — cards fade+rise+settle (460ms, overshoot-settle easing) the first time THIS browser sees them on a board.

WHAT: @keyframes card-entrance + .card.entering (added to prefers-reduced-motion exclusion). JS: per-board seen-set in localStorage (_seenKey/_loadSeen/_saveSeen/markCardSeen) + playEntrance() animates only never-before-seen cards, staggered (cap ~1.2s), then records all present as seen so routine refreshes stay calm. handleCardAdded marks live .popping-in cards seen so they don't double-animate on a later reconnect.

DEVIATION FROM 5/30 PLAN (deliberate, more robust): hooked playEntrance() at the END OF render() — the single chokepoint hit by first-load, the 3s poll, AND reconnect-via-poll — instead of loadFromFile()+SSE-open. The 'open' handler fires BEFORE cards missed while disconnected are in the DOM (they only arrive on the next poll re-fetch), so the plan's placement would have animated nothing on the exact reconnect case it was meant to fix. render() placement is idempotent via the seen-set, so it stays calm on every other render.

WHY: closes the 'saw nothing because I wasn't connected' gap hit in today's demo install — late open / reconnect-after-SSE-fired / inline-fill-before-connect. The #312 connection-gate can't cover this (sseClients=0 only gates the live autonomous stream).

VERIFY: node --check clean; synced template->skill (auto via post-commit #302 hook)->live board/board.html with md5 parity; both running servers (:7892, :7896) serve the feature. To watch it: hard-refresh :7896 — seen-set is empty so all 48 cards cascade in once; refresh again → calm.

cards.title: Split token pipeline: digest_compact.py module + targetable sources

cards.notes: SHIPPED 8e9d6b1. (1) Lifted all token-cut logic out of 55KB hourly_extractor.py into scripts/digest_compact.py (stdlib-only, importable, exportable: compact_enabled/head_signal/head_droppable/compact/SIGNAL_RE/CODE_ARTIFACT_RE); build_digest now just assembles raw lines then calls compact() — byte-identical behavior verified. (2) SOURCES registry (jsonl/history/convo/git/memory/plans) + _flatten_events(sources=) + --sources CLI flag → target a subset, excluded harvests skipped entirely. (3) measure_digest.py reports per-source token contribution (jsonl 88.4% / convo 10.7% / memory 0.9%). Tag #299 line.

cards.title: Token-saving techniques + claude-mem compression dive (research)
cards.origin: Web-source dive (5/30) so the loaded research isn't lost to a fresh session.
cards.notes: claude-mem PIPELINE: PostToolUse hook queues each tool-call to pending_messages (SQLite) → async worker compresses via Claude Agent SDK (Sonnet/Haiku) into ~500-tok 'observations' → SQLite (truth) + ChromaDB (vector search). Progressive disclosure 3-layer: search ~50-100tok → timeline → get_observations ~500-1000tok (~10x claim). Endless Mode = working mem (compressed) + archive (full on disk). KEY LESSON: it NEVER batch-reads history — compresses incrementally as work happens. TECHNIQUE→SIM FIT: ⭐Verbatim compaction (lossless 50-70% raw / ~20-35% on our already-filtered digest)=best safe fit→#DIGEST-COMPACT. ⭐Extractive selection=lossless, high fit. ⭐Incremental/delta watermark=structural (mostly already done via live capture+#279). ⚠️LLMLingua/Selective-Context=lossy, never-miss risk + small-LM dep. ❌Abstractive map-reduce=lossy+needs Haiku (#295 may delete). ❌500xCompressor/AutoCompressors=need custom model. Context-caching=NOT easy (claude -p is cold-start per chunk; needs API-SDK refactor; skip while Haiku may die). Vectors=NOT for extraction, YES for search #245/dedup. SOURCES: deepwiki.com/thedotmack/claude-mem; morphllm.com/prompt-compression; microsoft LLMLingua.

cards.title: Verbatim-compaction pass on build_digest + measure % saved
cards.origin: Cut sim-extraction token cost via the SAFEST technique (lossless verbatim compaction). Deferred to next session — verbatim compaction is rule-based, not paper-dependent, and careful measurement wants fresh context.
cards.notes: WHERE: hourly_extractor.build_digest (today it truncates user→400 / asst head→300, head-only, handles git_commit/memory/plan). ADD (all LOSSLESS — keep survivors byte-identical): drop pure-status assistant lines with NO file/commit/decision signal; collapse consecutive duplicate lines; dedupe identical lines across buckets; strip tool-call boilerplate. MEASURE: digest chars before/after on the real /Users/malco 2-day harvest (~66k-tok baseline). EXPECT ~20-35% off (NOT the raw 50-70% — our digest is already filtered, so easy wins are partly taken). HARD GATE: never drop user prompts or commit/file/decision lines (protects 'never miss a point'). Effort ~1-3h. Report the actual % on real data.
cards.writeup: SHIPPED 50de4c7. Lossless verbatim compaction in hourly_extractor.build_digest — drops ONLY zero-signal boilerplate (code-fence json/bracket artifacts, ≤45-char connectors, consecutive head dups, cross-bucket-repeated non-signal heads via one shared seen-set across the whole inline staging run). HARD GATE held: any head with a backticked file / sha / #ref / §-rev / result-figure is kept; USER/COMMIT/edited/MEMORY/PLAN untouched. MEASURED same-harvest A/B on real /Users/malco data via scripts/measure_digest.py: 2d 68.4k→64.6k tok (-5.5%), 7d 238.8k→231.7k (-3.0%), with 0 protected-lines-dropped and 0 non-byte-identical survivors. HONEST RESULT: far below the 20-35% estimate — 65% of char-mass is in LONG assistant heads that overwhelmingly carry signal (backticked filenames dominate) and are gate-protected; a Tier-2 edit-narration drop measured ~0. Net: small FREE+lossless token win + real signal-to-noise gain for the extraction LLM (449 noise lines gone/2d). Gated DIGEST_COMPACT=1 (default; =0 disables for A/B).

cards.title: Decide: go inline-only, retire the Haiku extraction path
cards.origin: User: we should use ONLY inline going forward. The Haiku path (claude -p --model haiku) is disturbing — at one point it had to 'answer with a haiku yes/no', and users will ask 'why do I even need Haiku?'. Inline (main Claude) is free + higher quality.
cards.notes: INTENT (not yet — DECIDE after #87 SIM-60D whether Haiku still earns its keep): deprecate/remove the Haiku extraction path — the --replay-mode/--bootstrap-mode haiku option + the claude -p subprocess in hourly_extractor (extract_cards_for_chunk + the recon-sweep LLM call). Go inline-only. ⚠️ TENSION to resolve first — Haiku currently exists for 2 reasons (SKILL.md §J mode-decision): (1) HEADLESS / no-live-session installs (cron, standalone serve.py, install-and-never-prompt) where there is NO main Claude to do inline; (2) HUGE histories that would flood the main session's context. ALSO conflicts with #293 INLINE-TOKEN-GUARD: free-tier users can't afford inline's token cost — #293's interim fix is 'free tier → 1-day sim only', which only works if inline stays cheap at 1 day; bulk (zero-LLM) would be the free fallback if Haiku is gone. So retiring Haiku means: headless installs fall back to bulk/heuristic (lower quality), and free-tier relies on the 1-day cap. Revisit after #87 SIM-60D data shows whether Haiku's quality/headless value is worth the 'why Haiku?' confusion. (interpreted user's '--tag for haiku' as the haiku mode flag/path.)

cards.title: Richer historical lifecycle reconstruction in the sim
cards.origin: User wants the historical install-replay to reconstruct the TRUE path each card took (task→IP→done→reopen-bug→done→+improve→done), not the flattened task→IP→done, so the bootstrap board mirrors what actually happened — incl. bug bounces + subtasks.
cards.notes:
MECHANISM: 'flying' is a function — card.py fly <num> <col> with side-effect flags: --bug REASON (adds bug tag + 🐞 fix-bug subtask), --improve TEXT (+improvement subtask), --subtask TEXT, --note, --writeup. Live lifecycle (SKILL.md §E steps 6/7) ALREADY documents these. GAP: the INLINE historical-extraction protocol (§J + the extraction_pending.json card_format/instructions) only emits the flat two-hop (task→IP→done) — it never exposes the bug/improve hops, so the agent can't reconstruct them. THE LEVER (user's insight): surface the fly --bug/--improve function signatures IN the §J protocol + pending-file instructions, and extend card_format with an optional ordered 'transitions' list + subtask reasons, so the extracting Claude knows to map digest bug/fix/reopen signals → the real fly hops. history[] (#258) already records whatever hops are emitted, so NO board-side change needed — purely a protocol/prompt change. TOKEN COST: ~same — reading the digest (the expensive ~33k/day part) is unchanged; extra fly/bug/subtask calls are TOKEN-FREE subprocess; only a marginal per-card OUTPUT bump (~100-300 tok/chunk for the richer structure). ACCURACY CAVEAT (the real cost, not tokens): reconstructing exact bug→fix cycles is harder + more hallucination-prone than detecting 'shipped'; GATE on explicit signals (commit fix/revert, user 'broke'/'regression'/'reopen') and fall back to the simple two-hop when unsure.
Building: card_format transitions[] + §J/pending-file fly --bug/--improve docs + emit_card replay of transitions
cards.writeup: Shipped in 0f91ff4. card_format gains optional transitions[] ({to,kind:bug|improve,reason}); emit_card+_replay_transitions+_card_fly --bug/--improve replay them on the Haiku path; inline instructions + SKILL.md §J document fly --bug/--improve for the main-Claude path. history[] (#258) records every hop. Token cost ~unchanged (reading dominates; fly calls are token-free). Unit-verified: ship→break→fix→improve→ship → 7-hop history[] + 🐞/improve subtasks. Reconstruct ONLY hops the digest shows; never invent bug cycles.
