§J + pending-file instructions: document fly --bug/--improve hops for historical reconstruction
Driver maps each detected transition → fly --bug/--improve; gate on explicit bug/revert/reopen signals, else flat two-hop

cards.title: Inline token-cost estimator + free-tier guard
cards.origin: User note 5/30: inline funnels every chunk digest through main Claude's own context, so on a free/limited tier one install consumes the user's ENTIRE token budget. 1M-context users are fine; free-version users effectively can't use inline at all.
cards.notes:
Inline is serial by nature (one context handles all chunks) — that's the 'why so slow', not a missing parallel flag. GUARD TO BUILD: before inline runs, estimate cost = total_digest_chars/4 (~33k tokens/day measured, x1.4 overhead; 2d=~66k, 7d=~320k). If estimate > budget threshold (free/200k tier), WARN + auto-recommend a cheaper fill. Inline should only DEFAULT on large/1M-context tiers. mandatory — 'free tier can't install' VISION blocker.

▼ STEP 0 FINDINGS 2026-05-31 (metrics-backed; see memory project_haiku_emit_serial_bottleneck):
ROOT CAUSE of haiku slowness = OUTPUT-TOKEN GENERATION. NOT harness/MCP boot, NOT agentic loop, NOT serial emit, NOT the UI, NOT input size — all DISPROVEN. One real chunk via 'claude -p --output-format json --model haiku --strict-mcp-config --disable-slash-commands': num_turns=1, duration_api_ms=103088, output_tokens=11744 (~2.3k tok/CARD), cost $0.063/call. Haiku ~114 tok/s, so ~12k out tok ≈ 100s. Strip flags changed nothing (104s). 90s timeouts = verbose chunks exceeding 90s → split-retry cascade. CARD QUALITY IS GOOD (accurate titles/origins/columns).
DUAL-COST INSIGHT: verbose output is ALSO the USER's cost ($/tokens), not just speed — terser cards = faster AND cheaper for the user. Aligns with the 'don't drain user tokens' goal.
BUILD PLAN (haiku fix is a prereq INSIDE #293):
  step1 — make haiku-mode emit TERSE cards (short origins, minimal/no multi-para writeups; writeups are the token hog) and/or smaller chunks so each call <~6k out tok (<60s, under the timeout); raise extract_cards_for_chunk timeout_s 90→120; THEN workers/parallelism actually pays off.
  step2 — the token-guard: estimate harvest size, pick the cheapest fill that fits the user's context window/budget, warn loudly if big.
CORRECTION to old note: there is NO 'bulk=instant' fallback for --harvest; '--fill discover' (no-LLM heuristic) is NOT wired for harvest. So haiku is currently the ONLY fallback to inline — it MUST be made fast+cheap before the guard can point users at it.
cards.writeup: Shipped 5116947. (a) Inline cost warning in _emit_extraction_pending: estimates digest tokens (chars/4 ×1.4) and warns >BOARD_INLINE_WARN_TOKENS (100k default), recommending narrower window or --fill haiku. (b) Forced thinking=0 on fills (was setdefault) + BOARD_THINKING_TOKENS escape; audited scope = _LLM_ENV copy only, never os.environ/disk, can't leak to main session/other skills/projects. Interim 1-day cap subtask superseded by the live warn (warn-not-block per user). step1/haiku-speed already solved 5/31 (MAX_THINKING_TOKENS=0). Smoke 14/14.

subtasks.text: INTERIM POLICY (user 5/30): FREE tier → 1-DAY simulation only (cap --days 1, ~33k tokens). Bigger windows (haiku/bulk or large-context tiers) only. Holds until the full token-estimator/guard ships.

cards.title: put loading bar when reading chunks
cards.writeup: Already implemented — closing the stale card. 'Loading bar when reading chunks' = the '🔄 extracting X/Y chunks…' live progress banner shipped in 6143a74 (#219 progress banner). _banner_create/_banner_update in scripts/hourly_emit.py create+tick a banner card per chunk as extraction reads them; the visual bar is .card.is-banner .card-progress .bar in board.html. Verified live — it runs in every haiku harvest (visible during the :7896 sims) and is the same banner #353 just fixed for tab-refocus. No new code needed; card was just never moved out of notes.

cards.title: Stale ~/.claude/projects/*/sessions/*.jsonl doc path (real layout is */*.jsonl)
cards.origin: Audit 2026-05-29: VISION.md/SKILL.md/2 scripts referenced a sessions/ subdir that matches 0 files; real layout is projects/*/*.jsonl (581).
cards.notes: SHIPPED 6fccbcb. Fixed 6 doc/comment strings (VISION.md, SKILL.md x3, _hook_find_board.py, health_check.py). Code globs were already correct — pure doc fix.

cards.title: hook_session_start.sh shipped non-executable → SessionStart digest hook silently dead on fresh install
cards.origin: Install-runs audit 2026-05-29: settings.json calls the hook by bare path, but hook_session_start.sh was -rw-r--r-- in the repo (only pre_tool_use had +x).
cards.notes: SHIPPED 6473a52. Two-part: (1) chmod +x all hook_*.sh in repo; (2) install_hooks.py apply_selection() chmods the script +x at install time if the bit is missing (survives git/rsync/zip stripping it); --dry-run only reports 'would chmod', never mutates. VERIFIED non-destructively: installer re-chmods a stripped COPY; dry-run leaves it untouched; real repo files unaffected.

cards.title: Investigate: ~/.claude/tasks + ~/.claude/sessions — is sub-agent/Task work logged outside projects/*.jsonl and thus never harvested?
cards.origin: Audit 2026-05-29 (fresh-install E2E): two transcript-ish dirs (~/.claude/tasks, ~/.claude/sessions) exist separately from projects/; unknown whether they carry uncaptured work.
cards.notes: WHY IT MATTERS: harvest_jsonl only walks ~/.claude/projects/*/*.jsonl. If sub-agent (Agent tool) runs or background Tasks write their turns to ~/.claude/tasks/ or ~/.claude/sessions/ instead of (or in addition to) the project JSONL, that work is invisible to every harvest path. Before claiming 'we capture everything end-to-end' this needs confirming. INVESTIGATION STEPS: (1) inspect the structure + contents of ~/.claude/tasks and ~/.claude/sessions; (2) determine if any turns/tool_use there are absent from projects/*.jsonl; (3) if so, add a harvester (or confirm projects/*.jsonl already mirrors them and close as no-op). Likely minor but unverified. NOTE: also a stale-doc fix shipped separately — VISION.md/discover2 docstring said projects/*/sessions/*.jsonl (0 matches); real layout is projects/*/*.jsonl (581).
cards.writeup: Investigated — ~/.claude/tasks and ~/.claude/sessions both exist but contain 0 *.jsonl files; no sub-agent/Task transcripts written there, so harvest_jsonl walking ~/.claude/projects/*/*.jsonl misses nothing. (Task/Agent output goes to a per-run tmp tasks dir.) No harvest gap. Optional future guard if Claude Code changes where it logs.

cards.title: Silent truncation: --harvest-days 2 / --max-tasks 40 / recent-first drop older work with no log line
cards.origin: Audit 2026-05-29 (fresh-install E2E): heavy/long-history users silently lose everything older than 2 days or beyond 40 tasks — VISION §4 forbids silent caps.
cards.notes: WHY IT HAPPENED: install.sh defaults HARVEST_DAYS=2 (L44); discover2 --max-tasks 40 + sorts recent-first then truncates (main L775-776). A user with months of history gets at most ~2 days, capped 40, with NO message about what was dropped. VISION §4 (Minimal friction / 'No silent caps') explicitly: if a workflow bounds coverage (top-N, sampling), log() what was dropped — silent truncation reads as 'covered everything' when it didn't. Right now it reads as complete but isn't. HOW TO RESOLVE: (1) raise/justify the default window (or make it adaptive to history volume), (2) when tasks are truncated, emit a visible line ('harvested 40 of 137 tasks; older history not shown — re-run with --days N') both to stderr and as a board note/banner. Don't change the cap silently — surface it. TEST: harvest a project with >40 candidate tasks → confirm a 'dropped N' line appears.
cards.writeup: SHIPPED afe6227. discover2.main() now emits a stderr warning when tasks are dropped by --max-tasks and adds tasksFound/tasksDropped to JSON output. Default window left unchanged (compute-light per keep-webapp-lightweight) — only the SILENCE is fixed, per VISION §4. Inline path processes all chunks (no task-drop cap). VERIFIED: --max-tasks 5 on 503-task window → 'harvested 5 of 503; 498 not shown'.

cards.title: Convo harvest reads the deprecated _raw_ summaries and ignores _verbatim_ (now PRIMARY per CLAUDE.md)
cards.origin: Audit 2026-05-29 (fresh-install E2E): harvest globs conversation_raw_*.md (summaries) but CLAUDE.md now declares conversation_verbatim_*.md the PRIMARY raw dump.
cards.notes: WHY IT HAPPENED: harvest_convo (discover2.py L234) globs ONLY conversation_raw_*.md and parses [USER]/[CLAUDE] HH:MM markers — the SUMMARY format. As of session 4 today, CLAUDE.md was rewritten: render_session_raw.py --append-daily → conversation_verbatim_YYMMDD.md is now PRIMARY (truly verbatim, feeds the #251 training pipeline) and the _raw_ summaries are demoted to optional human carry-forward. So the harvest reads the LOSSY file and ignores the AUTHORITATIVE one — a format-priority inversion distinct from #281's path-portability issue. The verbatim format is also different (no [USER] HH:MM markers; it's render_session_raw.py's layout) so a new parser is needed, not just a glob change. HOW TO RESOLVE: parse conversation_verbatim_*.md (match render_session_raw.py's actual markers) and prefer it; fall back to _raw_ only when verbatim is absent; dedupe so a day present in both isn't double-counted. TEST: a day with only a _verbatim_ dump (no _raw_) should still yield convo events. RELATED: #281.
cards.writeup: RESOLVED (decision A, f2d725a). NOT switching convo harvest to verbatim: conversation_verbatim_*.md is the *.jsonl re-rendered, and we already harvest raw *.jsonl directly → reading verbatim would double-count. Keep reading _raw_ summaries (curated structure jsonl lacks); _flatten_events dedupe drops overlaps. Documented in harvest_convo docstring so it isn't re-filed as a bug.

cards.title: Memory harvested as filename+mtime only — the synthesized content inside each memory/*.md is never read
cards.origin: Audit 2026-05-29 (fresh-install E2E): harvest_memory emits only the filename as event text; the richest context on the machine (the rules inside memory files) never reaches the digest.
cards.notes: WHY IT HAPPENED: harvest_memory (discover2.py L191-201) sets text=p.name and uses mtime purely as a timestamp signal — 'a memory was written at T', not WHAT it says. build_digest (hourly_extractor L111) likewise only echoes the memory filename. So even after #280 de-hardcodes the path, the harvest learns THAT a memory exists, not its content. The memory/*.md files are the densest synthesized project state on the box (~70+ files) and they are effectively content-blind to the extractor. HOW TO RESOLVE: read the body of each memory file (at least the frontmatter description: line + first paragraph) into the event text so the LLM/extractor can use it as real context for card titles/origins — capped to keep token cost sane (e.g. description + 500 chars). Respect the #280 portability fix (glob all memory dirs). TEST: a memory file describing a shipped feature should let the extractor produce a content-aware card, not just 'memory written'. RELATED: #280, #282.
cards.writeup: SHIPPED 6a031a3. _memory_summary() reads frontmatter description: + first body paragraph (capped 500) instead of just p.name, so the synthesized rules inside each memory file reach the extractor/digest. Flows through _attach_context→memory_writes→task_to_record. VERIFIED: sample events carry real content.

cards.title: Fresh-repo install = empty board — the VISION 'see your last week' promise silently fails for new adopters
cards.origin: Audit 2026-05-29 (fresh-install E2E): default ./install.sh in a new project yields an empty board despite the user having hundreds of Claude sessions in other projects.
cards.notes: WHY IT HAPPENED: Default ./install.sh (no --harvest) bootstraps the board in the CURRENT project and discover2 filters tasks by task_in_project (main() L772-773, no --all-projects). A user adopting board-steward in a brand-new repo has ZERO history in that repo → empty board. Meanwhile they have 581 sessions of history in OTHER projects (verified on this machine). VISION §'The test' + §3 promise: open the board and ALREADY SEE your last week of work, animated in. That only holds if you install into a project that already has history, or pass --harvest <existing repo>. New adopters get exactly the empty-state VISION says to avoid. This is the #1 first-run UX gap — bigger than the portability bugs. HOW TO RESOLVE: on first bootstrap of an empty/new project, either (a) run a cross-project harvest (--all-projects) scoped to recent history so the board is never empty on day one, or (b) detect empty-project + prompt 'mine your recent Claude history across all projects?' (#209 ask-flow), or (c) at minimum surface a non-empty welcome/onboarding state instead of a blank board. Decide the default policy. TEST: ./install.sh --demo (fresh temp project, no --harvest) → board should NOT be blank for a user with cross-project history.
cards.writeup:
SHIPPED b23d267. Fresh-repo install no longer yields a blank/incoherent board — VISION's 'see your last week of work' promise now holds for new adopters.

ROOT CAUSE (deeper than 'empty'): project-scoping at two seams stripped a fresh repo's signal. discover2's task_in_project → literally 0 tasks. hourly_extractor's _filter_events drops user_prompt events whose cwd≠project → on a fresh repo ALL 219 prompts dropped but 4441 asst_msg KEPT (asst isn't cwd-filtered) = an INCOHERENT board (answers with no questions), not just empty.

FIX = 'never-empty first-run seed' (card option (a), the only zero-user-input option — bootstrap runs in a bg thread so option (b)'s ask-flow can't apply). When a project has no local history, the FIRST fill falls back to the user's recent CROSS-PROJECT history, loudly (VISION §4 no silent caps), bounded by --days. Going-forward stays project-scoped; established boards keep in-project prompts so seed NEVER fires for them (no regression).

SEAMS (isolate, touch-the-minimum):
- hourly_extractor._filter_events: seed_if_empty param; fires only when (had prompts AND kept none in-project) → revert to unfiltered. +run() param +--seed-cross-project-if-empty CLI flag.
- discover2.main: symmetric fallback under the same flag.
- serve_bootstrap._stream_hourly_cards / _stream_discovered_cards: accept+forward the flag (also de-duped a doubled comment + fixed a stale docstring).
- serve.py._start_bootstrap_stream: passes seed_if_empty=True for BOTH modes → install always seeds; standalone CLI defaults OFF (strict scope preserved for power users).

VERIFY (fresh temp project, real board untouched): discover2 strict=0 → seed=40/527 tasks. hourly inline strict=4506 events (incoherent) → seed=4725 (219 prompts restored, coherent), stages 12 chunks. 4 modules import clean (no cycles). smoke_test.py new section H (strict→0 vs seed→non-empty + seed-notice assertion) → ALL GREEN 14/14.

RESIDUAL (orthogonal, NOT silently dropped): _cwd_in_project only scopes user_prompt, not asst_msg/convo_*/memory, so strict-scope still leaks cross-project asst noise into the cron/going-forward path. Pre-existing; out of #285 scope (changing it risks over-dropping cron cards since asst turns may lack reliable cwd). Worth a follow-up card if going-forward noise becomes a problem.

cards.title: Inline harvest path ignores find_convo_dir() — hardcodes convo path harder than #281
cards.origin: Audit 2026-05-29 (fresh-install E2E): the path real users install through (hourly_extractor._flatten_events) never uses the portable convo-dir resolver.
cards.notes: WHY IT HAPPENED: There are TWO harvest entrypoints. discover2.main() (L738) calls find_convo_dir() which searches 3 candidate dirs (~/Desktop/conversation_history, ~/conversation_history, project/conversation_history) + a board/discover.config.json override — reasonably portable. BUT the ACTUAL --harvest / inline default path goes through hourly_extractor._flatten_events (L652) which calls bare harvest_convo(since) with NO convo_dir → falls back to the hardcoded ~/Desktop/conversation_history. So the standalone tool is portable-ish; the path a real user actually installs through is NOT. #281 only fixes harvest_convo's default; this is the calling-site gap. HOW TO RESOLVE: in _flatten_events, resolve the dir via find_convo_dir(project) (and the same ask-flow), pass it into harvest_convo. Keep #281's glob fix (raw_ AND verbatim_) so both layers agree. TEST: run hourly_extractor --harvest from a project whose convo dir is ~/conversation_history (not Desktop) and confirm convo events appear. RELATED: #281.
cards.writeup: SHIPPED a5437a0. hourly_extractor._flatten_events (the real inline install path) now resolves convo via find_convo_dir(project) — same resolver discover2.main() uses — instead of bare harvest_convo(since) which hit the hardcoded default. Convo skipped (not hardcoded) when absent, since raw *.jsonl is harvested directly. VERIFIED: wired path yields 157 convo events (87 user/70 asst) on a 1-day WorkBoard window.

cards.title: also, can i do this skill for codex / cursor or other ai tools? ALSO, what if ppl do it on claude desktop or VS code or smth, can we find their convo history etc.? (ANSWERED, deferred)
cards.notes:
DEFERRED (scoping decision 2026-05-29). Splits into 3: (1) OTHER AI TOOLS (Codex/Cursor/Gemini) = out of VISION scope ('#1 kanban for CLAUDE'); per-tool adapter layer, open-ended maintenance — defer indefinitely. (2) Claude Code in VS Code/JetBrains = SAME engine, writes same ~/.claude/projects/*/*.jsonl → likely already harvested; verify under #289 (no new code). (3) Claude Desktop app / claude.ai web = separate local store / server-side, genuinely hard, separate effort. NOT mixed into the #279-289 harvest-portability sprint (that's portability across USERS; this is across TOOLS/HOSTS — orthogonal + far larger; premature abstraction would make #280/#281/#284 harder).

[260610 difficulty reassessment for Codex/Cursor port] Tool-AGNOSTIC core ports as-is (~60%): serve.py, board.html, card.py, board.json schema, kanban UI. HARD per-tool ~40% each: (a) history harvest — discover2 reads ~/.claude/projects/*.jsonl (Claude Code format); Cursor/Codex/Gemini each have a different transcript store → new adapter per tool; (b) live hooks — Cursor/Codex lack CC's SessionStart/UserPromptSubmit/PreToolUse/Stop hooks, so live-tracking has no host to fire from; (c) skill invocation differs. Verdict: not 'hard' code-wise but OPEN-ENDED maintenance (N tools x adapters) + off-VISION. STAYS DEFERRED; post-launch do as separate opt-in adapters, not core.
cards.origin:
⏺ Looked at the actual architecture. Here's the honest sizing — and the answer
  is "depends which half of #283 you mean," because the card bundles two
  different asks.
· Gro
  What #283 actually contains

  ▎ "can I do this skill for codex / cursor… ALSO can we find their convo 
  ▎ history?"

  That's two features, and they have very different costs:

  1. Passive history mining — read another tool's logs and retro-fly cards.
  2. Live in-session carding — the SessionStart-opens-board, card-before-edit
  nag, Stop-reconcile experience.

  The baseline that genuinely carries over (so it's minor per tool)

  The tool-agnostic core is everything downstream of the event stream: card.py,
  the board server + board.html, the reconcile/extract pipeline (hourly_*,
  discover2*). None of it cares which AI tool you used. Critically,
  discover2_sources.py already has the right seam — harvest_jsonl() normalizes
  every turn into {ts, source, kind, text, files, meta}. Adding a new tool =
  write one new harvest_*() that emits that same shape. The whole pipeline
  behind it is untouched.

  So passive mining is the minor half, and it varies by tool only in the reader:
  - Codex → near-minor. Codex CLI also writes JSONL session logs
  (~/.codex/sessions/…). A new harvest_codex_jsonl() with a remapped schema is a
  day-scale job, not a rewrite.
  - Cursor → moderate. Cursor stores chat in SQLite (state.vscdb in
  workspace/global storage), not JSONL — so you write a SQLite reader +
  reverse-engineer its schema. Bigger than Codex, but still just feeding the
  same normalized event, so nothing downstream changes.
  - Claude Desktop / VS Code Copilot → harder/undocumented local stores;
  spike-first.

  The part that is not minor

  The live/reactive half is the genuinely tool-specific, larger piece, and it's
  gated entirely by whether the host tool has a hook API. Your whole hooks.json
  (SessionStart / UserPromptSubmit / PreToolUse / Stop / SubagentStop) is Claude
  Code's plugin hook contract:
  - Codex — only a limited notify mechanism, no rich Pre/Post-tool hooks. You'd
  lose live card-before-edit enforcement and fall back to post-hoc extraction.
  Protocol injected via AGENTS.md instead of SKILL.md. 
  - Cursor — effectively no lifecycle hook system. Purely passive extraction;
  protocol injected via .cursorrules. The signature live experience can't be
  replicated.

  Bottom line
  
  - "Can we find their convo history + fly cards?" → minor-to-moderate per tool
  (Codex minor, Cursor moderate). The baseline does most of the work; you're
  writing one source adapter each.
  - "Can we run the skill live like in Claude Code?" → the big change, and
  partly impossible outside Claude Code because Cursor/Codex lack the hook
  surface.

  Two caveats: neither Codex nor Cursor is installed on this machine (only
  Claude Desktop's dir exists), so the external-tool storage formats above are
  from knowledge, not verified here — I'd spike each format before committing
  estimates. If you want, I can scope the Codex JSONL adapter as the first slice
  since it's the cheapest path and reuses the existing pipeline almost
  entirely.

cards.title: Unharvested sources: MEMORY.md index, global history.jsonl, per-project CLAUDE.md
cards.origin: Audit 2026-05-29: _flatten_events reads jsonl/memory/plans/convo/git but skips 3 sources from the Phase-1 map.
cards.notes: WHY IT HAPPENED: _flatten_events was built incrementally and never wired MEMORY.md (index), ~/.claude/history.jsonl (global chronicle), or <proj>/CLAUDE.md (project instructions). LOWEST impact of the four: MEMORY.md just indexes memory/*.md (already harvested → skip); history.jsonl largely duplicates per-project jsonl (likely skip); CLAUDE.md is genuinely useful project context. HOW TO RESOLVE: add a small harvest_claude_md → one 'notes' context card capturing project rules; evaluate history.jsonl for anything jsonl misses (probably nothing); leave MEMORY.md alone. Decide keep/skip per source. (Marked critical per user; in practice the smallest of the 4.)
cards.writeup: SHIPPED 818453c. history.jsonl wired as session-based GAP-FILL: both harvest paths exclude sessionIds already in jsonl, so only rotated/pruned sessions surface; slash/shell noise dropped. Verified: 0 survivors when jsonl intact (470 sessions), 291 clean prompts when all-gap, 0 noise leaked. MEMORY.md (pointer index, content already in #286) + per-project CLAUDE.md (static config, mined for convo-dir in #281) intentionally NOT wired — would add redundancy/noise.

cards.title: harvest_convo hardcodes ~/Desktop/conversation_history + only reads _raw_ (ignores _verbatim_)
cards.origin: Audit 2026-05-29: discover2.py harvest_convo (L228) defaults to one user's personal convo-dump path + convention; other users find nothing, and even here it reads the summaries not the verbatim.
cards.notes: REFINED APPROACH (user, 2026-05-29): do NOT hardcode ~/Desktop/conversation_history, and do NOT ask the user. DERIVE the convo dir from Claude-specific always-present data. Discovery ladder: (1) Mine CLAUDE.md (global ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md + ~/CLAUDE.md + project root CLAUDE.md) for a convo-dump path or render-script invocation — users who keep convo dumps usually DOCUMENT the path there (malco's global CLAUDE.md literally names render_session_raw.py --append-daily → ~/Desktop/conversation_history/conversation_verbatim_YYMMDD.md). (2) Mine the transcripts (~/.claude/projects/*/*.jsonl + ~/.claude/history.jsonl) for Write/Read/Bash file ops whose paths match convo-dump patterns (conversation_*.md, a dir matching *conversation*history*); take the most-frequently-touched dir. (3) Fall back to discover.config.json / skip — NO hardcoded path, NO interactive ask. KEY NUANCE: we ALREADY harvest raw *.jsonl directly (true verbatim), so convo-dump files are ENRICHMENT not essential (they're the user's SUMMARIES of conversations we already have verbatim) — discovery must never block. Also still glob BOTH conversation_raw_*.md AND conversation_verbatim_*.md (see #287 format inversion), and wire the resolver into hourly_extractor._flatten_events too (see #284). Supersedes the earlier 'candidate-list + ask' plan.
cards.writeup: SHIPPED a5437a0. Auto-derive convo dir from Claude's own always-present data (user's idea, replacing hardcode+candidate-list+ask): (1) mine CLAUDE.md global/home/project for a documented dump path/render-cmd, (2) mine ~/.claude/history.jsonl, (3) tally convo paths across 30 newest transcripts. Each hit validated by globbing for conversation_{raw,verbatim}_*.md → templated YYMMDD still resolves, junk→None. Cached to discover.config.json. harvest_convo no longer hardcodes (None→[]). VERIFIED: derives ~/Desktop/conversation_history via ~/CLAUDE.md (not hardcode); junk→None. NOTE: verbatim-FORMAT parsing is still #287 (this is dir-discovery).

cards.title: harvest_memory hardcodes '-Users-malco' → every other user's memory is silently never read
cards.origin: Audit 2026-05-29: discover2.py harvest_memory (L185) pins the memory dir to one machine's project slug — a real different user gets nothing from this source.
cards.notes: WHY IT HAPPENED: mem_dir = ~/.claude/projects/-Users-malco/memory was hardcoded during dev when only this machine mattered. Memory dirs are named by the project's cwd path-slug, so '-Users-malco' is specific to this user. For anyone else (or this user on a different project slug) the path doesn't exist → harvest_memory returns [] silently → memory source dropped. This is THE sim-vs-real-user discrepancy: sim==install holds for THIS user only. HOW TO RESOLVE: glob ALL memory dirs — ~/.claude/projects/*/memory/*.md — and dedupe (or derive the slug from the running user's HOME). Don't pin a username. TEST: run harvest from a non-malco project slug and confirm memory events appear.
cards.writeup: SHIPPED 6a031a3. harvest_memory now globs ~/.claude/projects/*/memory/*.md across ALL slugs (the '-Users-malco' pin was this machine's HOME slug, not a username — other users' memory was never read). Path.home() handles the username/OS. VERIFIED: 114 memory events, glob no longer slug-pinned.

cards.title: No Stop-hook session-end reconciliation — the live 'never-miss on sign-off' backstop is missing
cards.origin: Audit 2026-05-29: the live half of the skill has no automated backstop. When the agent signs off, nothing verifies every work-unit got carded or that shipped In-Progress cards moved to Done.
cards.notes: WHY IT HAPPENED: All reconciliation/completeness machinery was built for the EXTRACTION path only — #220 recon sweep, #224 inline recon, §J completeness sweep all run during install/bootstrap. The LIVE per-session path was only specced as advisory prose (SKILL.md §F) + discussed (move A Stop-hook) but never built. install_hooks.py HOOK_VARIANTS has session-start/user-prompt-submit/pre-tool-use — NO 'stop'. So live, it relies entirely on Claude's discipline; a session that ships work without carding has no safety net. HOW TO RESOLVE: add scripts/hook_stop.sh + HOOK_VARIANTS['stop']=('Stop','hook_stop.sh'). On Stop: harvest THIS session's turns since last card activity + read board non-done cards; reuse §J completeness logic (mandatory>notes>backlog) + reconcile_sweep stale-move logic. Under CLAUDECODE write recon_pending.json for next turn (free, like #224), else emit a digest line the next SessionStart surfaces. TEST: a session that ships without carding → Stop hook flags the uncarded units + any stale In-Progress.
cards.writeup: SHIPPED 6d8f049 (+ synced to deployed skill dir). Live session-end backstop: hook_stop.sh (non-blocking, captures stdin before backgrounding, 5s timeout) + _hook_stop_recon.py (self-contained, reads ONLY the session's own transcript via the Stop payload → fast). Detects (1) UNCARDED WORK (ship signals or >=3 edits but no card.py this session) + (2) OPEN IN-PROGRESS at sign-off; writes recon_pending.json (source=stop_recon) only on a gap; loop-guarded on stop_hook_active. SessionStart now surfaces recon_pending.json (🔁). install_hooks.py: new 'stop' variant; --hook all = session-start+pre-tool-use+stop. UNIT-VERIFIED (synthetic uncarded-work→flagged; real transcript→2 open IP flagged; loop-guard no-ops). REMAINING (user-gated, like #102): install into live settings.json (install_hooks.py --hook all) + CC restart to confirm it fires live. Fresh users get it automatically via install.sh.

cards.title: Live going-forward backstop: (A) Stop-hook session-end reconciliation + (B) de-hardcode harvest paths
cards.origin: User (2026-05-29), end of session: audit found the live half is thinner than the sim. Build next session.
cards.notes: SUPERSEDED 2026-05-29 — split into 4 granular critical cards: STOP-RECON-HOOK, HARVEST-MEMORY-PORTABLE, HARVEST-CONVO-PORTABLE, HARVEST-SOURCES-GAP. See those for detailed why+fix.
cards.writeup: Superseded — split 2026-05-29 into 4 granular cards, ALL now Done: STOP-RECON-HOOK #279, HARVEST-MEMORY-PORTABLE #280/#286, HARVEST-CONVO-PORTABLE #281/#287, HARVEST-SOURCES-GAP #282. Umbrella work complete.

cards.title: I feel the haikum even on 60 days might think multi workers is better... but i even 1day, i feel the inline is alr so much faster... i efel haiku is really not necessary, but we will keep for now. but we will run 60 days simulation on haiku vs inline and see difference.

cards.title: Enrich extraction prompt (origin + open/deferred flagging + manual-quality notes) + two-tier recent-first startup fill (last 1d fast, older backfills in background)
cards.origin: User (2026-05-29): wanted sim cards closer to the manual :7892 quality (rich 'Origin · why this exists' + Notes) AND a UX where install fills the last 1 day FAST so users can start working, then older history backfills in the background as they work. cards.writeup: Shipped in 9819169, verified live 2026-05-29. Enriched _LLM_PROMPT: added 'origin' field (user's WHY, distinct from notes' WHAT/HOW), richer notes (problem→approach→outcome w/ concrete file/commit names), and explicit OPEN/DEFERRED surfacing (deferred work → backlog/task with '⏸ OPEN — <remains + resume trigger>'). Added --end-days-ago (tier boundary) + --recent-first (newest buckets emit first; board still sorts chronologically). serve.py bootstrap now two-tier in the daemon thread: tier-1 last 1d (fast, user can start working) → tier-2 older backfill in background; writes serialize through server lock so concurrent editing can't corrupt. simulate_install.sh passes --recent-first. LIVE PROOF: 1d WorkBoard run produced card with origin='User wanted card extraction to match manual quality...' + notes naming hourly_extractor.py + params — manual-quality, no extra Haiku calls (same chunk count).

cards.title: render_session_raw.py renders wrong session when run from a subdir
cards.origin: Discovered 260529 session-end save: newest_session_for_cwd() maps cwd→project dir. When the renderer is invoked from a subdir (e.g. ~/Desktop/WorkBoard/scripts via a cd-compound), it resolves the WRONG project folder and silently renders a stale session (147f4ea3, 7KB) instead of the live one (e25ba903). It also mis-dated the output to 260528. Per CLAUDE.md the renderer runs at every session end → silent training-data corruption (#251 pipeline) with no error. Fix: detect the actual running session robustly (CLAUDE session id env if exposed, or scan ALL project dirs by mtime, or anchor on the primary working dir not cwd). Workaround used: pass explicit JSONL path.
cards.notes: HIT LIVE 2026-05-29: ran the CLAUDE.md command `render_session_raw.py --append-daily`; shell cwd had persisted to ~/Desktop/WorkBoard, so newest_session_for_cwd() resolved project=-Users-malco-Desktop-WorkBoard and rendered a STALE 5/28 session (147f4ea3) into conversation_verbatim_260528.md — NOT the live session. Fix by rendering the explicit jsonl path. ROOT: cwd-scoped project resolution breaks when cwd != the session's actual launch dir. Proposed fix: pick newest jsonl across ALL project dirs (the actually-live session), or accept a session-id, rather than cwd-scoping.
cards.writeup:
Shipped in 43a2204.

  43a2204  #259 RAW-DUMP-CWD: pick live session by global mtime, not $PWD project

Root cause: cwd-scoped project resolution. Fix: newest_live_session() = globally newest-mtime jsonl across all projects (the actively-written live session), cwd-independent. Verified the fix picks the live session 92259259 from both the bad cwd (~/Desktop/WorkBoard) and /tmp, and that the canonical CLAUDE.md command now writes to today's verbatim file. Protects the convo-save flow + the #251 training pipeline from silently capturing the wrong session.

Structured training-label timeline in board.json, complements JSONL extractor #251. Lifecycle-tested: creation + moves + bug-cycle all timestamped. Going-forward only (no backfill of past moves).

cards.title: Stop hook: auto-run verbatim dump every turn
cards.notes:
Optional automation for #252: extend install_hooks.py with a 'stop' variant running render_session_raw.py --append-daily on Stop. Idempotent so daily verbatim file always complete, zero manual action. User chose MANUAL for now (260529) — build only if the manual end-of-session dump starts slipping. Depends on #252 (done).
Recon → backlog: user chose MANUAL for stop-hook verbatim dump (260529); no follow-up signal to implement it
cards.writeup:
RESOLVED BY DECISION — NOT BUILT (2026-06-10). #253 proposed a Stop-hook variant in install_hooks.py to auto-run render_session_raw.py --append-daily every turn, automating the verbatim conversation dump.

Resolution: decided NOT to build it, and to keep the verbatim dump DEV-ONLY (not shipped in the board-steward plugin). Rationale settled this session: (1) the dump is orthogonal to the board's value prop; (2) silently writing full verbatim transcripts to a user's disk is a privacy/trust cost for a published tool; (3) it serves the dev's own transition-training pipeline (#251/#252), not end users; (4) Claude Code already keeps the raw JSONL, so it is redundant. Confirmed render_session_raw.py lives in dev/ and is NOT wired into any hook/install.sh/SKILL.md, and costs ZERO model tokens (pure stdlib) even when run.

The dev continues to run the dump MANUALLY at end-of-session per /Users/malco/CLAUDE.md (the original 260529 'manual for now' choice stands). The automation is only worth revisiting if that manual dump starts slipping — no current signal.

NOTE: distinct from this session's Stop-hook work (#590 KEY_FEATURES doc, #591 false-positive A/D fix, #592 advisory-default) — that was the CARDING sign-off backstop (_hook_stop_recon.py), a different hook entirely. #253 was about the verbatim-dump hook and remains unbuilt by choice.

cards.title: Inline hourly extractor when CLAUDECODE=1 (free for skill users)
cards.origin:
User 2026-05-28 mid-session-5: 'i really want this: --replay-mode hourly --bucket-min 30 --chunk-size 2 but on users claude can? cos if they have to use haiku, its a skill.. they shouldnt pay for it. it doesnt make any sense if they are paying for it, isnt it? so they should run locally isnt it?'

Core product principle: a skill should be free at point of use. Real-user installs must NOT spend the user's Anthropic credits.

EXTENDS #224 INLINE-RECON pattern from recon-only to the big-spender path (extraction).

CURRENT STATE (asymmetric):
- hourly_extractor.py extract path: subprocesses `claude -p --model haiku` per bucket
  → user pays Haiku $. ~$0.20 for a 60d/200-card backfill.
- hourly_extractor.py reconcile_sweep: already detects CLAUDECODE=1 → writes
  recon_pending.json → main Claude handles for free.

TARGET FLOW (CLAUDECODE=1):
1. simulate_install.sh --replay-mode hourly builds bucket digests as today.
2. Instead of subprocess.run(['claude','-p','--model','haiku',...]), write
   extraction_pending.json with all N bucket digests + the extraction prompt
   + thresholds.
3. Script returns with banner card '📋 N buckets waiting for main-Claude extraction'.
4. Main Claude reads file, processes buckets (chunk-of-K to bound context),
   emits card.py add per card → user watches board fill via SSE.
5. Fallback to Haiku subprocess only if CLAUDECODE unset (CI, sim_2d.py,
   sim_60d.py, non-Claude envs).

TRADEOFFS:
- ✅ Free for skill users (the point of a skill)
- ✅ Quality = whatever main Claude model the user runs (Opus/Sonnet often > Haiku)
- ⚠️ Costs main-Claude context (~few KB per bucket; 60 buckets bounded but real
  — mitigate via chunk-of-K, same shape as #217 --chunk-size 2)
- ⚠️ Serial vs Haiku parallel-4 — slower but install is one-shot

SCOPE: ~1-2h. Mostly reusing #224 machinery (recon_pending.json read/delete
pattern) and #217 chunk-size logic.

DEFERRED 2026-05-28 per user — revisit next session before any public install
flow ships. sim_2d.py keeps using --no-llm (heuristic) in the meantime since
it's a CI gate, not a real install.

cards.title: BOARD-CAL-STATE-PRESERVE — Esc-stack capture phase + scroll preserve

cards.notes: Two bugs: (a) _onCalDayModalKey on bubble phase fired AFTER init's Esc handler had nulled _openCardId — so closed day modal too. Fix: capture-phase listener. (b) renderCalendar wipes innerHTML on every render(), including the one openModal triggers — scroll position lost. Fix: capture .cal-weeks scrollTop before clear, restore after rebuild; reset only on intentional month nav (‹ Today ›).
cards.writeup: Shipped 2026-05-28 in f5a193e. Two fixes: (a) document.addEventListener('keydown', _onCalDayModalKey, true) — capture phase. removeEventListener mirrored. _onCalDayModalKey checks _openCardId FIRST; if set, bail. Init's bubble-phase handler then closes card modal. Day modal survives. (b) Module _calScrollY = 0. _captureCalScroll reads .cal-weeks.scrollTop pre-wipe. _restoreCalScroll restores post-append. renderCalendar wraps innerHTML='' with these calls. ‹ Today › nav buttons explicitly _calScrollY=0 to reset on intentional month jump. Filter chips + SSE re-renders keep scroll. Files: templates/board.html. Synced to skill dir.

cards.title: BOARD-CAL-FLICKER-2 — overflow-x hidden + 4px dead zone for +N pill
cards.origin: User: '+N still flickers, i think its cos of the scroll right or left one... remove that?' Horizontal scrollbar can flash on scroll, and the 1px hidden-chip threshold was oscillating at sub-pixel layout edges.
cards.notes: Two fixes: (a) overflow-x:hidden on .cal-events to suppress horizontal scrollbar; (b) recompute tolerance 1→4px so chips right at the edge don't flip hidden/visible.
cards.writeup: Shipped 2026-05-28 in 43925c5. .cal-events gains overflow-x:hidden so a sub-pixel horizontal overflow can't flash a scrollbar mid-scroll. recompute threshold raised from listBottom+1 to listBottom+4 — gives 4px hysteresis on the hidden-chip edge so a chip lying right at the scroll boundary doesn't oscillate. Files: templates/board.html. Synced to skill dir.

cards.title: BOARD-CAL-MORE-FLICKER — sticky +N more was thrashing on display toggle
cards.origin: User: 'the +13 more keeps flickering... idk why'. Feedback loop: hide pill → scrollHeight shrinks → scrollTop clamps → re-fires scroll → re-shows → repeat.
cards.notes: Two fixes: (a) visibility:hidden instead of display:none so the slot stays reserved + scrollHeight stable; (b) rAF-throttle scroll listener so at most one recompute per frame.
cards.writeup: Shipped 2026-05-28 in 16ee5fb. Two fixes: more.style.display='none'→more.style.visibility='hidden' (and the show branch uses ''; not '' on display) so the pill's slot stays reserved in flow — no scrollHeight oscillation. Scroll listener now schedules recompute via rAF (let rafId=0; if !rafId then requestAnimationFrame). Initial textContent set at construction so first paint is correct. Files: templates/board.html. Synced to skill dir.

cards.title: HOURLY-RETRY-TIMEOUT — bump 90s → 180s for heavy buckets
cards.origin: Deferred 5/28 S3: one chunk (08:30 UTC) still lost at recursion-depth-0 after tier1+tier2 retry, 100+ events in single bucket. Need larger LLM timeout.
cards.notes: scripts/hourly_extractor.py: TIMEOUT_S 90 → 180 OR per-retry-tier escalation (90 → 135 → 180). Smoke on 2026-05-27 --date which historically loses that chunk.
cards.writeup: Superseded by recursive sub-bucketing (_extract_chunk_with_retries Tier 2, hourly_extractor.py:613-637) — no timeout bump needed. Isolated smoke on the card's own recipe (--date 2026-05-27, the day that historically lost the heavy chunk): 18 buckets/2222 events; the 05:00 UTC bucket (180 events) FAILED at 60min, auto-halved 60→30min (depth=1), the 30min sub-bucket still failed, halved 30→15min (depth=2), then recovered 8 cards. 4 recursive-split events fired; heavy bucket no longer lost. Halving is strictly more robust than a fixed 180s ceiling. Live board untouched during test.

cards.title: INLINE-RECON: skip Haiku when running inside main Claude session (CLAUDECODE=1)
cards.origin: User 5/28: 'if u read the whole convo, why parse to haiku?' — main Claude has session context loaded, recon for free
cards.notes: When env CLAUDECODE=1, reconcile_sweep skips the claude -p haiku subprocess. Instead writes <board-dir>/recon_pending.json with {candidates, activity_digest, written_at}. Main Claude reads it next turn, applies moves via card.py move/fly, deletes the file when done. Autonomous runs (cron, no CLAUDECODE) still use Haiku as before.
cards.writeup: Shipped. CLAUDECODE=1 detection bypasses Haiku → writes recon_pending.json → main Claude actions next turn using session context + activity digest. E2E verified: 1 candidate PAN-2D → caught as already-shipped via 5/27 commit SHAs → flew to done.

cards.title: RECURSIVE-RETRY: keep halving bucket-min until LLM call succeeds
cards.origin: User 5/28: 'retry goes from chunk=2 → chunk=1 → half, meaning 30mins → 15mins. then half timing again until it passes'
cards.notes: Currently retry splits chunk-size 2 into two chunk-size 1 calls. If a single-bucket chunk STILL fails (saw 08:30 UTC fail), we lose it. Recursion: on chunk-size 1 failure, re-bucket events at half the bucket-min (30 → 15 → 7), retry each smaller bucket. Cap depth 3. 60s LLM timeout works on small digests.
cards.writeup: Shipped: tier-2 recursive retry. On single-bucket chunk failure, re-bucket events at half bucket-min (30 → 15 → 7), retry each sub-bucket. Cap depth 3. Catches heavy-hour timeouts the old retry couldn't recover.

cards.title: PROGRESS-BAR-UI: visual loading bar replacing extracting N/M text

cards.title: SNAPSHOT: save extraction outcome + --snapshot-load to test recon offline

Add --snapshot-load PATH flag that skips extraction entirely and runs only the reconciliation sweep against the saved board. Lets us iterate on recon LLM prompts without paying ~10min per test.
cards.writeup: Shipped: snapshot save at end of extraction + --snapshot-load PATH skips extraction entirely and runs only recon against saved state. Unblocks fast recon iteration (no more 10min waits per test).

cards.title: RECON-SWEEP: post-extraction reconciliation pass (SKILL.md §F automation)

cards.notes: After hourly_extractor finishes, scan non-done columns for stale/abandoned cards. One LLM call per column (task / backlog / inprogress) with cards + activity context, ask: 'which cards should move where, and why?'. Apply via card.py move. Catches the failure mode where user said 'nvm' or 'skip that' but the card never updated. Implements SKILL.md §F (reconciliation check) — currently a doc-only rule.
cards.writeup: Shipped: post-extraction LLM recon sweep + JSON-array extractor (handles model appending commentary after the array). Verified: 5/27 19 non-done candidates → 1 move (#24 PRELAUNCH-PLAN → done) with the LLM citing the actual user phrase 'stop for the day' as the abandonment signal. Conservative-by-design: stays when uncertain.

cards.title: PROGRESS-BANNER + BUCKET-TS: live X/Y card + real timestamps on extracted cards

cards.notes: Two pieces: (a) write a notes-col banner card at extraction start, update its title/notes per-chunk-completion (N/M done), fly to done at end. (b) Add card.py --created-at ISO to override server-side createdAt; hourly_extractor passes bucket-start ISO so each card lands with its real work time as createdAt. Board's natural sort = chronological without an end-pass.

(b) hourly_extractor.py spawns a Notes-col '🔄 extracting N/M chunks…' banner that updates per chunk, AND stamps each emitted card's createdAt with the first bucket's start time so the board sorts chronologically without an end-pass. Verified on the 5/27 sim run on :7896.

cards.title: DATE-FILTER + RETRY: --date pin + chunk-failure recovery
cards.origin: User 5/28: 'try chunk=2 for ONLY yesterday' (--days N includes today's morning; need a date pin)
cards.notes: Two small extractor improvements: (1) --date YYYY-MM-DD filters events to a single UTC day (so 'only yesterday' works); (2) on chunk failure (timeout / non-JSON), split chunk in half and retry the halves — recovers data lost to LLM timeouts. Bundled per user 5/28.
cards.writeup: Shipped: --date YYYY-MM-DD + chunk-failure retry (split-in-half). Smoke on 5/27: 73 cards, 4 timeouts, 3 recovered. 1 still lost — needs retry timeout bump.

cards.title: CHUNK-BATCH: --chunk-size N for multi-bucket LLM calls (cold-start savings)
cards.origin: User 5/28: 'each thread takes 1 hour (= 2 half-hour buckets), batches to 1 LLM call → 2x speedup'
cards.notes: claude -p cold-start is 5-7s per call. Group N consecutive buckets into ONE LLM call (digest with bucket headers). LLM returns flat card array. Pays cold-start N× fewer times. Default --chunk-size 1 (current behavior); set --chunk-size 2-4 for the speedup.
cards.writeup: Shipped: --chunk-size N. Saves ~10% on chunk=2; bottleneck moved to serial card-emit-with-lifecycle. Next perf win = parallelize emit (or shorter fly pause).

cards.title: HOURLY-PARALLEL: ThreadPoolExecutor + --bucket-min flag
cards.origin: Follow-up to #215. User: 'isnt 1 hour producing right data and laggy... shall we do 5mins?' → led to bucket-min flag + parallelization (4 workers) so 5/15/30/60-min buckets are all viable. 137-card run on 2d/30min/lifecycle used this code.
cards.notes: hourly_extractor.py: --bucket-min N flag + parallel ThreadPoolExecutor (default 4 workers) with chronological-emit-as-ready ordering. simulate_install.sh: --bucket-min passes through. 75 buckets/2d/30min → 137 cards in ~12min (5 LLM timeouts handled gracefully).
cards.writeup: Shipped: parallel + --bucket-min flag. The exact config that produced the 137-card 2d/30min/lifecycle run is now reproducible from HEAD.

cards.title: SIM-CARD-QUALITY: heuristic + LLM title rewrite for sim cards (Hybrid C)
cards.origin: User 5/28: 'shall we revert back to this? lets do C.' (Hybrid: heuristic for short prompts, claude -p rewrite for substantive ones)
cards.notes: Current sim cards are first-80-chars verbatim slices. Hybrid plan: (a) heuristic_title() strips openers (btw/can u/okays/oh wait), extracts noun cluster, derives short code; (b) llm_rewrite() invokes 'claude -p' headlessly with prompt + nearby asst context, asks for {title, code, notes_summary} JSON; (c) route to LLM only when prompt is substantive (len>=150 OR urgency OR work-verb). Cache by prompt_hash. --no-llm flag for pure heuristic mode.
cards.writeup: Shipped 189688a. Two scripts: (a) scripts/sim_titler.py (heuristic + per-prompt LLM rewrite, cache pre-warm) for the realtime turn-paced replay path; (b) scripts/hourly_extractor.py (THE final model, per user 5/28 reframe): bucket events by 1 hour, send each hour's digest to claude -p haiku, get back a JSON array of discrete WORK UNIT cards. LLM handles title quality, CODE prefix, column routing (commits→done, urgency→mandatory, edits→inprogress, decisions→notes), tags, and notes. Wired into simulate_install.sh as --replay-mode hourly. Smoke pass: WB 3 hours → 4 cards (notes/inprogress/task/mandatory) titled in the live :7892 voice. ~5s per hour LLM call, so 24h = ~2min.

cards.title: SIM-REALTIME: lifecycle replay at wall-clock × speedup
cards.origin: User 5/28: 'can u run as if it were running in real life? as if users were using?'
cards.notes: Replay per-task lifecycle from real timestamps × speedup. For each task: add@t_prompt → fly ip@t_first_edit → fly done@t_ship_or_commit → fly ip --bug@t_regression → fly done@t_reship. Globally interleaved across tasks so gaps between sessions are visible. New --replay-mode realtime --speedup N flag on simulate_install.sh.
cards.writeup:
Shipped 6872aa4. scripts/lifecycle_replay.py (270L) walks all harvested sources chronologically and dispatches board actions turn-by-turn — substantive prompt = card.py add, first asst file edit = fly inprogress + 'edit FILE' subtask, clean ship hit = fly done, bug language after ship = fly inprogress --bug, commit = fly done, trivial reply = attach as subtask to active card.

Two pacing modes: --turns-per-sec N (fixed cadence, default 2.0) OR --gap-speedup N (preserve real idle gaps between turns × speedup factor — feel session boundaries). Wired into simulate_install.sh as --replay-mode realtime. Bulk-discover path unchanged when flag omitted.

Smoke test: bash simulate_install.sh --project ~/Desktop/WorkBoard --port 7896 --sim-dir ~/Desktop/board-sim-realtime --days 1 --max-turns 25 --replay-mode realtime --turns-per-sec 8 produced 25 cards split done(9) / task(13) / ip(1) / mandatory(2) with edit subtasks tracking file activity in real time. Trivial replies ('[Request interrupted by user]') correctly attached as subtasks rather than opening new cards. Synced to skill dir.

Open follow-ups: --gap-speedup not yet smoke-tested with real idle gaps; no commit-mapped writeup yet (just 'shipped (replay)'); replay assumes one active card at a time so concurrent task interleaving could mis-attribute.

cards.title: Per-turn card discipline: min 1 card / substantive turn + 6-col default
cards.origin: User 5/28: 'each turn = almost MINIALLY 1 card, so long as something is built, so people can see sequentially and find by #no. and if turn can have multiple cards; eg. phases? task / backlog / inprogress / done / notes / mandatory'
cards.notes: 4-part ship: (a) template 6-col default + migration, (b) SKILL.md rule, (c) discover2 1-turn-1-card re-tune, (d) multi-card split for phase/numbered lists. Each lands as own commit.
cards.writeup:
Shipped 4b14340. Four-part discipline change:

1. templates/board.json — canonical 6-col default (task / backlog / inprogress / done / notes / mandatory). serve.py runs an idempotent migration on load that appends any missing default cols, matched by id OR case-insensitive name so a user's hand-named 'notes' column isn't duplicated.

2. SKILL.md §E — new opening rule: 'Every substantive Claude turn that ships, edits, decides, or plans MUST file at least 1 card before the turn ends. Each turn = 1 card minimum.' Multi-phase asks split into N cards.

3. discover2 retune — Heuristic A continuation-merge DROPPED, Pass 2 file-overlap stitch DELETED. Each substantive user prompt = its own card. Trivial replies (yes/ok/sure/Request interrupted) attach as follow-ups to the previous task. Cross-source dedup added so jsonl and conversation_raw don't double-card the same prompt.

4. discover2 multi-card split — split_into_subtasks emits N children when a prompt enumerates 3+ Phase/numbered/bullet items. Skipped when prompt is quoting Claude back (❯ / 'u said' / >3 lines starting with >). MANDATORY_RE catches must / need to / urgent / asap / p0 / p1 / blocker / mandatory; tagged tasks route to the mandatory column via serve.py _task_to_card_args.

Smoke pass: QM 5d window → 60 cards split 18 done / 38 ip / 3 mandatory / 1 task. Headline a08aac60 now yields 7 distinct cards (was 1 collapsed under old extract_tasks). Column order canonical.

Synced to skill dir.

cards.title: Bootstrap stream: spread cards across columns, not all to Done
cards.origin: User 2026-05-28: 'this one i only saw done in the cards.... i dont see others like tasks, backlog etc.' on testing serve.py --bootstrap.
cards.notes: Current _session_to_card_args defaults to 'done' for sessions without explicit ship/defer hints. Need a recency+signal-density heuristic so cards spread across Task / In Progress / Backlog / Done.
cards.writeup:
Shipped 2026-05-28. Two fixes to _session_to_card_args column heuristic:

1. SHIP_RE matches "done|fixed|works|live" — Claude says those casually in nearly every assistant reply, so a ship-hint alone was unreliable signal. Now requires shipHints AND filesEdited together (real_ship = bool(ship) and bool(files)) before counting as actually shipped. Sessions where the model just said "done" with no file activity no longer land in Done.

2. Replaced "if not shipped → done" default with a recency+signal heuristic:
   - real_ship → done
   - defer (explicit) → backlog
   - age≤2d + files → inprogress (active work)
   - age≤3d + no files → task (recent chatter)
   - bugs no ship → backlog (open bug)
   - age>7d + files → done (old work)
   - age>7d no files → backlog (old chatter)
   - default → backlog (mentioned, unactioned)

Tag fix: shipped tag now also requires real_ship (not just ship hint).

Verified: bootstrap into a path with real session file activity now produces a varied distribution. Bootstrap into a fresh temp dir (no matching files) correctly shows task/backlog only (no false Done).

Hooks no longer probe 7891-7895 and take first-hit; they resolve THIS board's designated port via port_registry.assign(board_dir) and probe ONLY it (hook_user_prompt.sh:34-39, hook_session_start.sh:186-187). Comment at hook_user_prompt.sh:35 restates #204's symptom as solved: 'rev hint reflects this project's own server, not whichever board holds 7891.' Cross-board digest/live_line mixing now structurally impossible. (|| echo 7891 fallback only on registry-lookup failure = degraded, not normal path.) No code change needed.

cards.title: SKILL.md core/reference split (Phase 5 followup)
cards.origin: Phase 1 #88 audit: SKILL.md is 7,666 tokens — biggest single cost when board-steward engages. TOKEN_BUDGET.md targets <=2,000 tok always-load body.
cards.notes:
Trim SKILL.md core to ~1,500 tok keeping ONLY: greet/signoff/telemetry mandatory + decision table + traversal tiers + lifecycle E + reconciliation F + canonical write recipe + 'where the board lives'. Move to docs/: PLAYBOOK.md (the 1,791-tok 'What to do by trigger' block), SCHEMA.md, TAGS.md, INSTALL.md, BOOTSTRAP.md. SKILL.md links; Claude reads on demand.

--- REFINED APPROACH (user 2026-06-08, DEFER — not now) ---
Phase-conditional SKILL loading instead of a flat trim. Gate on the EXISTING ~/.board-steward/.onboarded marker (already distinguishes first-run vs after):
 • FIRST-TIME install (no .onboarded): read docs/BOOTSTRAP.md — the one-time install/history-mining flow.
 • AFTER bootstrap (.onboarded set): SKILL.md = lean LIVE-protocol SPINE ONLY (laws + lifecycle E + reconciliation F + carding LAW + decision table) — SKIP all bootstrap/install content every engagement.
GOALS: (1) cut tokens on the common LIVE path (bootstrap content is dead weight post-install), (2) BETTER adherence to the skills + LAWS — less noise → tighter compliance with carding discipline.
WHY DEFER: user afraid of affecting how LIVE cards are carded; do AFTER the IP cleanup + reinstall, validated so live carding behaviour is unchanged.

cards.title: Subtask state on ship: drop force-auto-check + cycle-as-subtask history
cards.origin: User asked: 'what if only 1 of 5 task is done... how can it track?' Walked through 3 cards × bug/improve permutations; identified that current ship path force-completes ALL open subtasks (server _autocheck_subtasks + client commitCardDrag walk), making 'shipped 1/5' structurally impossible AND making bug history invisible (bug tag is stripped on ship, no trace). User: 'save this into as mandatory since it is mandatory pre launch? cos i dont want to go too deep into it for now'.
cards.notes:
PROBLEM (current state, commit b336007):
1. Subtask is binary {open, done}; no skipped state.
2. cmd_move (server) + commitCardDrag (frontend) force-mark ALL open subtasks done on any move-to-done. So Done card is always N/N — no way to ship 1/5.
3. 'bug' tag is auto-stripped on ship → bug history (how many times this card has been bugged) is invisible the moment it's fixed.
4. 'improve' adds a subtask + reopens, but original ship + every bug cycle leave no subtask trail.

3 DESIGN OPTIONS (per discussion):

Option 1 — 'Skip' state. Subtask states become {open, done, skipped}. On ship, prompt: 'X subtasks open. mark: done / skip / keep'. Badge: '3 done · 2 skipped'. Honest, small schema bump. Doesn't fix bug history blindness.

Option 2 — Don't force-auto-check. Drop _autocheck_subtasks + commitCardDrag walk. Done card can sit at 1/5; open subtasks remain TODO inside Done card. Closest to user's literal question. Trade-off: Done card with open subtasks feels like it lies unless 'Done = card-level shipped, not subtask-level' is communicated.

Option 3 (RECOMMENDED) — Every cycle = a subtask. Each transition that adds work auto-appends a subtask:
  - initial ship → '☑ initial ship'
  - bug → '☐ fix bug: <reason>' (gets checked on next ship)
  - improve → '☐ add: <description>' (already works for improve, extend to bug + initial ship)
Card-level n/m becomes meaningful: 'shipped 3 cycles, 1 cycle pending'. Bug history is first-class data forever. Pairs with Option 2: drop the force-auto-check so each cycle's subtask is checked only when THAT cycle ships.

RECOMMENDATION: Option 3 + Option 2 together.

IMPLEMENTATION SKETCH (do before launch):
1. Remove _autocheck_subtasks call in card.py cmd_move (or replace with: 'only mark lastTouchedSubtask done, leave others alone').
2. Remove commitCardDrag walk block.
3. Extend cmd_bug: append open subtask '🐞 fix bug: <reason>' before reopen. Take optional --reason arg.
4. Extend cmd_add (or create cmd_ship): when first ship of a card happens (no prior subtasks), append '☑ initial ship' subtask retroactively + mark it done.
5. Update countSubtasks UI to show 'shipped: X cycles · in flight: Y' if any open subtasks remain.
6. SKILL.md: document the new semantic ('Done = card goal achieved; subtask = work cycle history').

PRE-LAUNCH BLOCKING: yes. This is the right structural foundation BEFORE inviting users; retrofitting later means broken historical badges across every existing board.
cards.writeup: Fix: cmd_subtask was writing now_iso() into lastTouchedSubtask. Now writes the actual subtask id (the new sid for add, args.sid for done/undone, None for rm). The #188 'close lastTouched on ship' now actually finds the right subtask. Verified with sim v28 — write tests subtask now closes correctly on first ship.

cards.title: Drag-right flicker: revert zone needs hysteresis
cards.notes:
User: 'when I move one column RIGHT it kept flickering — In Progress, then something else, then In Progress, then something else.'

Cause: #131 revert zone = source's full original bbox (e.g. X=100-340). Preview fires when cursor exits at X=341. Revert fires when cursor re-enters at X=339. As cursor jitters around the slot boundary, they ping-pong.

Fix: inset the revert zone by ~30px on each side. Cursor needs to be deep inside source's original slot to trigger revert, not just barely. Boundary hysteresis kills the oscillation.
cards.writeup:
Shipped 35f5d60.

#131 revert zone in column dragover now uses an inset bbox: cursor X must be in [srcOrigRect.left + 30, srcOrigRect.right - 30] to trigger revert. Outside that center band → no revert. This creates hysteresis at the slot boundary — preview fires when cursor exits source's bbox (X > right edge), revert fires only well inside (X < right edge - 30), so cursor jitter near the boundary doesn't oscillate.

Side effect: you have to drag back deeper into the original slot to revert. If 30px feels too aggressive (slot might be only 240px wide), can tune.

cards.title: Drag back to source's original slot reverts preview
cards.notes:
When user previews a reorder then drags back toward the source's original slot, can't get back to the starting layout — every column hover only fires a NEW splice. No way to express 'never mind'.

Fix: at dragstart, source's original bbox is already captured in _colDragRects. In column dragover, if cursor X is within that bbox AND a preview is in flight, revert state.columns to snapshot + FLIP. After revert, source is back where it started; further hover doesn't re-revert (idempotence on _colLastPreviewTargetId).
cards.writeup:
Shipped aae04f2.

In column dragover handler, before stack-zone branching: if a preview is currently in flight (_colLastPreviewTargetId set) AND cursor X is within the source column's ORIGINAL bbox (looked up from _colDragRects which was captured at dragstart), revert state.columns to _colDragSnapshot, clear _colLastPreviewTargetId, render+FLIP. Net effect: dragging back over your starting slot snaps the row back to its dragstart layout. Drop anywhere now lands on the slot you're actually pointing at, including your original.

cards.title: Stacked-child drag: target column shifts to make space
cards.notes:
When the source column is currently stacked under a parent (i.e. has no horizontal slot), dragging it out toward another root column makes the moved column land AFTER the target instead of AT the target's slot. The target never shifts — feels broken.

Fix: in previewReorderColumn, branch on whether source was stacked at dragstart. Stacked source → insert at target's CURRENT index in next[] (post-removal). Root source → keep existing splice(ti, 0, moved) iPhone semantic.
cards.writeup:
Shipped 41731c3.

previewReorderColumn now branches on wasStacked = !!snap[si].stackUnder at the time of splice:
- Root source: insertAt = ti (original snapshot index). After splice removes src and shifts targets left by 1, splicing AT ti lands moved at target's OLD visual position. Iphone semantic: target glides left.
- Stacked source: insertAt = next.findIndex(c=>c.id===targetId). Because removing a stacked column doesn't change the visual row, target's new index equals its post-removal slot. Splicing there lands moved at target's CURRENT slot. Target glides right by 1.

Net behavior: dragging a stacked child out toward a root column now visibly pushes that root column over to make space, just like a normal column drag does.

cards.title: Stack-zone wins: revert reorder preview when cursor enters bottom 40%
cards.notes:
User: 'when I move one column to another to stack, it shifts left or right a bit too quickly before I can stack.'

Fix (option 1): when cursor enters _inStackZone of ANY column, revert in-progress reorder preview (state.columns ← snapshot, FLIP). Neighbors snap back so the stack target stays put while the user lands their drop.
cards.writeup:
Shipped ff683ab.

In templates/board.html column dragover handler — when _inStackZone(e) is true:
- Add .col-drop-stack-target as before
- NEW: if _colDragSnapshot && _colLastPreviewTargetId, restore state.columns from the snapshot, clear _colLastPreviewTargetId, render+FLIP.

Net behavior: as soon as the cursor crosses into a column's bottom 40%, neighbors snap back to their original positions and the target column stops drifting. Drop now lands on the column the user was actually aiming at. Source stays opacity:0 in original slot regardless.

cards.writeup:
Added '↺ Reset columns' button next to '+ Add column' in the board view. On click, prompts confirm, then:

1. Pins main workflow columns leftmost in order: Task → Backlog → In Progress → Done → Notes. Matches by id OR by case-insensitive name so it works regardless of column id (e.g., 'notes' lives at col-akbbnyy on this board after the 5/27 QM↔WB migration).
2. Sorts remaining columns by recency: max(card.updatedAt || card.createdAt) per column, descending. Empty columns sort to the end.
3. Clears stackUnder on every column so any vertical stacks (#117) collapse back to a flat row.
4. One pushUndo entry for the entire reset (Ctrl+Z restores prior order in one step). FLIP-animated via captureColumnRects + flipPlayColumns.

Shipped in commit 195c848.

cards.title: When i shift from left to right, it needs to glide smoothly EVEN BEFORE i place it down, so eg if i hover OVER the existing card, or maybe even 50% of the card, the card slides over. so eg. i shift A -->B...  then even before placing A down to replace B, B shifts over to A... just like how u rearrange apps in the iphone.
cards.notes:
[2026-05-27] Naive shift-down attempt c0863bf REVERTED in 3a2b175.

Re-read of original ask: iPhone home-screen REFLOW, not static shift.
  • Track source column AND source array-index when drag starts
  • During dragover, compute "would-be insert position" from cursor location
  • All cards between source-index and target-index translate to fill the gap
    (direction depends on drag direction)
  • On drop, do REAL reordering (insert at indicated position), not just column change

Couples conceptually with #117 (vertical column stacks) — best to build together
so reflow logic is stack-aware from the start. User confirmed both should ship
in same session, next session.

Touch sites: state.cards array ordering (need to track within-column index),
makeCard dragstart/dragover, column drop handler, render() — likely need
captureCardRects() + flipPlayCards() helpers mirroring the existing column
FLIP code at line 1129.
cards.writeup:
Shipped 2026-05-27 in commit 73cb351 — live iPhone-style card reflow.

What works:
- Drag a card; as cursor enters another column, target column's siblings reflow to make room (FLIP-animated, 260ms overshoot).
- Cursor over top half of a card → insert before; bottom half → insert after.
- Live preview = real DOM move during dragover, not a placeholder.
- Drop commits to state.cards array (real reorder, persists).
- Dragend without drop re-renders from canonical state (undoes the preview).
- Cross-column drag updates column + updatedAt + doneAt as before.
- Same-priority drag-reorder within a column works (priority sort is stable in JS, so array-order survives within bucket).

Known limit:
- Cross-priority drag visually previews but priority sort wins on render. E.g. dragging a low-priority card above critical cards lands it back in low bucket. Acceptable v1 — change priority via the chip if you want it to stick higher.

Architecture:
- _cardDrag state object lives next to _colDragRects
- captureCardRects/flipPlayCards mirror the column FLIP helpers
- reflowCardDragPreview is the live-preview heart (DOM move + FLIP)
- commitCardDrag and cancelCardDrag are the drop / no-drop terminators

cards.title: Allow card to be shiftable so it can "stack" vertically imagine there are 3 columns, each having 3 cards. i can shift Col B  to stack BELOW col A ... and Col C to stack BELOW Col B... so when i see its vertically stacked, 1 column, Col A, B, C stacked vertically.
cards.notes:
[2026-05-27] Deferred to next session — to be built alongside #119.

Design sketch:
  • Schema: column gains optional `stackUnder: <columnId> | null`.
    null = top-level (own slot in horizontal layout); non-null = sits
    directly under that column in a vertical stack.
  • Render: walk state.columns; for each column with stackUnder=null,
    gather its descendants (chain via stackUnder links) into a vertical
    stack container. Multiple stacks render in horizontal flex.
  • Drop UX: drop column on BOTTOM 40% of target column → set dragged.stackUnder
    = target.id. Drop on side or top → clear stackUnder (back to top-level).
  • Animation: FLIP captures rects pre-render, plays inverse post-render
    (mirrors existing column FLIP).
  • Back-compat: existing boards have no stackUnder field → all top-level,
    no behavior change until user actively drags.

Coupling with #119 surfaced by user: build together so card reflow understands
the 2D column layout from the outset (vs retrofitting later).
cards.writeup:
Shipped 2026-05-27 in 2 commits.

c35b410 — schema (no visual change)
  + column.stackUnder: <columnId> | null
  + helpers: getStackRoots, getStackChildren, walkStack (depth-cap 32), stackOrderedColumns
  + migrateStackUnder on every _absorbState (idempotent; fills missing, resets dangling refs, breaks self-cycles)
  + templates/board.json seeds with stackUnder: null on default columns

4338bb9 — render (visual)
  + .column-stack wrapper class (flex-column, transparent)
  + render() walks getStackRoots → wrap each root + descendants
  + drop UX: BOTTOM 40% of target → stackColumnUnder; TOP 60%/side → flat reorder
  + .col-drop-stack-target dashed-bottom hint while drag hovers stack zone
  + cycle guard in stackColumnUnder (target must not be descendant of src)
  + reorderColumn auto-clears source.stackUnder when source moves back to row

Bonus: discovered + fixed card.py SERVER_URL hardcode that was clobbering QM from WB CLI calls. Recovered QM from git HEAD. Commit 0768718 routes by board-path now.

cards.title: Robustness umbrella: flock + rolling backups + recover/migrate/repair-links
cards.origin: Plan v2 Phase 3.5a-e. Data loss is unforgivable failure. Fix: (a) card.py atomic_save wraps fcntl.flock LOCK_EX, msvcrt on Win. (b) serve.py keeps board/.backups/board-{rev}.json for last 10 revs. (c) card.py recover restores from backup. (d) card.py migrate applies schemaVersion migrations. (e) card.py repair-links walks linkedCards, fixes one-sided. Arch gaps #8, #9, #13, #14.
cards.notes: 3.5a/b: flock spans read->write in card.py main() no-server path; server path POST unchanged. 20-way parallel = 0 lost updates; backups cap 10.
cards.writeup: SHIPPED 260529 — full robustness umbrella across 3 commits. 3.5a flock + 3.5b backups (b790eb7): scripts/_boardio.py shared by card.py + serve.py; cross-process board_lock spanning read->write in main() (no-server path) = 0 lost updates on 20-way parallel test; rolling .backups/board-<rev>.json keep 10. 3.5c recover (5718c70): list/dry-run/--apply restore w/ JSON+cards[] validation, restore itself reversible. 3.5d migrate + 3.5e repair-links (f547178): idempotent schemaVersion migrations (v1 per-card fields, v2 board fields) + linkedCards integrity repair (dangling/self/dupe drop, one-sided reciprocity restore), both dry-run default. All verified on crafted + live boards. Live dry-runs surfaced: schemaVersion 1->2 (0-change) and 5 real dangling links (#83/#65/#66/#72/#74) — live cleanup offered to user separately.

Maps board absolute path → {port, pid, started_at}. serve.py writes BEFORE serve_forever() (so card.py running immediately after boot can resolve), removes on graceful shutdown (best-effort, stale entries self-heal on next boot). sys.path manually extended so the import works under launchd absolute paths. card.py _resolve_server_url priority: 1) $BOARD_SERVER env override, 2) port_registry.lookup + /health verify, 3) probe 7891-7900 (back-compat + ad-hoc servers). cards.title: Hook CWD fallback when walk-up misses the board
cards.origin: Plan v2 Phase 1.5b. User ran Claude in /HFTAgents and steward did not fire (yesterday). Migration to SessionStart fixed per-prompt bleed but CWD-walk reliability is separate. Fix: hook_session_start.sh reads ~/.claude/projects/*/sessions/*.jsonl for active session's recorded cwd → resolve board there. If still nothing, print one-line install hint. Pain signal #2.
cards.writeup:
Shipped 2026-05-28 (Phase 1.5b). hook_session_start.sh now has a jsonl-driven fallback when the CWD-walk fails.

Implementation:
- Walk up from PWD looking for board/board.json (existing).
- If not found, invoke scripts/_hook_find_board.py PWD (new helper, kept separate from the shell script because nested heredocs inside $() proved fragile). The helper searches the 30 most-recently-modified ~/.claude/projects/*/sessions/*.jsonl, finds the first line with a `cwd` field, accepts only cwds that are PWD or descended from PWD (so a parent shell with a board under it gets picked up, but unrelated projects don't leak), walks UP from that cwd looking for board/board.json, stops at PWD so we don't escape scope.
- If still nothing → stay silent (original behavior preserved — no noise in non-board projects).

Verified 3 scenarios from CLI:
  TEST 1 (cd WorkBoard): CWD-walk hits → WB board.
  TEST 2 (cd ~/Desktop): CWD-walk misses → fallback finds QM (most recent session was in HFTAgents).
  TEST 3 (cd /tmp): no descendant board → silent exit.

Resolves the "ran Claude in /HFTAgent and steward didn't turn up" report from 2026-05-27.

cards.title: Bootstrap auto-populates cards from history (zero-input install)
cards.origin: Plan v2 Phase 1.5a. discover.py mines history but is read-only; user has to ask Claude to translate it into cards. VISION §3 promises auto-bootstrap + §4 promises zero input. Fix: serve.py --bootstrap invokes discover.py inline, picks top 10-20 sessions, streams card.py add calls at 200ms pacing. --no-discover for genuine empty start. Pain signal #1.
cards.writeup:
Shipped 2026-05-28 (Phase 1.5a). serve.py --bootstrap now streams cards from prior Claude sessions instead of producing an empty board.

Implementation:
- _session_to_card_args(session): maps a discover.py session summary to card.py add args. Confidence filter drops sessions with no firstUserPrompt AND no filesEdited, or very-thin (<15 char prompt + no ship/files). Column: shipped→done, deferred/bug-only→backlog, default→done (past work). Tags: bug/deferred/shipped from heuristic hints + always 'discovered'. Origin: "Discovered from session <sid> (<date>). User said: <verbatim>". Notes: turn counts, files (capped 10), ship/defer/bug hint excerpts.
- _stream_discovered_cards(project_root, board_dir, port, days, max_sessions, delay_s=0.25): background thread. Waits for HTTP server to bind (poll /health), runs discover.py, reverses session list so oldest streams first (user watches history fill chronologically), POSTs each via subprocess card.py add at 0.25s pacing. SSE fires card-added events, browser animates them in.
- main() now spawns the thread on --bootstrap unless --no-discover. New flags: --no-discover, --discover-days N (default 7), --discover-max N (default 20), --title <X>.

Verified end-to-end in ~/Desktop/test-bootstrap-<ts>/: 5 cards streamed in ~5s with correct tags/columns/titles. Brand-new temp dirs unrelated to history correctly produce 0 cards (filter rejects unrelated sessions).

This is the headline VISION moment: "user opens the board for the first time and already sees their last week of work, chronologically, animated in card-by-card."
