cards.title: Linux (systemd) + Windows (Task Scheduler) autostart parity
cards.origin: Audit (2026-05-27) flagged ship blocker: install_launchd.py is macOS-only. Non-Mac users get no one-command autostart parity — degrades VISION.md §3 "instant startup" promise. Build (a) install_systemd.py for Linux, (b) install_taskscheduler.py for Windows, (c) install_autostart.py dispatcher detecting sys.platform. Same --port/--project/--status/--uninstall/--dry-run flags so install recipe stays platform-uniform. Tracked in /Users/malco/.claude/plans/sprightly-marinating-prism.md Phase 4.
cards.notes: Phase 4 BOARD-CROSSPLAT: building install_systemd.py (Linux --user unit), install_taskscheduler.py (Windows schtasks XML), install_autostart.py (sys.platform dispatcher). Same --project/--port/--status/--uninstall/--dry-run flags as install_launchd.py.
cards.writeup:
Shipped in 55efffc.

  55efffc  #103 BOARD-CROSSPLAT: Linux systemd + Windows Task Scheduler autostart parity

Phase 4 complete. Three installers shipped + dispatcher, all mirroring install_launchd.py's flag surface (--project/--port/--status/--uninstall/--dry-run). install_systemd.py = Linux systemd --user service (Restart=always, suggests loginctl enable-linger); install_taskscheduler.py = Windows schtasks ONLOGON task via pythonw.exe (no console window); install_autostart.py = sys.platform dispatcher delegating via runpy. Unprivileged on every OS. Verified on this Mac: all 3 --dry-run print correct units; dispatcher routes darwin->launchd; real install on wrong OS refuses cleanly with pointer to correct installer; uninstall dry-runs + py_compile all pass. SKILL.md gains Autostart section + scripts-table row. Synced to skill dir.

cards.title: File-edit → card-link auto-discovery via PreToolUse hook
cards.origin: VISION.md §4 promise: PreToolUse hook on Edit/Write inspects file path; if any open card's notes or linkedFiles reference that path, card's lastTouchedAt updates atomically and SSE event flashes the card border. Read-only on settings.json; non-blocking on hook failure. Tracked in /Users/malco/.claude/plans/sprightly-marinating-prism.md Phase 3c.
cards.notes: Phase 3c: PreToolUse hook on Edit/Write flashes card border via SSE when a linked file is touched
cards.writeup: Fixed in ed6a6c5 (WB main, pushed). _hook_flash_linked.py now anchors find_board on the EDITED FILE's path (which is always inside the project), falling back to PWD only if that misses. This is the millions-of-users case from VISION.md: anyone whose Claude Code launches from $HOME would have had a dead hook. Verified LIVE 260529: edit of _hook_flash_linked.py → #102 pulsed coral + '⚡ #102 — touched _hook_flash_linked.py' toast on :7892 (sseClients=1). Synced skill-dir deploy copy identical to WB source.

subtasks.text: 🐞 fix bug: Hook resolved board by walking UP from PWD; when Claude Code runs from $HOME the board is a CHILD dir, so find_board never matched and no card flashed. Manual /flash worked but the live PreToolUse hook silently no-op'd.

cards.title: Auto-promotion to Done when Claude ships
cards.origin: VISION.md §4 promise: when Claude completes a task matching an in-progress card (heuristic: card code/title appears in assistant's last 3 turns + assistant says shipped/done/merged/verified), Claude auto-runs card.py move <num> done with writeup from git log of last commit. ZERO user input. Risk: wrong card. Mitigation: ambiguous matches surface a one-line "→ #N or #M?" disambiguation. Tracked in /Users/malco/.claude/plans/sprightly-marinating-prism.md Phase 3b.
cards.notes: Phase 3b: actually ship the feature. Prior done state was a recon false-positive (empty writeup, no code, no commit).
cards.writeup:
Shipped in aa13384.

  aa13384  #101 BOARD-AUTO-MOVE: card.py auto-ship subcommand

Self-hosted dogfood ship: this card was promoted to done by its own implementation. Scan mode confirmed via 'card.py auto-ship --since-ref HEAD~3' (run before --apply). Scoring breakdown: code 'BOARD-AUTO-MOVE' present in subject = 3pts, '#101' marker = 2pts, total 5. Matched single commit aa13384.

cards.title: Auto-card-creation when user signals intent
cards.origin: VISION.md §4 promise: when user says "I have an idea: X" / "remember to" / "todo:" / "later we should", Claude detects intent via dense-keyword routing and runs card.py add to Ideas column with the user's exact phrasing as origin. ZERO user input. Risk: false positives (Claude over-adding). Mitigation: confidence threshold + 2-second "added ✕ undo" toast via SSE. Tracked in /Users/malco/.claude/plans/sprightly-marinating-prism.md Phase 3a.
cards.notes: Phase 3a: auto-card from idea-intent keywords + 2s undo toast
cards.writeup: Shipped Phase 3a in b6e52d5. card.py add --auto + --auto-source flags stamp card.meta = {autoCreated:true, autoSource}; auto-defaults --column to ideas (created on demand via _ensure_ideas_col helper mirroring _ensure_super_urgent_col). board.html: new .undo-toast CSS (top-center, shimmer ring, WAAPI timer bar) + showAutoCardToast(card,ms=5000) helper (Keep dismisses early, Undo pushes onto _undoStack and removes via scheduleSave); SSE card-added handler fires it when card.meta.autoCreated. SKILL.md §G locks the canonical command + 5 hard skips (same-turn task, <20 char body, dup card, recap quote, nvm/wait) — under-engage when uncertain. E2E live smoke #248 on :7892 ✓.

cards.title: Verify SessionStart hook fires in fresh Claude session inside HFTAgents
cards.origin: Session 1 of 5/27 shipped SessionStart migration (8b45500). Installation verified this session: settings.json matcher present, script executable, manual invocation from HFTAgents produces correct digest. But this session started in /Users/malco (no board upstream) so the hook silent-gated correctly — autostart fire was never observed empirically.
cards.notes:
Test:
1. Quit this Claude session.
2. Start fresh Claude inside /Users/malco/Desktop/QuantifyMe/HFTAgents.
3. First turn should show <board-steward-session-start> block in context.

Pass: digest visible → SessionStart migration empirically validated, close as done with writeup.
Fail: no block → debug. Possible causes: Claude version doesn't support SessionStart matcher, settings.json schema mismatch, hook stderr swallowed, CWD mismatch (Claude might launch from $HOME not project dir).

Links: c-board-sessionstart (#97), c-board-protocol-miss (#84).
cards.writeup:
Verified by scripts/health_check.py — all 4 checks green:
  ✅ launchd:7891 pid=6512  ✅ /health rev=165 cards=70 (HFTAgents)
  ✅ launchd:7892 pid=6515  ✅ /health rev=1  cards=44 (WorkBoard)
  ✅ SessionStart hook installed → hook_session_start.sh
  ✅ hook fired in last session (14s ago)

Hook also empirically fired this very session (proof: <board-steward-session-start>
digest at top of conversation). Phase 0 verification gate satisfied.

cards.title: SKILL.md: dense-keyword description + Must Use/Skip decision table (#4)
cards.origin: Research agent finding: ui-ux-pro-max packs description: with trigger keywords for deterministic skill auto-routing. Our description was descriptive but lacked verbs Claude could match on. Plus #84's drift class: Claude shipped 7 features in a row yesterday without carding, because nothing told it 'this counts'. A decision table fixes both.
cards.writeup:
Two complementary changes to SKILL.md to make the skill self-gate correctly:

1. **Dense-keyword `description:`** (line 3 in frontmatter): rewrote from descriptive prose to a keyword-packed router. Now leads with "MUST USE when user says — shipped, deployed, merged, fixed, completed, finished, verified, done, deferred, blocked, paused, moved, add card, log this, track it, what shipped, what's left, status, where are we, what did we do yesterday, sprint, backlog, todo, kanban". Plus "Triggers — git commit / push / systemctl restart..." and an explicit "SKIP for pure code questions (debug, explain, refactor, rename) that don't ship anything." Mirrors ui-ux-pro-max's pattern — Claude reads all skill descriptions on every prompt; dense keywords make routing deterministic instead of inferred.

2. **"When to engage" decision table** (new section between telemetry mandatory and "Where the board lives"): 10-row table mapping concrete user situations to Must-use / Use lightly / Skip actions. Specifically calls out the drift class from #84 ("Conversation just shipped something but no card moved → Must use — backfill NOW. Don't batch to session end.") and the SessionStart-digest-first reflex ("If a user asks 'did we do X?' your first instinct should be card.py list — not 'I recall doing X'").

Default bias rule added: "under-engage when uncertain. A missed card is recoverable. An over-eager skill that interjects on every code question is noise."

Files changed: SKILL.md (1 file, +27 lines). Synced to ~/.agents/skills/board-steward/SKILL.md.

Verification deferred — like the SessionStart hook itself, this only takes effect when Claude reloads skill descriptions on next session start. To verify: next session in a non-board project, confirm board-steward does NOT engage on "debug this function"; next session in HFTAgents, confirm it engages on "ship X".

cards.title: Migrate hook UserPromptSubmit → SessionStart (claude-mem pattern)
cards.origin: Research agent on claude-mem/graphify/ui-ux-pro-max revealed claude-mem uses SessionStart (fires once per session, reliably). User's UserPromptSubmit hook silently failed inside HFTAgents yesterday. SessionStart fixes both the no-fire bug AND the per-prompt token bleed.
cards.writeup:
Replaced UserPromptSubmit with SessionStart as the default hook primitive.

Shipped:
- scripts/hook_session_start.sh (new): walks up for board/board.json, probes :7891-7895 for live server, auto-spawns serve.py if missing (10s lock), generates digest (column counts + last-shipped card with relative time) via inline python, injects <board-steward-session-start> block.
- scripts/install_hooks.py: rewrote to support --hook session-start|user-prompt-submit|both. Default = session-start. Backup + idempotent + auto-uninstalls non-selected variants.

Verification:
- Smoke-tested hook from inside HFTAgents → injected clean digest "🚨 SUPER URGENT: 3 · 📌 MANDATORY: 5 · 💡 Ideas: 2 · Backlog: 36 · Blocked: 2 · Done: 42 · Consideration: 1 · notes: 3 · Last shipped: #92 BOARD-AUTOSTART (<1h ago)"
- Installed for self at ~/.claude/settings.json with backup at .bak-20260527T001411Z.
- Status verified: ✓ session-start installed, ✗ user-prompt-submit uninstalled.

Why SessionStart > UserPromptSubmit:
- Fires ONCE per session, not per prompt → token cost paid once not N times.
- Doesn't depend on prompt timing (UserPromptSubmit was failing on session boot in HFTAgents — separate bug we suspected but couldn't reproduce; SessionStart sidesteps).
- Mirrors claude-mem's design (npx claude-mem install registers SessionStart).

Caveat: takes effect on NEXT Claude Code session restart, not this one. To verify: close + reopen Claude in HFTAgents, look for <board-steward-session-start> block in first turn.

Token math: ~200 tokens once at session boot, vs old hook ~80 tokens × every-prompt. Break-even at ~3 prompts; in practice we run 20-50 prompts/session so net savings ~5-10× on hook cost.

cards.title: On session start, auto-open http://127.0.0.1:7891 in any installed browser
cards.origin: User: 'write in kanban prelaunch to make sure http://127.0.0.1:7891 is opened... even if browser is not open, it will open any browser it has; safari; chrome; firefox etc.'
cards.writeup:
Even with server auto-started via launchd, the URL still needs to be visible — user shouldn't have to remember to type the URL or click a bookmark.

Open design questions for next session:
- WHERE does the auto-open trigger? Two candidates:
  (a) launchd plist runs a 'open http://...' once at login (macOS native, single-shot)
  (b) hook_user_prompt.sh detects "no tab open" and opens — but it can't tell what's open in the browser without OS-level scripting.
- WHICH browser? macOS `open <url>` uses default browser. If chrome/safari/firefox isn't default, it might not open the user's preferred one. User wants "any browser it has" — implies fallback chain.
- ANNOYANCE GUARD: don't re-open a tab every session start if one's already open. macOS has no portable way to detect this without AppleScript per-browser.

Path A (simplest): launchd's RunAtLoad opens the URL once per login via `osascript -e 'tell app "Google Chrome" to open location ...'` or `open <url>` for default browser.
Path B (defensive): write a `open_board.sh` that probes for an open tab via AppleScript on the 3 majors; opens only if none found.

Linked to #88 (autostart) — both serve the "always-there" UX.

cards.title: Server auto-starts on login (launchd) + hook auto-spawn fallback
cards.origin: User: 'kanban should automatically open. is my agent running? is skill installed?' — found NO autostart wired (server died with shell). Hook installed but only ran when board found in CWD.
cards.writeup:
Shipped two layers of "server is always there":

1. launchd plist (install_launchd.py NEW): idempotent macOS installer. Generates ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.boardsteward.<port>.plist with RunAtLoad+KeepAlive. Args: --project --port --status --uninstall --dry-run. Logs to ~/Library/Logs/boardsteward/. Backs up existing plist before overwriting. Refuses non-macOS.

2. hook_user_prompt.sh auto-spawn (NEW): when board/board.json is found in CWD tree but port :7891 is unreachable, hook spawns serve.py via nohup+disown. 10s lock file prevents double-spawn on rapid prompts. Covers (a) fresh installs before launchd is set up, (b) launchd crash recovery, (c) user killed it for debugging.

Verification today:
- Installed for self pointing at HFTAgents board → loaded as PID 3952, /health returns rev 128 89 cards.
- Killed launchd, fired hook from HFTAgents, server came up 1.5s later. Re-loaded launchd cleanly.

Files in WorkBoard repo: scripts/install_launchd.py (new) + scripts/hook_user_prompt.sh (extended). Synced to ~/.agents/skills/board-steward/scripts/.

For other users: README quick-start needs to add install_launchd.py as 3rd line after install_hooks.py (defer to commit message).

cards.title: Pre-launch checklist: skill cross-checks board + asks 'OK to launch with these undone?'
cards.origin: User 2026-05-26: 'where check with users against work board, this isnt done yet / are u okay with launching without this being done?' Idea = when user signals an imminent ship/launch/publish/deploy-to-public, the skill should not silently let it through. Instead it should surface every incomplete SUPER URGENT and MANDATORY card and ask explicit confirmation: 'These N items are still open: [list]. Launch anyway?' Prevents the 'forgot we hadn't done X before going public' class of regret.
cards.notes:
DESIGN SKETCH:

Trigger signals (assistant detects these in own about-to-send response OR in user message):
- 'push to public', 'ship to prod', 'launch', 'publish', 'go live', 'release', 'make public', 'open the gate'
- gh release create, GitHub repo flip from private→public, npm publish, pypi upload
- domain DNS go-live, CDN purge, marketing/announcement send
- false-positive guard: NOT triggered by routine `git push origin main`, only public-facing surface changes

What the gate does:
1. Read board index.json — filter cards in columns: super-urgent + mandatory + any column with kind=todo
2. Filter to: priority=critical OR priority=mid AND not in done/blocked
3. If list non-empty, BLOCK the assistant from proceeding until user confirms — surface as:

   ⚠️ Pre-launch check: 4 items still open
   - [SUPER URGENT] #84 BOARD-PROTOCOL-MISS (critical)
   - [SUPER URGENT] #88 BOARD-TOKEN-AUDIT (critical)
   - [MANDATORY]    #85 BOARD-AUTO-URGENT (mid)
   - [MANDATORY]    #86 SIM-2D (mid)
   OK to proceed with launch anyway? (yes / no / skip-once)

4. On 'yes': log a launch-event in telemetry with the skipped items so we can review later what shipped-with-known-debt
5. On 'no': halt; user can either bump those items to done or genuinely cancel the launch
6. On 'skip-once': proceed without nag for this turn only

How to wire:
- Option A: A separate hook (PreToolUse on gh/git push/npm publish?) that runs scripts/launch_gate.py
- Option B: Inject into the existing UserPromptSubmit hook — detect launch language in user prompt, surface the gate
- Option C: Lightweight CLI: `card.py launch-check` that Claude is INSTRUCTED to run before any launch-shaped action (relies on Claude remembering — same failure mode as the original protocol miss, see #84)

Recommended: Option A (real hook) for serious enforcement; Option B as the lightweight default that ships with install_hooks.py.

OUTPUT (what would land):
- scripts/launch_gate.py (CLI)
- hook_user_prompt.sh extended to detect launch keywords + call launch_gate.py
- install_hooks.py wires whichever option chosen
- telemetry/events.jsonl logs each launch-check w/ skipped items
- SKILL.md section: "Pre-launch gate (mandatory)"

WHY MANDATORY: this is what makes the board MATTER at the moment that matters most. Every other feature is convenience; this one prevents shipping a broken/incomplete public surface.
Phase 2e: card.py prelaunch-check CLI + hook detection of launch/publish keywords
cards.writeup: Shipped 2026-05-28. card.py prelaunch-check exits 9 if any super-urgent/mandatory card with critical/mid priority is still open, 0 if clear. --json + --count modes for scripting. SessionStart hook digest gains '🚨 LAUNCH-BLOCKING: N open' line when count>0 (no per-prompt token bleed; same pass that counts columns). SKILL.md decision table gains rows for launch-keyword (publish/launch/go live/gh release) and urgency-keyword (links to #85). Picked option C from card spec (CLI + doc) over option A (PreToolUse hook) and option B (UserPromptSubmit hook) — the latter would re-introduce the per-prompt bleed we explicitly migrated away from in yesterday's S1.

cards.title: Done column: order LATEST first within each day group
cards.origin: User 2026-05-26 16:21: 'Today · 26 May 2026 for done, and all the tasks, i want to order it as LATEST first.' Currently Done groups by day (TODAY · YESTERDAY · 2026-05-25 …) but within each day group the within-day ordering may not be strict newest-doneAt-first. User wants strict LIFO so the most-recently-shipped item is the top of the list always.
cards.notes:
Investigation needed in board.html renderColumn / Done-grouping logic:
- Verify current within-day sort key — is it doneAt desc, num desc, or insertion order?
- If sort key is insertion order or by num, change to doneAt desc within each day bucket
- Day groups themselves already newest-first (TODAY at top) — preserve that
- Edge case: cards with same-minute doneAt should tie-break by num desc
- After fix: visually verify on QM board that today's most recent done (#84 BOARD-PROTOCOL-MISS) appears ABOVE other today-done items
- File: ~/Desktop/WorkBoard/templates/board.html (renderColumn or Done-specific grouping)
- Sync to ~/.agents/skills/board-steward/templates/board.html + active install ~/Desktop/QuantifyMe/HFTAgents/board/board.html
cards.writeup:

(fallback updatedAt → createdAt). Day-headers were already newest-first;
this fixes the within-day ordering so the most recently shipped card is
visually at the top of its day.

Hard-refresh to see it.

cards.title: Benchmark vs popular Claude-memory skills + harden against token degradation
cards.origin: User 2026-05-26: 'check against current skills that are popular that gives claude memory one, cos of token cost, to make sure this skill also optimises token and prevents degradation of tokens etc.' Real risk: board-steward injects a <board-steward-protocol> reminder on EVERY user prompt (newly wired in #84). Plus card.py/index.json/board.json reads consume tokens whenever the Steward acts. If the skill is heavier than peers like mem0/claude-memory/MCP memory servers, it taxes the same context budget it claims to organize — defeating its purpose.
cards.notes:
INVESTIGATION PLAN:

1. Identify the popular comparison set (do not guess — actually look up current state of art):
   - claude-memory / claude-mem skills on the Claude skills directory
   - mem0 (mem0.ai) — MCP server, popular
   - letta (formerly MemGPT) — MCP server, conversational memory tier
   - Cursor's @docs / @file context — different pattern, but relevant comparator
   - Built-in CLAUDE.md / project memory — the no-skill baseline
   - Other kanban/todo MCP servers if any exist

2. Measure THIS skill's current cost honestly:
   - Hook injection per prompt: count tokens in <board-steward-protocol> block (currently ~150 tokens)
   - index.json read (Tier 1): currently ~33KB → ~8K tokens — expensive if Claude reads on every Steward invocation
   - Full board.json read (Tier 2): currently ~115KB → ~30K tokens — should be RARE, only for deep card edits
   - card.py output: tiny, fine
   - SKILL.md preamble loaded into context on invocation: count it
   - TOTAL per session if user does N updates: model it

3. Benchmark each peer:
   - Per-prompt context overhead (tokens)
   - Per-action overhead (tokens)
   - Storage growth over 30/60/90 days (does it bloat?)
   - Compaction/decay strategy (or lack of one)

4. Identify degradation risks specific to board-steward:
   - Hook fires EVERY prompt — even read-only / "what's next" questions
   - index.json grows linearly with cards (33KB at 78 cards → projection at 500 cards / 2000 cards)
   - Done-column writeups bloat over time (some writeups are 2-3KB each)
   - archive_done.py mitigates this for >14d done cards — verify it actually keeps index lean
   - SKILL.md is 286+ lines — loaded on every Skill() invocation; trim ruthlessly to essentials

5. Hardening targets (the deliverable):
   - HOOK SLIM: keep <board-steward-protocol> block under 100 tokens; consider variable detail by prior-turn signal (only fire full reminder if last assistant turn used Edit/Write/Bash)
   - INDEX SLIM: cap index.json at top-N most-recent-activity cards; rest live in archive
   - DONE COMPACTION: writeups in archive don't need to live in active index; one-line summary suffices
   - SKILL.md TIER: split SKILL.md into a 30-line core (always loaded) + reference sections (loaded only on need)
   - INSTRUMENT: log per-Steward-invocation token cost in telemetry (estimated from char count) so report.py can flag bloat over time

6. Output:
   - docs/TOKEN_BUDGET.md in WorkBoard repo with measured numbers + ranking vs peers
   - Concrete cuts implemented (commit hash)
   - "Token cost of this skill" line added to README so users can decide before installing

WHY SUPER URGENT: this hook just shipped and now runs on every prompt for me + every future user. If it's a tax instead of an asset, the skill ships harm. Validate cost BEFORE anyone else installs.
cards.writeup:
Shipped 2026-05-28.

MEASUREMENTS (local, rev 1156, 82 cards):
- SessionStart hook: 888B -> trimmed to 529B (222 -> 132 tokens, 40% cut)
- SKILL.md body: 30,664B / 7,666 tokens (loads only on board engagement)
- board.json: 131KB / ~33K tokens — never auto-loaded (CLI primitives only)
- index.json: 39KB / ~10K tokens — cold
- card.py show <num>: ~1,180 tokens per card
- Done writeup bloat: 8,979 tokens across 54 done cards

PEER BENCHMARK (full table in docs/TOKEN_BUDGET.md):
board-steward is the CHEAPEST per-prompt skill of 5 benchmarked.
- claude-mem ~thousands at SessionStart + on-demand fetch
- graphify ~1,500 per PreToolUse fire (every Grep/Glob)
- mem0 ~6,700 per search_memory call
- letta ~1,000/turn baseline (always-on core blocks)
- CLAUDE.md baseline: full file every turn (cached); grows unbounded — hit by Claude Code 2.1.96 ballooning bug

SHIPPED:
- docs/TOKEN_BUDGET.md (measurements + peer table + degradation curve + hard caps + README line)
- Hook block trimmed 888 -> 529B (40% cut)
- est_tokens field added to telemetry schema (log_event.py + SKILL.md telemetry section)
- README.md: token-cost paragraph + link to TOKEN_BUDGET.md

FOLLOW-UPS FILED (backlog):
- #202 BOARD-SKILL-SLIM: split SKILL.md core 7,666 -> 1,500 tok, move detail to docs/
- #203 BOARD-ARCHIVE-SCHED: schedule archive_done.py daily (currently manual)
- #204 BOARD-HOOK-MULTIPORT: hook port-probe must match board path, not first-live server

Closes Phase 1 of pre-launch plan (sprightly-marinating-prism.md). #89 LIFO shipped 2026-05-27 (0397d60); #90 deleted (was stale — superseded by #119 iPhone reflow); #88 closed here.

cards.title: Simulate 60-day deep-history recovery: someone installs the skill after 2 months of work
cards.origin: User 2026-05-26: 'WHAT IF SOMEONE SPENDS 60DAYS, how will this skill help get previous undone work?' Stress test = the late-adopter scenario. The skill's recovery promise: 'install today, recover all undone work from your last 60 days.' Until tested with real volume this is a marketing claim, not a feature.
cards.notes:
Simulation plan:
1. Point discover.py at QM's actual ~/.claude/projects/*/sessions/*.jsonl for 60d window (already real data)
2. Measure: turns processed · sessions found · token cost · time-to-card-1 · drift items detected
3. Critical question — how does it find OPEN/UNDONE work specifically (vs noise)? Heuristics:
   - User said 'next session' / 'tomorrow' / 'later' / 'defer' / 'pending'
   - Assistant said 'shipped' / 'merged' / 'done' for closure markers
   - Carry-forward memory files are gold (lots of pending items)
4. Output two artifacts:
   - Recovery report: "From 60d of history, found N pending items, M deferrals, K never-finished features"
   - Auto-populated board with all of the above as cards
5. Compare against the manual backfill we did Session 3 (25+ cards from May 12-19) — does discover.py find the same items?
6. Bonus: package this as a 'first-run audit' that runs once when skill installed on existing project
Phase 2d: sim_60d.py — 60-day window + token budget assertion vs docs/TOKEN_BUDGET.md caps
Harness built + smoke-tested in commit (default WARN passes, --strict FAILs on archive-on-install gap). Deferred 2026-05-28 per user — resume to (a) decide strict-cap policy for fresh-install scale, (b) integrate sim_60d --strict into Phase 2e launch-gate, (c) consider historic-doneAt on bootstrap so archive sweeps immediately.
cards.writeup: CLOSED with #203 — the 60-day archive-gate sim is moot while archiving itself is deferred (0 sweepable today; token-cold). Revisit if/when archive scheduling is actually wired. Per user 2026-06-09.

cards.title: Simulate 2-3 day product build: fresh skill install watches a real solo-dev flow
cards.origin: User 2026-05-26: 'to simulate product building in a new folder eg. people spend 2-3 days building a product and someone installs this skill and watch it populate.' Use case = the canonical first-user demo — open empty dir, install skill, code for 2-3 days, watch the board fill in real-time. This proves the live-build moment (BOARD-V4 discover.py + SSE) actually works for the target persona.
cards.notes:
Simulation plan:
1. Fresh dir ~/Desktop/sim-product-2d/ (different port, e.g. :7894)
2. Scripted persona: build a small toy app (say, a markdown todo CLI) over a fake-time-compressed 2-3 day arc with realistic mix: planning, code, bug fixes, deferrals, refactors
3. After each scripted dev-action, optionally inject a card via card.py — or test discover.py mining the fake jsonl session files
4. Measure: how many genuine deferrals does discover.py catch? false-positive rate? did the board ever drift?
5. Compare A: card-as-you-go (Steward fires per shipped) vs B: pure discover.py mining at end-of-day
6. Output = recommended default install flow for new users
Phase 2c: build sim_2d.py wrapping #206 SIM-HARNESS with assertions
cards.writeup: Shipped 2026-05-28. scripts/sim_2d.py wraps the #206 simulate_install.sh harness with assertions: ≥5 cards, ≥3 distinct columns, ≥1 card in done. Reuses production sim infra (same code path users will hit) — no test-only fork to drift. --json mode for CI. Smoke test against WB project 2d window: cards=31, cols=3, done=20, PASS. Boot-timeout + auto-teardown built in; 7891/7892 explicitly refused. #87 SIM-60D will build on this same pattern with token-budget assertions.

cards.title: Auto-detect urgency keywords + spawn SUPER URGENT column on demand
cards.origin: User 2026-05-26: 'if people were to write it this urgently, ONLY if its this urgent, the skill will create a new column called SUPER URGENT.' Idea = the skill is context-sensitive: a regular task → backlog; a request framed with URGENT / CRITICAL / ASAP / BLOCKER / EMERGENCY / 'super urgent' → auto-create the column (if missing) + place card there. Keeps default board clean (4 cols on install) while letting the board respond to actual urgency without manual column ops.
cards.notes:
Design questions for next session:
- Keyword set: URGENT, SUPER URGENT, ASAP, EMERGENCY, BLOCKER, CRITICAL, P0, "fire", "broken"
- False-positive guard: only fire on user message, not assistant; require ALL-CAPS or exclamation? OR explicit framing ("this is urgent")
- Column kind = "todo", position 0 (leftmost), red accent
- Auto-archive: card moved off SUPER URGENT after Xd → column auto-collapses if empty for Yd
- Telemetry: log every auto-creation event for review (over-trigger = noise)
- This card itself was the first instance — column was created manually this turn
Phase 2b: keyword detection in card.py add → auto-create SUPER URGENT col if missing
cards.writeup: Shipped 2026-05-28 (commit below). card.py add scans title+origin via _detect_urgency() — strong markers (ASAP/P0/EMERGENCY/BLOCKER/super urgent/production down) fire unconditionally; weak markers (urgent/critical bug/broken/fire) require ALL-CAPS form OR '!' within 40 chars OR explicit 'this is X' framing. Fired cards route to 'super-urgent' col (auto-created at position 0 if missing) with priority forced to critical. --urgent forces, --no-auto-urgent suppresses. Telemetry event via log_event.py with trigger=trigger-keyword:<word>. 6 smoke cases all pass.

cards.title: Investigate why board misses updates during long active-coding sessions
cards.origin: Session 8 (2026-05-26) shipped 10+ board features (multi-select, drag-trash, miniConfirm, undo/redo, WorkBoard repo) WITHOUT a single card landing on the board. User caught it at 17:35 ('did u save all these inside the work board?'). 7 cards (#77-#83) had to be backfilled retroactively. Pattern: when Claude is the actor (heavy code build, not Steward invocation), trigger keywords (shipped/deployed/merged/verified) fire INSIDE the work narrative but Steward never re-invokes.
cards.notes:
Root causes to investigate:
1. Trigger keywords detected by the harness only re-spawn Steward if user message contains them — but the actor here is the assistant writing the shipped narrative
2. Stop hook is documented as opt-in in SKILL.md v4 — never installed → no truly-continuous loop
3. SKILL.md "after shipped task" rule relies on assistant remembering to re-invoke Steward mid-flow; doesn't survive deep flow state

Prevention options to weigh:
- A) Install the Stop hook in user's settings.json automatically on `serve.py --bootstrap` (1-2s/turn always-on)
- B) Hook UserPromptSubmit + StopHook (belt-and-braces)
- C) Telemetry alarm: if 30+ min passed since last card-added but Edits/Writes happened, surface a yellow banner in board.html ("⚠ N changes since last board update — likely drift")
- D) Make the trigger keyword detection work on ASSISTANT text too via post-tool-use hook

This is THE point of the skill — if it can't survive long active-coding sessions it fails its primary job for any user installing it.
cards.writeup:
ROOT CAUSE (confirmed via evidence sweep across 9 jsonl files for 2026-05-26):
- No hooks were installed in ~/.claude/settings.json — SKILL.md v1-v4 documented them as "opt-in snippets" and nobody (including me) ever wired them up.
- Skill('board-steward') tool calls today: 0. Every board update happened only because the user asked or I remembered to run card.py mid-flow.
- Session 8 time gaps between card.py invocations: 34.5 min · 29 min. The drift window perfectly aligns with the multi-select/drag-trash/undo-redo build burst.
- Telemetry: 6 of 10 events tagged `asked-permission-for-mandatory` (= Steward waiting on user prompt instead of acting).

WHAT SHIPPED (commit 12c1976 in ~/Desktop/WorkBoard):
1. scripts/hook_user_prompt.sh — UserPromptSubmit hook; fires ONLY when board/board.json exists in CWD tree (no leakage to non-board projects); injects <board-steward-protocol> block into Claude's context with mandatory "if your last turn shipped anything, run card.py NOW" rule. Cost <50ms. Includes board path + live server rev when reachable.
2. scripts/install_hooks.py — idempotent installer. Honors $CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR; resolves hook path via __file__ (no hardcoded /Users/*); merges into existing settings.json preserving all other keys; auto-backs up to .bak-<ts>; refuses to touch malformed JSON; supports --dry-run / --status / --uninstall.
3. serve.py --install-hooks / --uninstall-hooks / --hooks-status flags delegate to install_hooks.py. --bootstrap now prints the recommended install command when the hook isn't yet wired — visible the moment any new user creates a board.
4. SKILL.md section renamed "Optional hooks" → "Hook install (required, one-time, idempotent)" with full safety notes.
5. README.md Quick start now includes `python scripts/install_hooks.py` as a required second line after --bootstrap.

VERIFIED for ANY NEW USER (not just this machine):
- Silent from /tmp (no board → no chatter)
- Fires correctly from HFTAgents (board found → protocol block with rev)
- Install merges into existing settings.json (preserves enabledPlugins, ui-ux-pro-max, effortLevel, agentPushNotifEnabled unchanged)
- Auto-backup to ~/.claude/settings.json.bak-20260526T083805Z
- Idempotent re-install: no-op (already-installed)
- Installed on this machine using the same command path a downloader would run — proven working.

RESIDUAL: hook takes effect on NEXT Claude Code session restart. This session continues without it (so protocol-miss risk technically still applies through end-of-session). #85 (auto-urgent-column detection) and the SIM-2D/SIM-60D simulations remain open.

cards.title: Live make-room column shift (iPhone-style) — RE-ATTEMPT

Fix is 1 line at top of column dragstart:

Plus full implementation already exists in conversation history (state vars _colDragSrcId/_colDragCurInsert/_colShiftPx + applyColumnPreviewShift + clearColumnPreviewShifts + rewritten dragover/drop handlers + CSS transition tweak).

cards.title: Global Ctrl+Z / Ctrl+Y undo-redo across structural + text edits
cards.origin: User: 'when i move the Columns around.... i want to Ctr Z... and when i delete some columns.. i want to be able to Ctr Z..... or when i change some text in backlog'
cards.writeup:
- _undoStack + _redoStack (cap 80); _snapState() JSON-clones title/columns/cards; _restoreSnap()
- pushUndo() with adjacent-duplicate de-dupe (no spam from same-state focus events)
- undo() / redo() blur focused el first, swap stacks, restore, scheduleSave + render + refreshTagLegend, toast
- armUndoOnFocus(el): snap-on-focus once per session (not per-keystroke) — text edits restore cleanly
- Hooked into: reorderColumn, deleteColumn, card drag-drop into column, modal card delete, addCol button, column rename, modal card title, notes, writeup, board-title dblclick rename
- Global keydown: Cmd/Ctrl+Z = undo; Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+Z OR Cmd/Ctrl+Y = redo; intercepted globally (overrides native textarea undo by design)

cards.title: Centered header (title + live dot wrapped, no clipboard prefix)
cards.origin: User across 4 turns: title not centered visually → live pill beside header → bigger font → no clipboard 📋 prefix

cards.title: miniConfirm dialog replacing native confirm()

Replaced 3 native confirm() sites (modal card delete, column × delete, trash drop) with custom async miniConfirm(msg, opts) → Promise<bool>.

Dialog: boho card with blurred backdrop, pop-in animation (cubic-bezier 0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1), bold "Confirm?" + small grey subline, Cancel / Delete buttons. Enter confirms, Esc cancels, click-outside cancels.

cards.title: Column delete + grip drag + drag-to-trash + FLIP reorder

cards.title: Tag taxonomy + semantic colors + 3-section legend
cards.origin: User asks (260526 PM): tags don't have matching colors, AI keeps inventing new ones — need a capped taxonomy with industry profiles (software/marketing/research/etc), max ~10 main + ~10-15 sub, and consistent colours per tag across the board.
cards.writeup:
Shipped a tag taxonomy + colour system so tags stop drifting and same tags always render the same colour. Schema. board.json now carries a `tagTaxonomy` root field: { profile: "software" | "marketing" | "research" | "product" | "operations", main: [{ name, color }, ...], // cap 10 sub: [{ name, color }, ...] } // cap 15 Default = software (bug, feature, infra, security, perf, ui, docs, refactor + 12 subs: shipped, deferred, discuss-first, aging, stress-test, test, deploy, staging, correctness, verify, dependency, breaking). Four other industry profiles ship in templates/tag-profiles.json (marketing, research, product, operations). serve.py --bootstrap --profile

picks one at create time. Colours. Each taxonomy entry has a hex colour picked for distinguishability and warm-light readability. Unknown tags fall back to a deterministic 8- colour neutral palette (FNV-ish hash of tag name → palette index) so the same custom name always renders the same colour even without a taxonomy entry. Helper: tagColor(name) and tagKind(name) — single source of truth used by card chips, modal chips, legend chips, and titles. Legend redesign. The Tags popover now has three sections: • Main (cap meter X/10) • Sub (cap meter X/15) • Custom (no cap — off-profile, review periodically) Each chip carries the tag's colour. Used-count chip beside. Unused taxonomy entries shown faintly so users can see "what's available" not just "what's used". Cap meter turns amber within 2 of cap, red at cap. Files changed: • templates/tag-profiles.json (new — 5 industry profiles) • scripts/serve.py — _load_tag_profile + --profile flag + bootstrap injection • board/board.json — injected software taxonomy in-place at rev 55 • board/board.html — tagColor/tagKind helpers; .card-tags chips use color-mix(--tag-c); refreshTagLegend rewritten with 3 sections + cap meters; modal tag chips also coloured. • templates/board.html (synced) • SKILL.md — new "Tag discipline (v5)" section: read taxonomy first, prefer existing tags, no synonyms, caps are soft-but-real, custom tags are OK for project-specific nouns but reviewed periodically. Validation: 72 cards · 62 unique tags · 65 uses are in-taxonomy (13 unique) · 107 uses are custom (49 unique — mostly project nouns like server-g, fact, chatgpt, mcp — appropriately custom). node --check on extracted script returned 0. Served HTML carries all new markers (tag-c, tag-legend-section, _CUSTOM_TAG_PALETTE). User needs Cmd+Shift+R on the open board tab to see the new colours.

Shipped four changes to board.html + serve.py (skill template kept in sync).

1. Project-name centralisation. Header H1 now reads from state.title (board.json

3. Calendar v2 (in-place rewrite of renderCalendar + .cal-* CSS).

4. Tag legend.

templates/board.html (synced).

Smoke tests: node --check exit 0. Curl / shows new HTML markers and no

cards.title: Deadline feature — revisit design (NOT per-card dueAt)
cards.origin: User (260526 EOD): 'i honestly dw each card to be dueAt cos itll be so messy. lets revisit this again.' Wants deadline tracking but rejected the per-card field approach proposed this session. Also wants: AI never auto-sets deadline (bad UX if it says 2 weeks but user finishes in 3). Reminder discipline = subtle only (no popups/toasts), only surface overdue or due-in-24h at session start.
cards.notes: Open design questions for next session: (a) what's the alternative to per-card dueAt — separate 'deadlines.json' file? a single 'sprint' concept? scheduled reminders not tied to cards? (b) how does AI propose without setting silently? (c) what's the at-risk surface — header pill, separate view, calendar overlay? Bring the rename discussion (todo-board vs current name) into the same session since both touch SKILL.md.

cards.title: Live SSE streaming + auto-discover bootstrap + pop/slide animations
cards.origin: User asked for the install moment to be a *show*: skill spawns server → opens browser → discovers cards from ~/.claude/projects/*/sessions/*.jsonl → streams cards one-at-a-time with visible pop animations. Plus: lock card-anatomy taxonomy (every text/tag/priority/column field AI-filled; only num and timestamps mechanical), default columns narrowed to 4 (ideas/backlog/inprogress/done), Blocked/Consideration appear only when needed. Spec discussed across 4 turns this session.
cards.writeup:
Shipped board-steward v4. Six tasks, all green.

**1. SSE in serve.py** — new `/events` endpoint with `text/event-stream`. Every POST `/board.json` is diffed against the in-memory prior state; per-card/per-column changes broadcast as named events (`card-added`, `card-updated`, `card-removed`, `column-added`, `column-removed`, `column-renamed`, `rev-bumped`). Per-client `queue.Queue` (maxsize=256), 15s keepalive comments, BrokenPipe cleanup on disconnect. `_load_initial_cache` seeds the diff baseline at startup.

**2. card.py v4** — added `column add/rm/rename/list`. `atomic_save` now POSTs to `127.0.0.1:7891/board.json` first (so SSE fires → browser animates), falls back to direct file write if server down. `BOARD_NO_SERVER=1` forces fallback for batch ops. Dropped the closed-list `choices` on `--column` so dynamic columns work.

**3. board.html animations** — new CSS keyframes `card-pop-in` (320ms, `cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1)` overshoot), `column-slide-in` (400ms, ease-out, no overshoot — feels "established" vs cards which "pop"), `card-shrink-out` (240ms reverse-pop with max-height collapse), `card-pulse` (200ms scale 1→1.02→1). `prefers-reduced-motion` honored. JS SSE consumer with `handleCardAdded` / `handleCardUpdated` (with FLIP for column moves — getBoundingClientRect before/after + Web Animations API translate interpolation) / `handleCardRemoved` / `handleColumnAdded` / `handleColumnRemoved` / `handleColumnRenamed` / `handleRevBumped`. Polling kept as safety net. Auto-reconnect via native EventSource.

**4. discover.py** — mines `~/.claude/projects/*/<session>.jsonl`. Per session emits `{startedAt, endedAt, durationMin, cwd, turns, firstUserPrompt, lastUserPrompt, filesEdited, shipHints, deferHints, bugHints}`. Permissive cwd matching (cwd == project OR cwd parent of project OR cwd child of project OR session edited files inside project) — strict filter killed all signal since the user often runs claude from $HOME. Optional `--memory` includes MEMORY.md content. NOT a card creator — Claude reads the output and applies judgment.

**5. SKILL.md v4 + template** — added "First-time bootstrap — the live build install moment" section with the exact bash flow (spawn server → open browser → discover → stream at 200ms pace). Template `board.json` narrowed to 4 default columns (ideas/backlog/inprogress/done); Blocked/Consideration added on demand only. Helper-scripts table gained `discover.py`.

**6. End-to-end smoke** — concurrent reader connected to `/events`, ran `card.py add`, received `card-added` event in <0.5s. Tested `card-removed` (removed smoke card via direct POST → server emitted `["card-removed"]`). Server log confirms SSE clients counted via `/health` (sseClients field). Locked spec confirmed by user: pop=320ms overshoot, slide=400ms ease-out, pace=200ms, order=chronological.

Files changed:
- `~/.agents/skills/board-steward/scripts/serve.py` (rewritten, +SSE)
- `~/.agents/skills/board-steward/scripts/card.py` (server POST, column ops)
- `~/.agents/skills/board-steward/scripts/discover.py` (new, ~220 lines)
- `~/.agents/skills/board-steward/templates/board.json` (4 default cols, no welcome card)
- `~/.agents/skills/board-steward/templates/board.html` (synced)
- `~/.agents/skills/board-steward/SKILL.md` (v3 → v4)
- `~/Desktop/QuantifyMe/HFTAgents/board/board.html` (animations + SSE consumer)

Server running on 127.0.0.1:7891. User's existing tab will pick up SSE on hard refresh (⌘+Shift+R).

Caveats: visual eyeball of pop/slide deferred to user's browser refresh. `--memory` flag in discover.py points to MEMORY.md candidates but doesn't yet parse the index format. discover.py currently summarizes sessions; clustering into card drafts stays in Claude's hands (intentional).

cards.title: card.py — CLI helper for board mutations
cards.origin:
User 2026-05-26: "everytime i ask u to update, you write the whole list of codes... shouldnt there be like a function so that u can easily insert using minimal tokens? so perhaps just a basic dict or json structure so u can insert easily?"

Real pain — every card mutation was a 50-line inline Python script with full dict literal, rev bump, savedAt boilerplate. Now it's `card.py add --title '...' --column ideas`.
cards.writeup:
Shipped scripts/card.py — a single-file argparse CLI that handles 95% of board mutations as one-line shell commands instead of inline Python.

Subcommands: add, update, move, subtask add|done|undone|rm, link, unlink, show, list. Long-text fields (origin, notes, writeup) accept either --foo or --foo-stdin (read from stdin — escapes shell-quoting pain for multi-paragraph writeups).

Every mutator: walks up cwd to find board/board.json (or --board override), loads → mutates → bumps rev → sets savedAt/savedBy=claude → atomic write (tmp + rename) → regen index.json. Auto-generates card ids from code/title slug and bumps nextNum. Subtask add generates stable ids like s-<code>-<n>. Link/unlink are bidirectional by default.

Verified end-to-end with 8-test smoke suite: show, list, add (auto-num/auto-link), update (--priority + --add-tag), subtask add + done with auto-id, move with multi-line --writeup-stdin, bidirectional link verified, unlink + cleanup. Board returned cleanly to 67 cards at rev 46.

SKILL.md updated: §"Saving cleanly" now leads with card.py examples, raw Python recipe demoted to "fallback for bulk migrations / schema changes." Helper-scripts table gains card.py as the default mutator. "What you must NOT do" gains: "Don't write inline dict literals when card.py works."

Trade-off: 280 lines of code that I (Claude) and the user both need to learn. Payoff: most card ops drop from ~30 lines of inline Python to ~1 line of shell.

cards.title: Board steward v3 — local HTTP server (cross-browser)
cards.origin: User asked: "so when u develop the skill, it has to be GLOBALLY USABLE." v2 board.html used Chrome-only File System Access API + file:// URL. Path A (localhost server) chosen over B (Chrome-only switch-board button) and C (both) because the skill is intended to ship to other Claude users — Safari/Firefox support is table-stakes for that.
cards.writeup:
Shipped v3 of the board-steward skill on 2026-05-26. Replaced Chrome-only File System Access API with a stdlib Python HTTP server bound to 127.0.0.1:7891.

Files shipped:
• scripts/serve.py (227 lines) — ThreadingHTTPServer. Routes: GET / (board.html), GET /board.json, GET /index.json, GET /health, GET /archive/*, POST /board.json. Atomic writes via tmp+rename. Auto-regens index.json after every POST. Walks up from --project (or cwd) looking for board/board.json. --bootstrap flag creates starter board from templates if missing. Path-traversal guard on /archive/. Same-origin only (127.0.0.1).

• templates/board.html — stripped IndexedDB helpers, fileHandle global, pickFile, ensurePerm, showOpenFilePicker, reconnect button, setup-screen file picker, Safari/Firefox unsupported banner. Replaced with fetch('/board.json') for reads and POST for writes. init() now just hits the server, no permission dance. Sync'd to live project board.

• SKILL.md v2→v3. New §A.1 'ensure server up' step at session start with spawn snippet (curl health check, nohup serve.py if down). Bootstrap section rewritten — `serve.py --bootstrap` does mkdir+copy+spawn in one shot. Helper-scripts table gains serve.py row.

Verified end-to-end with 8-test smoke suite: health endpoint, FSAA refs absent from served HTML, GET round-trip on board.json + index.json, POST round-trip (rev 37→38→restore to 37) with auto-regen confirmation, bad JSON returns 400, missing cards key returns 400, path traversal blocked. All passed.

Caveat: visual rendering in Safari/Firefox not yet eyeballed in this session — structurally guaranteed since no Chromium-only APIs remain, but user should open http://127.0.0.1:7891 in Safari to confirm before declaring fully done.

Trade-off accepted: skill now requires one Python process running per active project (vs zero before). Lifecycle: spawn at session start via §A.1 snippet; user can also start it manually. Server is cheap (~10MB RSS, idle most of the time).

subtasks.text: SKILL.md v2→v3 (bootstrap, helper table, session-start spawn)
8-test smoke suite (GET, POST, auto-regen, errors, traversal)

cards.title: Board Steward v2 — index.json digest + auto-archive + auto-triggers + visual movement
cards.notes:
Designed 5/26 EOD (memory: [[board-steward-v2-design]]). Today's board.json is 64 cards = ~50KB; Steward pays that on every invocation. Four upgrades, in order of impact:

A) `board/index.json` digest — one line per card (~5KB total). Steward writes alongside every rev bump. Startup reads index only; expand to board.json on demand. **10× context savings.**
B) Auto-archive Done >14d → `board/archive/board-YYYY-MM.json`. Active board stays ~15-25 cards.
C) Auto-triggers: Steward fires when user msg contains shipped/deployed/merged/verified/done, OR Bash ran `git commit` / `systemctl restart` (extract SHA → attach to in-progress card).
D) Optional Stop hook promoted from opt-in to recommended default. 1-2s/turn for real-time feel.

Bonus UI: CSS flash 800ms on cards whose updatedAt changed since last poll → user *sees* the movement. Header '🔄 last update: 18s ago' so silence is visible.

Self-contained 1-2hr build: edit `~/.agents/skills/board-steward/SKILL.md` + `templates/board.html` + add an index-regen helper. No backend changes.
cards.writeup:
Shipped 2026-05-26. Board-Steward v2 — five upgrades to make the kanban scale + feel real-time.

**Files added** (in `~/.agents/skills/board-steward/scripts/`):
- `regen_index.py` — generates compact `board/index.json` from `board/board.json`. ~30KB digest for 65 cards (Tier 1 read). One-line-per-card with short keys (n/code/title/col/prio/upd/done/tags/p/links/origin-snippet-140ch). Steward now reads this on every invocation; full board.json only on demand.
- `archive_done.py` — sweeps cards where `column='done' AND doneAt < now-14d` into `board/archive/board-YYYY-MM.json`. Idempotent, `--dry-run` flag, normalizes naive/aware datetimes (early board entries were date-only). Run at session end.

**SKILL.md fully rewritten** for v2:
- New §Traversal — 4 tiers (Tier 1 always = index; Tier 2 = recent on demand; Tier 3 = specific old card by ref; Tier 4 = archive, only if #N referenced).
- New §Saving cleanly — canonical write recipe now includes regen of index.json via subprocess.
- New §Helper scripts pointing at the two new tools.
- New §Optional hooks — UserPromptSubmit (recommended) + Stop (alternative), both copy-pasteable into `~/.claude/settings.json`. Trigger keywords documented as discipline rule for main Claude.
- index.json schema documented.

**`board.html` upgrades** (active board + skill template synced):
- CSS `@keyframes card-flash` — 1.2s green flash on any card whose `updatedAt` or `column` changed since the last poll. User now *sees* the movement live.
- Header `↻ updated Xs ago` indicator next to status; ticks every 1s; goes amber after 5min of silence.
- State: `_prevSnapshot` Map (id → updatedAt|column) + `_flashIds` Set. `detectChangesAndSnapshot()` called after loadFromFile + after poll-detected rev change. First load seeds (no flashing). renderCardCompact applies the flashing class and removes it after 1300ms.

**CONTEXT.md §18 updated:**
- §17 Pointers entry now mentions index.json + archive dir.
- §18 header + source-of-truth split updated for v2.
- Step 0 expanded with trigger keywords, traversal tiers, both hook snippets (UserPromptSubmit + Stop).

cards.title: Priority chip first-click no-op — fix the SSE-echo race that clobbers local edits
cards.origin: When press top left, need to press multiple times. from C --> M --> L. Make sure it doesnt affect the other work. this is the final piece of the puzzle.
cards.writeup:
Fixed the priority-chip first-click no-op (regression of #548) — isolated, single-file change in templates/board.html.

ROOT CAUSE
makePrioChip's cycle code was correct. The bug was upstream in handleCardUpdated (line 6488): Object.assign(state.cards[idx], card) runs on EVERY SSE card-updated event, including ones arriving during the 400ms scheduleSave debounce. If the server payload carried the pre-click priority, the merge reverted the local change — looking exactly like #548 came back.

FIX (mirrors _localMoveLog precedent from #518)
- Added _localEditLog Map (cardId→ts).
- makePrioChip stamps the log right before scheduleSave().
- handleCardUpdated: if a fresh stamp (<2s) exists, strips 'priority' from the SSE payload before Object.assign. Other fields merge normally.
- In-place re-render uses state.cards[idx] (the merged state) instead of the raw SSE payload, so the protected priority isn't visually re-painted with the stale server value.

ISOLATION
- Single file: templates/board.html.
- Three edits: 1 declaration (line 1796), 1 stamp in chip handler (3695), 1 merge guard + render-source swap in handleCardUpdated (6503/6552).
- grep _localEditLog → exactly 3 hits.
- No drag/render/modal/calendar/SSE-connection code touched.
- 2s window auto-expires; no cleanup needed.

/code-review high-effort pass: 1 finding (asymmetric strip vs stamp — promised title/tag protection that wasn't backed by stamps). Applied: narrowed the merge strip to 'priority' only; updated comments to be honest about scope.

COMMIT
- Pushed origin/main.

NOW
Marketplace installers running 'claude plugin install board-steward@workboard' will get 0.9.29 with the priority-chip fix.

cards.title: Rewrite README hero tagline — 'knowledge graph of your work'

cards.writeup:
Rewrote the README hero tagline to lead with the knowledge-graph framing.

BEFORE
### A live kanban board your Claude Code agent keeps up to date — on its own.
**So you never lose an idea, never forget a half-finished task, and never have to ask 'did you update the board?' again.**

AFTER
### A live knowledge graph of your work.
**Never lose an idea. Never lose a workflow.**

WHY
'Kanban board' undersells the structure. The knowledge-graph framing (set up in docs/COMPARISON.md, which contrasts WorkBoard with claude-mem's memory-store model) now leads the README so the positioning is consistent from the first sentence. The 'did you update the board?' angle is preserved in the 'What makes it different' section.

USER PICK
Custom write-in (refined from 3 AI-drafted options). Typo 'and idea' → 'an idea' fixed.

COMMIT
- (this commit) docs: rewrite README hero tagline — 'knowledge graph of your work' (#685)
- Pushed origin/main.

cards.title: Expand 'Under the hood' with Core Components — hooks, multi-Claude, reinforcement, JSON-not-DB, Haiku bootstrap
cards.origin: claude-mem has 'How It Works / Core Components' section listing 5 lifecycle hooks, SQLite, Chroma vector DB, etc. Can i make mine too? Mention multi-claude, hooks, reinforcements.
cards.writeup:
Expanded 'Under the hood' with a claude-mem-style Core Components list — 10 bullets covering what's actually special about WorkBoard.

10 COMPONENTS HIGHLIGHTED (in order)
1. 4 Claude Code hooks (SessionStart / UserPromptSubmit / PreToolUse / Stop) — frames the hook enforcement as 'self-correcting event, not silent drift'.
2. Local board server (pure Python stdlib, 127.0.0.1:7891, launchd/systemd/Task Scheduler).
3. board.json as 'knowledge graph in JSON, not a vector DB' — positions WB against claude-mem's SQLite+Chroma deliberately (diff-able, copy-pasteable, git-friendly).
4. card.py CLI — the single canonical write path; agents that bypass it get caught.
5. Live SSE updates with typed event names — 'no polling'.
6. Multi-Claude / multi-board / multi-tab — sid-bound pulses, port registry, rev-as-CAS (#609).
7. Reinforcement: advisory default + BOARD_STEWARD_STRICT=1 opt-in.
8. History Replay — detached Haiku subprocess, cheapest tier, runs out-of-band.
9. Crash-safe (flock + backups + recover/repair-links/migrate CLIs).
10. Token budget claim with links to TOKEN_BUDGET.md and COMPARISON.md.

DIFFERENTIATION SHAPE
- Mirrors claude-mem's structure (Core Components list) so the comparison is direct.
- Each bullet ties an architectural choice to a USER-FACING advantage (e.g. 'JSON not vector DB → readable, copy-pasteable, git-friendly').
- Multi-Claude is its own bullet per user ask.
- Reinforcements addressed via advisory/strict mode bullet.
- Old short-paragraph version (4 paragraphs) replaced; CLI examples block kept after a 'The CLI Claude actually uses' subhead.

Refresh README.preview.html.

Merged Installation + Quick start into ONE 'Quick start' section, mirroring claude-mem's compact shape.

NEW STRUCTURE
- Lead: '/plugin marketplace add' + '/plugin install' code block (the simplest path inside Claude Code).
- Follow-up: 'Then just talk to Claude normally' + the conversation transcript.
- Alternative: 'Prefer to install from source?' + git clone + install.sh block.
- Requirements + 'no account, no cloud, no API key' compressed into ONE inline line.

REMOVED
- 'WorkBoard runs as a Claude Code plugin. Pick one of two paths.' (the bifurcation is now implicit).
- 'Option A' / 'Option B' subheads — kills false-choice friction.
- Separate '### Requirements' subhead — folded inline as a single requirements line.
- The 'Then open http://127.0.0.1:7891 and watch it move.' standalone trailer (URL was already mentioned earlier).
- Section divider between Installation and Quick start (now one section).

WHAT I DID NOT DO
- npx wrapper. WorkBoard is Python (stdlib only), not Node. No npm/npx exists; the closest parallel is './install.sh' from clone, which is already there. User explicitly said Claude-Code-only is fine, so no need to build a Node-side install path.

cards.title: caledar done doesnt reigster, when i click done, works. when click critical, works. but when done +critical, doesnt work. check other tags
cards.writeup:
Fixed Calendar filter to AND-between-groups, OR-within-group (the intuitive Linear/Trello behavior).

ROOT CAUSE
Calendar filter chips were a flat OR — 'card passes if it matches ANY active filter.' Plus a quirky  guard on Critical/Mid/Low filters. Net effect when user clicked Done + Critical:
- Done filter matched every done card (any priority).
- Critical filter was excluded from matching done cards (the guard).
- Union = every done card + every open-critical card. The Critical filter looked like it 'didn't register' because the Done union drowned it out.

FIX (templates/board.html)
Added helper  right before renderCalendar(). Logic:
- Status group: {done, open}. Within group → OR.
- Priority group: {critical, mid, low}. Within group → OR.
- Between groups → AND (must satisfy both groups if both have active chips).
- 'created' is the wildcard short-circuit, unchanged.

Removed the buggy  guard — now Critical filter alone shows ALL critical cards (done or open), as a user would expect. To narrow to 'open critical work', they'd click Open + Critical.

Replaced the OR for-loop at both filter sites (line 5300 area, line 5438 area) with  — single source of truth.

VERIFIED
- 12 JS unit assertions pass (Node smoke test): done+critical=only done-critical; done alone unchanged; critical alone NOW includes done+critical; critical+mid OR-within-group works.
- 3 refs to _calCardMatchesFilter (helper def + 2 call sites); old 'matches ANY active filter' comment gone.
- node --check on helper: parses clean.

USER ALSO ASKED about #722's other-tag follow-ups ('check other tags'). The fix covers ALL 5 filter chips (Done/Open/Critical/Mid/Low) uniformly via the helper — no more chip-specific edge cases.

cards.title: one other thing. see latest ss. the workboard overlaps with the the other buttons.  what do u suggest?
cards.writeup:
Fixed header title overlapping the right-side buttons (#731).

ROOT CAUSE
.title-wrap is position:absolute; left:50%; translate(-50%,-50%) — dead-centered independent of the flex layout. The right cluster (Reset Columns · Sort Time · 🏷 Tags · Board/Calendar toggle) are normal flex items. The centered title and the buttons don't reserve space from each other, so once the window narrows the title floats ON TOP of the buttons.

FIX (templates/board.html, header h1 CSS)
Added a @media (max-width: 860px) block that, on narrow widths, drops the title out of absolute-center into normal flow:
- .title-wrap → position:static, transform:none, max-width:none, min-width:0, flex:0 1 auto, z-index:auto.
- h1 → min-width:0 so its existing white-space:nowrap/overflow:hidden/text-overflow:ellipsis actually engages inside the flex item.
Result: at ≤860px the layout becomes [Work Board ...spacer... buttons] (title LEFT-aligned, ellipsis-truncates, never collides). Above 860px the dead-center title is unchanged.

Per user: keep centered on wide, LEFT-align on narrow (not hidden) — title always visible.

VERIFIED
- Media query present in templates/board.html and in the served HTML at :7891.
- Existing h1 ellipsis rules (line 68) do the truncation; only needed min-width:0 to enable it in flex.

To test: shrink the browser window < 860px → title should jump to the left and truncate, buttons stay clear. Local-only; no commit per iterate-locally mode.

cards.title: Trim --project / --demo flag examples from README Quick start (bootstrap auto-detects)
cards.origin: ./install.sh --project / --demo ... is this even needed? because our bootstrap auto detects isnt it?
cards.writeup:
Trimmed the source-install block to just bare './install.sh'.

REMOVED
- ./install.sh --project ~/code/foo  (power-user override to skip the auto-detect picker)
- ./install.sh --demo                (isolated dry-run)

WHY
install.sh auto-detects projects from session history when run from $HOME (the #375 picker), or uses the current dir if run inside a project. So the bare command does the smart thing for the common case. --project and --demo are power-user flags still documented in install.sh's own header (lines 8-12) for anyone who looks.

NEW
'./install.sh   # sets up + auto-detects your projects and bootstraps a board'

Matches the 'keep it claude-mem-simple' direction. Refresh README.preview.html. Local-only.
